revision.text:
{{Short description|German association football club}} {{Use American English|date=December 2019}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox football club | clubname = Berliner FC Dynamo | fullname = Berliner Fussball Club Dynamo e. V. | image = Berliner Fußballclub Dynamo.svg | upright = 0.8 | nickname = ''Die Weinroten'' (The Clarets) | short name = BFC | founded = {{Start date and age|df=yes|1966|1|15}} | ground = [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen#Football stadium|Stadion im Sportforum]] | coordinates = {{coord|52|32|27|N|13|28|34|E|region:DE-BE_type:landmark|display=title, inline}} | capacity = 4,500{{cite news |last=Shulze |first=Lukas |date=1 March 2024 |title=BEI EINEM AUFSTIEG IN DIE 3. LIGA: WIRD DAS BFC-STADION RECHTZEITIG MODERNISIERT? |url=https://www.tag24.de/berlin/sport/bei-einem-aufstieg-in-die-3-liga-wird-das-bfc-stadion-rechtzeitig-modernisiert-3115637 |language=German |newspaper=Tag24 ([[:de:Tag24|de]]) |location=[[Dresden]] |publisher=TAG24 NEWS Deutschland GmbH |access-date=15 August 2024}} | owntitle = President | owner = Norbert Uhlig | chairman = Peter Meyer | chrtitle = Chairman of
Economic Council | manager = [[Dennis Kutrieb]] | mgrtitle = Head coach | league = [[Regionalliga Nordost]] (IV) | season = [[2024-25 Regionalliga|2024–25]] | position = Regionalliga Nordost, 8th of 18 | website = {{URL|http://bfc.com/|bfc.com}} | pattern_la1 = _bfc2122h | pattern_b1 = _bfc2122h | pattern_ra1 = _bfc2122h | pattern_sh1 = | pattern_so1 = | leftarm1 = 471E24 | body1 = 471E24 | rightarm1 = 471E24 | shorts1 = 471E24 | socks1 = 471E24 | pattern_la2 = _blackshoulders | pattern_b2 = _thinblackstripes | pattern_ra2 = _blackshoulders | pattern_sh2 = | pattern_so2 = | leftarm2 = FFFFFF | body2 = FFFFFF | rightarm2 = FFFFFF | shorts2 = FFFFFF | socks2 = FFFFFF |}} '''Berliner Fussball Club Dynamo e. V.''', commonly abbreviated to '''BFC Dynamo''' ({{IPA|de|beːʔɛfˌt͡seː dyˈnaːmo|-|De-BFC Dynamo.ogg}}) or '''BFC''' ({{IPA|de|beːʔɛfˈt͡seː|-|De-BFC.ogg}}), alternatively sometimes called '''Dynamo Berlin''', is a [[Football in Germany|German football club]] based in the locality of [[Alt-Hohenschönhausen]] of the [[Boroughs and neighborhoods of Berlin|borough]] of [[Lichtenberg, Berlin|Lichtenberg]] of [[Berlin]]. The team competes in the [[Regionalliga Nordost]], the fourth tier of [[German football league system|German football]]. BFC Dynamo was founded in [[East Germany]] in 1966 from the football department of sports club [[SC Dynamo Berlin]]. BFC Dynamo established itself as a top-team in the [[DDR-Oberliga]] in the mid-1970s. Supported by extensive [[#Youth academy during East German era|youth work]], BFC Dynamo eventually became one of the most successful clubs in [[East Germany|East German]] football.{{cite book |last1=Luther |last2=Willmann |first1=Jörn |first2=Frank |date=2003 |title=BFC Dynamo – Der Meisterclub |location=Berlin |publisher=Das Neue Berlin GmbH |page=75 |isbn=3-360-01227-5}}{{cite news |last=Kopp |first=Johannes |date=16 January 2006 |title=40 Jahre BFC Dynamo – "Wir sind doch sowieso die Bösen" |url=https://www.spiegel.de/sport/fussball/40-jahre-bfc-dynamo-wir-sind-doch-sowieso-die-boesen-a-395493.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Spiegel (website)|Der Spiegel]] |publisher=Der Spiegel GmbH & Co. KG |location=Hamburg |access-date=3 August 2022}} BFC Dynamo is the record champion in East Germany, with ten consecutive [[List of East German football champions|league championships]] from 1979 through 1988, under coach [[Jürgen Bogs]]. In 1989, the team became the first and only winner of the [[DFV-Supercup]]. BFC Dynamo renamed itself to FC Berlin during ''[[Peaceful Revolution|Die Wende]]''. One of the largest [[Football hooliganism|hooligan]] scenes in Germany was formed around FC Berlin.{{cite news |date=29 October 2009 |title=FC Berlin – Das Schicksal der Ostvereine #4 |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/fc-berlin/384815 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=20 August 2021}} Young FC Berlin-supporter Mike Polley was killed by German police during football riots in [[Leipzig]] in 1990. FC Berlin just narrowly failed to qualify for the [[2. Bundesliga]] in [[1990–91 NOFV-Oberliga#2. Bundesliga play-off|1991]]. The club lost the equivalent of two complete teams in players to other clubs in the first one or two years after the fall of the [[Berlin Wall]].{{cite news |last=Gartenschläger |first=Lars |date=15 January 2016 |title=50 Jahre BFC Dynamo: Der tiefe Fall des verhassten Stasi-Klubs |url=https://www.welt.de/sport/fussball/article151020667/Der-tiefe-Fall-des-verhassten-Stasi-Klubs.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} FC Berlin struggled in [[German reunification|re-unified]] Germany and never progressed beyond the third tier of German football. The club took back its old name Berliner FC Dynamo in 1999. BFC Dynamo suffered a financial crisis in 2001 and eventually became [[Insolvency|insolvent]]. The club's supporters played an important part in saving the club from bankruptcy.{{cite web |url=https://www.dw.com/en/stasi-club-bfc-dynamo-what-happened-to-the-record-east-german-champions/a-51084561 |title='Stasi club' BFC Dynamo: What happened to the record East German champions? |last=Ford |first=Matt |date=7 November 2019 |website=Deutsche Welle |location=[[Bonn]] |access-date=7 May 2019}} The insolvency proceedings were brought to a positive conclustion in 2004 and the club consolidated in the [[NOFV-Oberliga Nord]]. After an undefeated season in the NOFV-Oberliga Nord under coach Volkan Uluc, the team finally won promotion to [[Regionalliga Nordost]] in 2014. BFC Dynamo has since established itself firmly in the Regionaliga Nordost. In 2022, the team won its first [[Regionalliga]] title, under coach [[Christian Benbennek]]. BFC Dynamo has become one of the most successful teams in the [[Berlin Cup]] in recent years. The team has won six Berlin Cup titles since 2013. BFC Dynamo saw the biggest increase in membership of any club in Berlin in 2021, apart from [[Hertha BSC]] and [[1. FC Union Berlin]].{{cite news |last=Bühler |first=Karin |date=28 April 2022 |title=Der BFC Dynamo hatte 2021 den größten Mitgliederzuwachs aller Berliner Vereine |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/sport-leidenschaft/der-bfc-dynamo-hatte-2021-den-groessten-mitgliederzuwachs-aller-berliner-vereine-li.224643 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=28 April 2022}} By 2023, the club had finally managed to win back the rights to its previously lost traditional crest. During the [[2023-24 Regionalliga|2023-24 season]], BFC Dynamo reported its highest attendance figures since 1990.{{cite news |last1=Mertes |last2=Noack |last3=Schmidt |first1=Peter |first2=Frank |first3=Brian |date=16 April 2024 |title=BFC Dynamo macht Geheimnis aus Preiserhöhung gegen Energie Cottbus |url=https://www.lr-online.de/energie-cottbus/regionalliga-nordost-bfc-dynamo-macht-geheimnis-aus-preiserhoehung-gegen-energie-cottbus-73573597.html |work=[[Lausitzer Rundschau]] |language=de |location=[[Cottbus]] |publisher=Lausitzer VerlagsService GmbH |access-date=3 November 2024 }} BFC Dynamo plays its home matches at the [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen#Football stadium|Stadion im Sportforum]]. The club enjoys a traditional cross-city rivalry with 1. FC Union Berlin. The rivalry with Union Berlin is part of the [[Berlin derby]]. BFC Dynamo has won recognition for its [[#Youth_department|youth work]]. Since 2003, the club also operates an award-winning day care project for local children. The club is based in the [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen]]. The sports complex is the location of the club offices, the clubhouse and the youth teams. ==History== {{Very long section|date=February 2025}} {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo}} ===Background: SC Dynamo Berlin (1954–1966)=== {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo (1954–1978)}} [[BFC Dynamo]] started as a football department of sports club [[SC Dynamo Berlin]]. SC Dynamo Berlin was founded on 1 October 1954 as one of the new elite [[Sports club (East Germany)|sports clubs]] in East Germany.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=98}} The sports club was affiliated to [[Sports associations (East Germany)|sports association]] [[SV Dynamo]]. The new sports club SC Dynamo Berlin became a center of excellence ({{langx|de|Leistungsschwerpunkte}}) of sports association SV Dynamo.{{cite web |url=https://vtnvagt.de/index.php/9-startseite/aktuelles/1924-70-jahre-sportvereinigung-dynamo-sv-dynamo |title=70 Jahre Sportvereinigung Dynamo (SV Dynamo) |author= |date=27 March 2023 |website=vtnvagt.de |location=[[Strausberg]] |publisher=Verband zur Pflege der Traditionen der Nationalen Volksarmee und der Grenztruppen der DDR e. V. |language=de |access-date=1 February 2025 }} In order to establish a competitive side in Berlin, the team of [[SG Dynamo Dresden]] and its place in the [[DDR-Oberliga]] was transferred to the new sports club SC Dynamo Berlin. The relocation was designed to give the capital a team that could rival teams from [[West Berlin]], such as [[Hertha BSC]], which were still popular in [[East Berlin]].{{cite book |first1=Mike |last2=Grix |last1=Dennis |first2=Jonathan |date=2012 |title=Sport under Communism – Behind the East German 'Miracle' |location=[[Hampshire]] |edition=1st |publisher=Palgrave Macmillan (Macmillan Publishers Limited) |pages=136–137 |isbn=978-0-230-22784-2}}{{cite journal |last=Dennis |first=Mike |year=2007 |title=Behind the Wall: East German football between state and society |url=http://www.gfl-journal.de/2-2007/dennis.pdf |journal=German as a Foreign Language |issue=2 |volume=2007 |pages=46–73 |access-date=9 June 2019 |issn=1470-9570 |archive-date=9 April 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409232204/http://www.gfl-journal.de/2-2007/dennis.pdf }}{{cite book |last=Pleil |first=Ingolf |date=2013 |title=Mielke, Macht und Meisterschaft: Dynamo Dresden im Visier der Stasi |language=de |location=Berlin |edition=2nd |publisher=Ch. Links Verlag GmbH |page=17 |isbn=978-3-86153-756-4}}{{refn|group=nb|This was not the first or last relocation or transfer of entire football teams in East Germany at the time. Other examples are: * Sports association SV Deutsche Volkpolizei relocated its three second-tier teams [[SG Volkspolizei Potsdam|SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Potsdam]], SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Weimar and [[FC Mecklenburg Schwerin|SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Schwerin]] to sports communities in larger cities during the summer of 1952. SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Potsdam was relocated to Berlin, SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Weimar to [[Erfurt]] and SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Schwerin to [[Rostock]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|pp=7-8}} * The team of SV Vorwärts der KVP Leipzig was relocated to East Berlin in 1953. The team continued the [[1953-54 DDR-Oberliga]] as SV Vorwärts der KVP Berlin. SV Vorwärts der KVP Berlin then became ASK Vorwärts Berlin, which later became [[1. FC Frankfurt|FC Vorwärts Berlin]].{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|pp=137–138}} * The relatively successful team of BSG Empor Lauter and its place in the [[DDR-Oberliga]] was transferred to sports club SC Empor Rostock in 1954. The football department of SC Empor Rostock later became [[F.C. Hansa Rostock]].{{cite book |last=Hesse-Lichtenberger |first=Ulrich |date=2003 |title=Tor!: The Story of German Football |location=London |edition=3rd |publisher=WSC Books Ltd. |isbn=0-9540134-5-X|pages=225–226}} * The team of BSG Turbine Halle and its place in the DDR-Oberliga was transferred to sports club SC Chemie Halle-Leuna in 1954.{{cite book |last=McDougall |first=Alan |date=2014 |title=The People's Game: Football, State and Society in East Germany |location=[[Cambridge]] |edition=1st |publisher=Cambridge University Press |page=101 |isbn=978-1-107-05203-1}} The football department of SC Chemie Halle-Leuna later became [[Hallescher FC|Hallescher FC Chemie]].}}{{refn|group=nb|[[SG Dynamo Dresden]] itself had a background that involved political interference and player delegations. SG Dynamo Dresden was previously known as SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Dresden. When [[Dresdner SC|SG Friedrichstadt]] was forcibly dissolved by East German sports authorities after the [[1949–50 DDR-Oberliga]], the playing right in the DDR-Oberliga was transferred to SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Dresden.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=136}}{{sfn|Hesse-Lichtenberger|2003|p=225-226}}{{cite web |url=https://www.dynamo-dresden.de/verein/gestern-und-heute/gestern-und-heute.html |title=Dynamo Dresden - Gestern und Heute |website=dynamo-dresden.de |location=Dresden |publisher=SG Dynamo Dresden e.V. |language=de |access-date=15 August 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210413154646/https://www.dynamo-dresden.de/verein/gestern-und-heute/gestern-und-heute.html |archive-date=13 April 2021}} SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Dresden had previously played in the lower-tier Stadtliga Dresden and was thus able to enter the DDR-Oberliga without having to progress through divisions.{{cite news |last=Leimert |first=Jochen |date=16 April 2020 |title=Als Ulbricht im Heinz-Steyer-Stadion den Volkszorn zu spüren bekam |url=https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/als-ulbricht-im-heinz-steyer-stadion-den-volkszorn-zu-spuren-bekam/ |language=de |newspaper=Sportbuzzer |location=Hannover |publisher=Sportbuzzer GmbH|access-date=22 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230322224433/https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/als-ulbricht-im-heinz-steyer-stadion-den-volkszorn-zu-spuren-bekam/ |archive-date=22 March 2023}}{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=136}} In order to keep the place in the [[DDR-Oberliga]], the team of SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Dresden would be reinforced with players from Volkspolizei teams all over the country.{{cite web |url=http://www.3-liga.com/news-3liga-die-geschichte-dynamo-dresdens-13721.html |title=Die Geschichte Dynamo Dresdens |author= |date=29 June 2014 |website=3-liga.com |location=[[Lübeck]] |publisher=Niels-Frederik Popien |language=de |access-date=15 August 2020}} The head of the [[Volkspolizei]] [[Kurt Fischer (politician, born 1900)|Kurt Fischer]] ordered that best football players in the sports communities of the Volkspolizei around East Germany should be concentrated in the now first-tier SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Dresden.{{cite news |last=Schramm |first=Stefan |date=11 April 2023 |title=Dynamo Dresden feiert sich und seine 70 Jahre |url=https://www.dnn.de/sport/regional/jubilaeum-12-april-1953-dynamo-dresden-feiert-sich-und-seine-70-jahre-6M2QJTCRBBDR5LFX7IMQ4BO7HI.html |work=[[Dresdner Neueste Nachrichten]] |language=de |location=Hannover |publisher=Verlagsgesellschaft Madsack GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=8 July 2023}} The 40 best players of the various Volkspolizei teams in East Germany were then brought together for a training session in Forst in July 1950. Coaches Fritz Sack and Paul Döring then picked out 17 players from 11 different cities who were delegated to Dresden to form the team.{{sfn|Hesse-Lichtenberger|2003|p=226}} [[SG Volkspolizei Potsdam|SV Deutsche Volkspolizei Potsdam]] lost its five best players to Dresden and was severely weakened.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=136}}{{cite news |last=Grüne |first=Hardy |date=1 June 2020 |title=Der angefeindete Serienmeister des Ostens |url=https://www.fussball-woche.de/artikel/der-angefeindete-serienmeister-des-ostens/ |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=16 August 2020}}{{Dead link|date=March 2021 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes}}}} Among the players delegated from SG Dynamo Dresden were [[Johannes Matzen]], [[Herbert Schoen]] and [[Günter Schröter]]. The team played its first match as SC Dynamo Berlin on 21 November 1954 against BSG Rotation Babelsberg in the [[1954-55 DDR-Oberliga]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=98}}[[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-68058-0001, ASK Vorwärts Berlin - SC Dynamo Berlin 2-1.jpg|thumb|left|Günter Schröter (left) and Martin Skaba (right) during a match between ASK Vorwärts Berlin and SC Dynamo Berlin in 1959]] Most players of the former SG Dynamo Dresden team had aged by the late 1950s.{{cite magazine |last=Kelemen |first=Luci |date=5 February 2018 |editor-last=Saleem |editor-first=Omar |title=Eleven Pigs and the secret police: the story of BFC Dynamo |url=https://thesefootballtimes.co/2018/02/05/eleven-pigs-and-the-secret-police-the-story-of-bfc-dynamo/ |magazine=[[These Football Times]] |access-date= 16 June 2019}} The team was now instead shaped by a new generation of players, including [[Martin Skaba]], [[Werner Heine]], [[Waldemar Mühlbächer]], [[Hermann Bley]] and Konrad Dorner.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=269, 275, 289}} SC Dynamo Berlin won its first trophy in the [[1959 FDGB-Pokal]]. However, the team was not allowed to participate in the [[1960–61 European Cup Winners' Cup]]. The [[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|East German Football Association]] ({{langx|de|Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR}}) (DFV) decided that local rival and league runner-up [[ASK Vorwärts Berlin]] was a better representative.{{cite book |last1=Luther |last2=Willmann |first1=Jörn |first2=Frank |date=2003 |title=BFC Dynamo – Der Meisterclub |language=de |location=Berlin |edition=1st |publisher=Das Neue Berlin |page=28 |isbn=3-360-01227-5}} SC Dynamo Berlin had some success in the first two seasons of the 1960s. The team finished runners-up in the [[1960 DDR-Oberliga]] and reached the final of the 1961-62 FDGB-Pokal. However, SC Dynamo Berlin found itself overshadowed in the capital by the [[ASV Vorwärts|army-sponsored]] ASK Vorwärts Berlin.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|pp=146–147}} The team of SC Dynamo Berlin during the 1960s would eventually prove relatively weak.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|pp=146–147}} By the end of the [[1962–63 DDR-Oberliga]], SC Dynamo Berlin had become a lower-table side. ===Founding and rise (1966–1978)=== {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo (1954–1978)}} East German football was reorganized in 1965–1966 when the football departments of ten sports clubs (SC) were made into ten designated [[Football club (East Germany)|football clubs]] (FC). As part of this reorganization, the football department of SC Dynamo Berlin became football club Berliner FC Dynamo. BFC Dynamo was founded on 15 January 1966.{{cite news |date=15 January 2016 |title=Jubiläum: BFC Dynamo wird 50 Jahre alt |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/berlin-sport/bfc-dynamo-berlin/jubilaeum-bfc-dynamo-wird-50-jahre-alt |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=17 June 2019}} Manfred Kirste was elected club chairman and the SV Dynamo President [[Erich Mielke]] was made [[Title of honor|honorary]] chairman.{{cite journal |last=Wyschek |first=Helmut |year=1999 |title=Erich Mielke, soll unser Führer sein |url=https://telegraph.cc/archiv/telegraph-3-1999/erich-mielke-soll-unser-fuehrer-sein/ |journal=Telegraph (De) |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Prenzlberg Dokumentation e.V. |issue=3 |volume=1999 |access-date=27 December 2019}} The new designated football clubs were formed as centers of excellence in East German football, with the right to draw on talents within designated geographical and administrative areas.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=141}} BFC Dynamo was initially assigned [[Bezirk Cottbus]] and one third of East Berlin as catchment area.{{cite book |last1=Luther |last2=Willmann |first1=Jörn |first2=Frank |date=2000 |title=Und niemals vergessen – Eisern Union! |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=BasisDruck |pages=64–65 |isbn=978-3-86163-106-4 |quote=Aber der Reihe nach: Die Fußballclubs hatten bei ihrer Gründung einen oder zwei Bezirke zugewiesen bekommen, aus deren Trainingszentren sie ihren Nachwuchs rekrutierten. Bis zu diesem Zeitpunkt verfügte Union über ein Drittel der Berliner Leistungszentren und den Bezirk Potsdam als Einzugsgebiet. Als der FC Vorwärts jedoch nach Frankfurt umgesiedelt wurde, bekam er als Talentequelle neben dem nun "eigenen" Bezirk Frankfurt/Oder auch noch Potsdam zugewiesen. Und der BFC übernamn das Drittel der Berliner Nachwuchsschmieden, das vordem die Armeesportler inne hatten. Damit verfügten die Dynamos über das Einzugsgebiet Cottbus plus zwei Drittel Berlins.}}{{cite thesis |last=Dost |first=Robert |date=17 January 2011 |title=Der zivile Club - Die gesellschaftliche Stellung des 1.FC Union Berlin und seiner Anhänger in der DDR |type=BA |language=de |location=[[Berlin]] |publication-place=[[Mittweida]] |publisher=[[Hochschule Mittweida]] |url=https://monami.hs-mittweida.de/frontdoor/deliver/index/docId/940/file/BA.Arbeit.Robert.Dost.pdf |page=12-13 |access-date=13 April 2022}} BFC Dynamo was officially a club of the Ministry of the Interior and the club's official sponsor was the [[Volkspolizei]].{{cite news |last=Bläsig |first=Horst |date=21 August 2005 |title=Lokalderby unter Polizeischutz |url=https://www.welt.de/print-wams/article131250/Lokalderby-unter-Polizeischutz.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=4 July 2021}}{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/01/fussball-bfc-dynamo-55-jahre-ddr-oberliga-frank-terletzki.html |title=BFC Dynamo – Geliebt und verachtet |last=Büchner |first=Philipp |date=31 January 2021 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |access-date=4 July 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210709184253/https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/01/fussball-bfc-dynamo-55-jahre-ddr-oberliga-frank-terletzki.html |archive-date=9 July 2021}} However, Honorary Chairman Mielke was the head of the [[Stasi]], and BFC Dynamo would eventually come to receive personal, organizational and financial support from the Stasi.{{cite web |url=https://www.bundesarchiv.de/themen-entdecken/online-entdecken/themenbeitraege/schild-und-schwert-des-bfc-dynamo/ |title="Schild und Schwert" des BFC Dynamo |website=bundesarchiv.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[German Federal Archives]] |language=de |access-date=16 February 2025 }} BFC Dynamo was relegated to the second-tier [[DDR-Liga]] in [[1966–67 DDR-Oberliga|1967]] and subsequently began a rejuvenation of the team.{{cite magazine |last=Fischer |first=Werner |date=31 December 1968 |title=Mit Schütze als Regisseur kam die erhoffte Wende |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1968_12_31.html |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1968 |issue=53 |page=6 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=9 April 2022}} The team eventually dominated the 1967-68 DDR-Liga Nord and immediately won promotion back to the DDR-Oberliga. Among the talented players from the youth department who were integrated into the first team in the late 1960s and early 1970s were [[Harald Schütze]], [[Norbert Johannsen]], [[Peter Rohde (footballer)|Peter Rohde]], [[Frank Terletzki]], and [[Bernd Brillat]]. BFC Dynamo reached the final of the 1970-71 FDGB-Pokal. The team lost the final 1–2 in overtime to SG Dynamo Dresden, but qualified for the [[1971-72 European Cup Winners' Cup]] as runner-up.{{cite magazine |author= |date=22 June 1971 |title=Der 'Lange' in einer Spielertraube |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPokal/197071pokFH.html |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1971 |issue=25 |page=5 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=9 April 2022}} The Ministry of Defense decided to relocate [[1. FC Frankfurt|FC Vortwärts Berlin]] to [[Frankfurt an der Oder]] ahead of the 1971–72 season.{{cite news |last=Max |first=Jens |date=5 November 2019 |title=Insolvenzen, Abstiege, Neuanfänge: Das wurde aus den letzten 14 DDR-Oberligisten |url=https://www.mz.de/sport/fussball/insolvenzen-abstiege-neuanfange-das-wurde-aus-den-letzten-14-ddr-oberligisten-3137724 |language=German |newspaper=Mitteldeutsche Zeitung |location=[[Halle (Saale)|Halle]] |publisher=Mediengruppe Mitteldeutsche Zeitung GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=19 December 2021}} BFC Dynamo and [[1. FC Union Berlin]] were from now on the only major football clubs in East Berlin. BFC Dynamo was allowed to take over the catchment area in East Berlin that had previously belonged to FC Vorwärts Berlin.{{cite thesis |last=Japke |first=Josephine |date=21 June 2018 |title=Die gesellschaftspolitische Stellung des 1. FC Union Berlin zu Zeiten der DDR |degree=Bachelor |language=de |location=[[Königs Wusterhausen]] |publication-place=[[Mittweida]] |publisher=[[Hochschule Mittweida]] |url=https://monami.hs-mittweida.de/frontdoor/deliver/index/docId/8807/file/BA+UNION.pdf |pages=35–37 |access-date=5 June 2022}} The team would also get the opportunity to play more matches at the larger and more centrally located [[Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark]] in [[Prenzlauer Berg]], which led to increased interest in the club and growing attendance numbers.{{cite thesis |last=Japke |first=Josephine |date=21 June 2018 |title=Die gesellschaftspolitische Stellung des 1. FC Union Berlin zu Zeiten der DDR |degree=Bachelor |language=de |location=[[Königs Wusterhausen]] |publication-place=[[Mittweida]] |publisher=[[Hochschule Mittweida]] |url= https://monami.hs-mittweida.de/frontdoor/deliver/index/docId/8807/file/BA+UNION.pdf |pages=38–40 |access-date=4 April 2021}} BFC Dynamo stood out among other teams within SV Dynamo. The team was located at the frontline of the [[Cold War]]. It was also a representative of the East German capital. This meant that the club had to be well equipped.{{cite book |last=Braun |first=Jutta |year=2015 |editor1-last=Münkel |editor1-first=Daniela |title=State Security: A reader on the GDR secret police |url=https://www.stasi-unterlagen-archiv.de/assets/bstu/en/Downloads/E_bstu_lesebuch_englisch.pdf |location=Berlin |publisher=[[German Federal Archives]] |pages=86–87 |access-date=13 April 2022 |isbn=978-3-942130-97-4}} BFC Dynamo would get access to a nationwide scouting network, which included numerous training centers ({{langx|de|Trainingszentrum}}) (TZ) of SV Dynamo across East Germany.{{cite book |last=Friedemann |first=Horst |date=1991 |title=Sparwasser und Mauerblümchen: Die Geschichte des Fussballs in der DDR, 1949–1991 |language=de |location=[[Essen]] |edition=1st |publisher=Klartext Verlag |page=128 |isbn=978-3-88474-462-8 |quote=Das DDR-weite Sichtungssystem mit 33 Trainingszentren der SV Dynamo sowie im Partnerbezirk Cottbus hat den Talentenachschub nie abreiß en lassen.}}{{refn|group=nb|SV Dynamo would eventually operate numerous training centers (TZ) across East Germany. The training centers were divided between BFC Dynamo and SG Dynamo Dresden depending on catchment area.{{cite book |last1=Luther |last2=Willmann |first1=Jörn |first2=Frank |date=2003 |title=BFC Dynamo – Der Meisterclub |language=de |location=Berlin |edition=1st |publisher=Das Neue Berlin |page=93 |isbn=3-360-01227-5 |quote=Jürgen Bogs: 'Dynamo hatte in der ganzen DDR flächendeckend Trainingszentren, die je nach Einzugsgebiet dem BFC oder Dynamo Dresden zugeordnet waren. Bereits in diesen Trainingszentren wurde besser trainiert als anderswo, denn dort wurden ja schon Spieler zusammengefasst, die aufgrund ihres individuellen Könnens aufgefallen sind. Und die Arbeit der Zentren wurde vom BFC zusätzlich beobachtet und gesteuert. Es gab einen zentralen, mehrtägigen Sichtungslehrgang, dort wurden die besten Talente der einzelnen Trainingszentren zusammengefasst, von diesen vierzig Mann wuden vielleicht zehn genommen.'}}}} BFC Dynamo would eventually be able to recruit young talented players from 38 training centers (TZ) across East Germany for its youth department. By comparison, 1. FC Union Berlin only had access to 6 training centers (TZ) in the Berlin area.{{cite book |last1=Braun |last2=Teichler |first1=Jutta |first2=Hans Joachim |date=2006 |title=Sportstadt Berlin im Kalten Krieg: Prestigekämpfe und Systemwettstreit |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=Ch. Links Verlag GmbH |page=380 |isbn=978-3-86153-399-3 |quote=Bei den Clubmannschaften existierte eine Zweiklassenesellschaft. In Berlin genoss der BFC Dynamo besondere Privilegien. So standen dem von Ministerium für Staatssicherheit finanzierten und als Lieblingskind Erich Mielkes bekannten Club aus Hohenschönhausen republikweit 38 Trainingszentren (TZ) zur Verfügung, aus denen er seine Talente rekrutiere konnte. Der 1. FC Union hingegen musste sich mit 6 TZs im Berliner Raum zufrienden geben.}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-N1130-0021, BFC Dynamo - BSG Wismut Aue 6-0.jpg|thumb|right|Wolf-Rüdiger Netz (right) scores a header in the match against BSG Wismut Aue in 1974.]]The team was joined by [[Forward (association football)|forward]] [[Wolf-Rüdiger Netz]] from [[FC Mecklenburg Schwerin|SG Dynamo Schwerin]] in 1971. BFC Dynamo made its first appearance in an [[UEFA club competitions|UEFA Competition]] in the [[1971-72 European Cup Winners' Cup]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=113}} The team reached all the way to the semi-finals. BFC Dynamo thus became the first team from Berlin to reach the semi-finals in one of the two most prestigious UEFA club competitions (the [[European Cup]] and the [[European Cup Winners' Cup]]). BFC Dynamo was eventually eliminated by [[FC Dynamo Moscow|Dynamo Moscow]] in the semi-finals, after a [[Penalty shootout|penalty shoot-out]] in the return leg.{{cite magazine |last=Simon |first=Günter |date=25 April 1972 |title=Das Finale war zum Greifen nahe! |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201972/FUWO%201972%2017.pdf |journal=Neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1972 |issue=17 |pages=5–6 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=10 April 2022}} Harry Nippert became the new coach in 1973. BFC Dynamo also recruited midfielder and [[East Germany national football team|national team]] player [[Reinhard Lauck]] from relegated 1. FC Union Berlin the same year. BFC Dynamo had the youngest team in the league in the [[1975-76 DDR-Oberliga]], with an average age of 22.5 years.{{cite thesis |last1=Farshi |last2=Hadi |first1=Sabbagh |first2=Mohammad |date=20 May 2011 |title=Deutsch-Deutsche Transfers: Der Wechsel von Thomas Doll vom BFC Dynamo zum HSV 1990 |type=BA |language=de |location=Hamburg |publication-place=[[Mittweida]] |publisher=[[Hochschule Mittweida]] |url=https://monami.hs-mittweida.de/frontdoor/deliver/index/docId/1235/file/BA_Arbeit_Pruefungsexemplar_Hadi_Farshi.pdf |page=22 |access-date=14 April 2022}} Talented players from the youth department were continuously integrated into the first team in the 1970s, such as [[Hans-Jürgen Riediger]], [[Lutz Eigendorf]], [[Norbert Trieloff]] and [[Bodo Rudwaleit]]. BFC Dynamo established itself as a top team in the DDR-Oberliga in the mid-1970s. 30-year-old [[Jürgen Bogs]] became the new coach in 1977.{{cite magazine |author= |date=August 1977 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201977%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201977-78.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag GmbH |volume=1977 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=8 |issn=0323-6420|access-date=3 April 2023}} ===Golden era (1978–1989)=== {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo (1978–1989)}} BFC Dynamo had developed a very successful youth academy. Numerous players from the youth department were integrated into the first team during the 1970s. The average age in the team was only 22.7 years at the start of the 1978–79 season.{{cite magazine |author= |date=August 1978 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201978%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201978-79.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1978 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=6 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=24 May 2022}} BFC Dynamo under [[Jürgen Bogs]] played an aggressive football that focused on attacking.{{cite news |last1=Rosentritt |first1=Michael |last2=Schmidt-Tychsen |first2=Ingo |date=6 May 2006 |title=Die BFC-Schule |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/die-bfc-schule/708474.html |language=German |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=29 September 2023}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-U0529-0306, BFC Dynamo - Dynamo Dresden 3-1, Mannschaftsfoto.jpg|thumb|right|The team of BFC Dynamo after winning its first title in the DDR-Oberliga in 1979.]] BFC Dynamo had a very successful start in the [[1978-79 DDR-Oberliga]] and became ''[[Herbstmeister]]''. The team reached the [[1978–79 FDGB-Pokal|1979 FDGB-Pokal final]], but was defeated by 1. FC Magdeburg. Midfielder [[Lutz Eigendorf]] [[Emigration from the Eastern Bloc|defected]] to [[West Germany]] on 20 March 1979 in connection with a friendly match in [[Kaiserslautern]].{{cite news |date=12 March 2009 |title=Der Mann, der den 'Ballack der DDR' ausforschte |url=https://www.giessener-allgemeine.de/giessen/mann-ballack-ddr-ausforschte-12180899.html |newspaper=Gießener Allgemeine |location=[[Gießen]] |publisher=Mittelhessische Druck- und Verlagshaus GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=4 April 2023}} His defection was considered a slap in the face of the East German regime; Eigendorf had been one of the most promising players in East Germany.{{cite news |last=Gareth |first=Joswig |date=7 March 2018 |title='Eventuell vergiftet' |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/eventuell-vergiftet/595940?position=seiteninhalt&seite=1#seiteninhalt |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210615034859/https://11freunde.de/artikel/eventuell-vergiftet/595940?position=seiteninhalt&seite=1#seiteninhalt |archive-date=15 June 2021 |access-date=17 May 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.sport.de/news/ne3108316/der-raetselhafte-tod-des-beckenbauer-des-ostens/ |title=Der rätselhafte Tod des "Beckenbauer des Ostens" |last=Amshove |first=Ralf |date=7 March 2018 |website=sport.de |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=4 April 2023}} BFC Dynamo eventually won its first DDR-Oberliga title in 1979.{{cite magazine |last=Schlegel |first=Klaus |date=29 May 1979 |title=BFC-Konter wie nach Maß |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201979/FUWO%201979%2022.pdf |journal=Neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1979 |issue=22 |page=5 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=18 April 2022}} The team broke several league records during the 1978–79 season, such as: most number of matches won since the start of a season (10), most number of unbeaten matches since the start of a season (22), most goals scored in one season under the current format (75) and the biggest win in the DDR-Oberliga in the last 30 years (10–0 against [[FSV Zwickau|BSG Sachsenring Zwickau]] on the 17th matchday).{{cite news |author= |date=17 March 1979 |title=Im Spiel der Rekorde auch ein Rekordsieg |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLLiga/197879ST17.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=4 April 2023}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=120}} Hans-Jürgen Riediger became second best goalscorer in the 1978-79 DDR-Oberliga with 20 goals.{{cite magazine |author= |date=12 June 1979 |title=26. Spieltag: Torschützenliste |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201979/FUWO%201979%2024.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1979 |issue=24 |page=3 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=4 April 2023}} BFC Dynamo made its debut in the [[European Cup]] in 1979. The team reached the quarter-finals of the [[1979–80 European Cup]], where it faced [[Nottingham Forest]] under [[Brian Clough]]. The team won the first leg 0–1 away, after a goal by Riediger. BFC Dynamo thus became the first German team to defeat an English team in [[England]] in the European Cup.{{cite web |url=https://www1.wdr.de/stichtag/stichtag-gruendung-bfc-dynamo-100.html |title=15. Januar 1966 – "Stasi-Klub" BFC Dynamo gegründet |date=15 January 2021 |website=wdr.de |location=Cologne |publisher=[[Westdeutscher Rundfunk]] |language=de |access-date=17 April 2021}} The team won its second consecutive DDR-Oberliga title in [[1979-80 DDR-Oberliga|1980]], after defeating first-placed SG Dynamo Dresden 1–0 on the final matchday in front of 30,000 spectators at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark.{{cite news |last=Klein |first=Daniel |date=11 April 2018 |title=Der Rivale aus Berlin |url=https://www.saechsische.de/der-rivale-aus-berlin-3915035.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Sächsische Zeitung|Sächsische.de]] |location=Dresden |publisher=DDV Mediengruppe GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=27 May 2022}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=415}} The [[East Germany national football team]] won silver medal at the [[Football at the 1980 Summer Olympics|1980 Summer Olympics]] in [[Moscow]]. BFC Dynamo was represented by five players in the squad: [[Bodo Rudwaleit]], [[Artur Ullrich]], [[Norbert Trieloff]], [[Frank Terletzki]] and [[Wolf-Rüdiger Netz]]. All five played in the final against [[Czechoslovakia national football team|Czechoslovakia]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=112}} More talented players from the youth department were integrated into the first team at the end of the 1970s and in the early 1980s, such as [[Rainer Ernst]], [[Bernd Schulz]], [[Frank Rohde]], [[Falko Götz]] and [[Christian Backs]]. BFC Dynamo won the league again in [[1980-81 DDR-Oberliga|1981]], after defeating second-placed FC Carl Zeiss Jena 2–1 in a deciding match on the final matchday.{{cite book |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=2015 |title=BFC Dynamo Fußballfibel |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=CULTURCON medien, Sole trader: Bernd Oeljeschläger |page=67 |isbn=978-3-944068-38-1}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|pp=122-123}} BFC Dynamo reached the 1982 FDGB-Pokal final, but lost to SG Dynamo Dresen in a penalty shoot-out.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=430}} The team won its fourth consecutive league title in 1982, after defeating 1. FC Magdeburg 4–0 on the 23rd matchday. Supporters of BFC Dynamo invaded the pitch of the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark in celebration of the league title. It was the first pitch invasion by the supporters of BFC Dynamo in the DDR-Oberliga.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=124}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1982-0915-037, Europapokal, BFC Dynamo-Hamburger SV 1-1.jpg|thumb|right|Hans-Jürgen Riediger in the match against Hamburger SV in the 1982-83 European Cup at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark.]] BFC Dynamo was drawn against [[List of German football champions|West German champion]] [[Hamburger SV]] in the first round of the [[1982-83 European Cup]]. The first leg was played at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark. The Stasi feared riots, political demonstrations and supporters who might express sympathy for West German stars. Only 2,000 tickets were allowed for ordinary fans. The rest was instead allocated to a politically hand-picked audience.{{cite book |last=Braun |first=Jutta |year=2015 |editor1-last=Münkel |editor1-first=Daniela |title=State Security: A reader on the GDR secret police |url=https://www.stasi-unterlagen-archiv.de/assets/bstu/en/Downloads/E_bstu_lesebuch_englisch.pdf |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Stasi Records Agency]] |pages=88–90 |access-date=21 August 2019 |isbn=978-3-942130-97-4}}{{cite web |url=https://www.bpb.de/geschichte/zeitgeschichte/deutschlandarchiv/268956/der-fussballfan-in-der-ddr |title=Der Fußballfan in der DDR – zwischen staatlicher Regulierung und gesellschaftlichem Widerstand |last=Wojtaszyn |first=Dariusz |date=5 August 2018 |website=bpb.de |location=[[Bonn]] |publisher=[[Federal Agency for Civic Education]] |language=de |access-date=21 August 2019}} BFC Dynamo defender [[Norbert Trieloff]] later said: "When we came out for that game, we realized something was wrong."{{harvnb|Karas|2022|loc=p. 125 "Als wir zu diesem Spiel rauskamen, merkten wir, dass etwas nicht stimmte, erinnerte sich Norbert Trieloff."}} The match ended 1–1, with a goal by Riediger. BFC Dynamo was eventually eliminated after a 2–0 defeat at the [[Volksparkstadion]] in the return leg.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=434}} Key players on the team in the 1982–83 season were [[Bodo Rudwaleit]], Christian Backs, [[Rainer Troppa]], Frank Rohde, Frank Terletzki, Hans-Jürgen Riediger, Norbert Trieloff, [[Artur Ullrich]], Wolf-Rüdiger Netz, Michael Noack, [[Ralf Sträßer]] and Rainer Ernst.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=433}} BFC Dynamo had come to dominate the DDR-Oberliga by 1982. The team went through the entire [[1982-83 DDR-Oberliga]] undefeated. BFC Dynamo was defeated 1–2 by [[Chemnitzer FC|FC Karl-Marx-Stadt]] on the seventh matchday of the [[1983-84 DDR-Oberliga]]. It was the first loss since the 22nd matchday of the [[1981-82 DDR-Oberliga]]. BFC Dynamo had then been undefeated in 36 matches, which set a new record in the DDR-Oberliga for the longest unbeaten run.{{cite magazine |last=Hempel |first=Wolf |date=4 October 1983 |title=Primus Neuhäuser und sein Tor |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201983/FUWO%201983%2040.pdf |journal=Neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1983 |issue=40 |page=4 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=29 May 2022}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=126}} BFC Dynamo was drawn against [[FK Partizan Belgrade]] in the second round of the [[1983-84 European Cup]]. Players Falko Götz and Dirk Schlegel defected to West Germany during a shopping tour in [[Belgrade]] the day before the second leg.{{cite news |last=Gartenschläger |first=Lars |date=31 October 2013 |title=Mit Mielkes Flugzeug reiste Götz Richtung Freiheit |url=https://www.welt.de/sport/fussball/article121245362/Mit-Mielkes-Flugzeug-reiste-Goetz-Richtung-Freiheit.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=17 May 2020}} As a replacement for Götz, the talented 18-year old forward [[Andreas Thom]] from the youth department was given the chance to make his international debut in the match.{{cite news |last=Thom |first=Andreas |date=7 May 2001 |title=Mein Schicksalsspiel (IX): Nicht ohne Verluste |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/mein-schicksalsspiel-ix-nicht-ohne-verluste/225572.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=14 May 2022}} BFC Dynamo was eventually eliminated by [[AS Roma]] in the quarter finals of 1983-84 European Cup. It was the fourth time in five seasons that BFC Dynamo had been eliminated in the European Cup by an eventual finalist; three times had BFC Dynamo been eliminated by the champion: Nottingham Forrest in [[1979-80 European Cup|1979]], [[Aston Villa]] in [[1981-82 European Cup|1981]] and Hamburger SV in [[1982-83 European Cup|1982]]. BFC Dynamo captured its sixth consecutive league in 1984. Rainer Ernst became the best goal scorer in the 1983-84 DDR-Oberliga with 20 goals.{{cite magazine |author= |date=22 May 1984 |title=26. Spielag: Torschützenliste |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201984/FUWO%201984%2021.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1984 |issue=21 |page=3 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=7 April 2023}} BFC Dynamo reached the 1984 FDGB-Pokal final, but lost to SG Dynamo Dresden.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=445}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1985-0601-012, Mannschaftsfoto BFC Dynamo.jpg|thumb|right|The team of BFC Dynamo celebrates its seventh DDR-Oberliga title in 1985.]] BFC Dynamo recruited [[Frank Pastor]] from relegated [[HFC Chemie]] in 1984.{{cite magazine |author= |date=August 1984 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201984%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201984-85.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1984 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=4 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=30 May 2022}} The team was drawn against [[Aberdeen FC]] under [[Alex Ferguson]] in the first round of the [[1984–85 European Cup]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=128}} BFC Dynamo eventually won the round after dramatic penalty shoot-out at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark in the return leg. Goalkeeper Bodo Rudwaleit saved the last two penalty kicks for Aberdeen FC.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=13 November 2001 |title=Der Spind von Jürgen Bogs ist leer |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/der-spind-von-juergen-bogs-ist-leer/270446.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=3 October 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=128}} BFC Dynamo finished the [[1984-85 DDR-Oberliga]] in first place, six points ahead of SG Dynamo Dresden. The team had scored a total of 90 goals in 1984-85 DDR-Oberliga, which set a new record.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=129}} No team would ever score more goals in one season in the DDR-Oberliga. With 24 goals, Rainer Ernst was once again the best goal scorer in the league. Frank Pastor was the second best goal scorer in the league with 22 goals.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=129}} BFC Dynamo reached the [[1984-85 FDGB-Pokal|1985 FDGB-Pokal final]], but was again defeated by SC Dynamo Dresden in the final.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=453}} BFC Dynamo was in first place in the league before the winter break 1985–1986.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=455}} The team faced [[1. FC Lokomotive Leipzig]] on the 18th matchday of the [[1985-86 DDR-Oberliga]]. 1. FC Lokomotiv Leipzig led the match 1–0 in overtime. BFC Dynamo was then awarded a penalty in the 95th minute by referee [[Bernd Stumpf]].{{cite news |last=Dieckmann |first=Christoph |date=10 August 2000 |title=Pfiff löst Aufstand aus: Der Schand-Elfmeter von Leipzig |url=https://www.zeit.de/2000/33/Der_Schand-Elfmeter_von_Leipzig |newspaper=Zeit Online |language=de |issue=33/2000 |location=Hamburg |publisher=Zeit Online GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091214130252/https://www.zeit.de/2000/33/Der_Schand-Elfmeter_von_Leipzig |archive-date=14 December 2009 |access-date=21 August 2019}}{{cite news |last=Leske |first=Hanns |date=22 March 2006 |title=Foul von höchster Stelle |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/foul-von-hoechster-stelle/695462.html |language=de |access-date=8 April 2023}} Frank Pastor converted the penalty and the match ended in a 1–1 draw.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=149}} The penalty was highly controversial and would later become known as the "[[Shame penalty of Leipzig]]". BFC Dynamo won the 1985-86 DDR-Oberliga. The team finished just two points ahead of runner-up 1. FC Lokomotive Leipzig. {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo (1978–1989)#Controversy, complaints and sanctions (1985–1986)|l1 = Controversy, complaints and sanctions}} BFC Dynamo had the best material conditions in the league and the best team by far.{{cite news |last=Voss |first=Oliver |date=29 June 2004 |title=Der Schiri, der hat immer Recht |url=https://taz.de/!734771/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=3 August 2022}} But controversial refereeing decisions in favor of BFC Dynamo gave rise to speculation that the dominance of BFC Dynamo was not solely due to athletic performance, but also due to help from referees.{{cite web |url=https://gdrobjectified.wordpress.com/2013/04/19/bfc-dynamo/ |title=The Darth Vaders of East German Soccer: BFC Dynamo |last=Kleiner |first=John Paul |date=19 April 2013 |work=The GDR Objectified (gdrobjectified.wordpress.com) |location=[[Toronto]] |publisher=John Paul Kleiner |access-date=21 August 2019}} BFC Dynamo was a representative of both the Stasi and the capital. The team was therefore viewed with more suspicion than affection.{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=226}} The overbearing success of BFC Dynamo in the 1980s made fans of opposing teams easily aroused as to what they saw as manipulation by bent referees. The team was met with aggression and shouts such as "Bent champions!" ({{langx|de|Schiebermeister}}) and "Jews Berlin!" ({{langx|de|Juden Berlin}}) at away matches.{{cite news |last=Bartz |first=Dietmar |date=8 December 2003 |title="Die Stasi war nichts Spezielles" |url=https://taz.de/!669107/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=28 August 2020}}{{cite web |url=http://www.bpb.de/gesellschaft/medien-und-sport/bundesliga/166321/fussball-land-ddr?p=all |title="Die Mauer muss weg!" |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=18 June 2014 |website=bpb.de |location=Bonn |publisher=[[Federal Agency for Civic Education]] |language=de |access-date=17 May 2020}}{{sfn|McDougall|2014|pp=226-227}} Complaints of alleged referee bias accumulated into the hundreds in the mid-1980s.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|pp=148-149}} The [[East German Football Association]] (DFV) eventually conducted an internal analysis of the 1984–85 season.{{cite news |last=Völker |first=Markus |date=18 July 2015 |title=Geschichte des BFC Dynamo: Weinrote Welt ohne gelbe Karten |url=https://taz.de/Geschichte-des-BFC-Dynamo/!5214731/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=12 September 2020}} Among other things, the analysis found that BFC Dynamo had incurred only one third of the [[Penalty card#Yellow card|yellow cards]] incurred by rival SG Dynamo Dresden.{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=236}}{{refn|group=nb|The German author Steffen Karas points out that it is almost impossible to check the objectivity of the facts described in the documents about the 1984-85 season afterwards. Karas writes in his book "66 Jahre BFC Dynamo - Auswärts mit 'nem Bus" that he believes that the DFV report on the 1984–85 season, for several reasons, presents a rather one-sided or incomplete appearance. For example, the authors regularly refer to descriptions in the East German football weekly Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo), but never mention the refereeing decisions against BFC Dynamo described in FuWo. Karas raises the question of whether the report may have been prepared to legitimize forthcoming actions? It was not an uncommon occurrence in East Germany. At the time the report was written, the DFV was under political pressure to act against BFC Dynamo. Karas claims that the DFV was "forced to act".{{harvnb|Karas|2022|pp=55–56}}: "Es ist nahezu unmöglich, die geschilderten Sachverhalte im Nachhinein auf Objektivität zu überprfüfen ... Die Autoren bezogen sich oft auf Schilderungen der Neuen Fußballwoche, was generell nicht zu beantstanden ist. Allerdings wurden alle in der Neuen Fußballwoche geschilderten Entscheidungen gegen den BFC in diesem Bericht gar nicht erst erwähnt, so z.B. ein klaren, nicht geahndetes Handspiel von Schnuphase (Erfurt) im Strafraum. Die Analyse war also entweder einseitig und/oder unvollständig ... Es zeigt sich deutlich, dass einzelne Sachverhalte in diesem Bericht entweder einseitig oder übertrieben dargestellt wurden. Warum, ist schwer ermitteln. Wurden die Berichte mit dem Ziel angefertigt, bevorstehende Entschiedungen zu begründen? Das war in der DDR durchaus üblich. Schließlich war der DFV ja zum Handeln geswungen (seihe 5c)."}} A review was also made of the final of the [[1984-85 FDGB-Pokal|1985 FDGB-Pokal final]] between BFC Dynamo and SG Dynamo Dresden. This analysis concluded that 30 percent of the referee decisions were wrong, and found that 80 percent of those had been of disadvantage to SG Dynamo Dresden.{{cite journal |last=Dennis |first=Mike |year=2007 |title=Behind the Wall: East German football between state and society |url=http://www.gfl-journal.de/2-2007/dennis.pdf |journal=German as a Foreign Language |issue=2 |volume=2007 |page=67 |access-date=9 June 2019 |issn=1470-9570 |archive-date=9 April 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409232204/http://www.gfl-journal.de/2-2007/dennis.pdf }}{{cite news |last=Gröning |first=Marion |date=9 August 2013 |title=Verpfiffen |url=https://www.saechsische.de/plus/verpfiffen-2636378.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Sächsische Zeitung]] |location=Dresden |publisher=DDV Mediengruppe GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=16 May 2020}}{{sfn|McDougall|2014|pp=237-238}} A number of referees were sanctioned for their performances in matches involving BFC Dynamo in the following months, including the referees involved in the 1985 cup final.{{sfn|McDougall|2014|pp=237-238}} A particularly controversial episode was [[Shame penalty of Leipzig|the penalty]] awarded to BFC Dynamo by referee Bernd Stumpf in extra time in the match between 1. FC Lokomotive Leipzig and BFC Dynamo in March 1986.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|pp=149-151}} The penalty caused a wave of protests. [[Socialist Unity Party of Germany|SED]] General Secretary [[Erich Honecker]] and the Secretary for Security, Youth and Sport of the [[Socialist Unity Party of Germany#Central Committee|SED Central Committee]] [[Egon Krenz]] were fed up with the "football question" and the "BFC-discussion". Honecker wanted quiet. An example was consequently made out of referee Stumpf. He was permanently banned from refereeing by the DFV. The sanctions against Stumpf were approved by Honecker and Krenz in the SED Central Committee.{{cite news |last1=Horeni |first1=Michael |last2=Reinsch |first2=Michael |date=8 November 2009 |title=Fußballautor Leske im Gespräch: "Von Manipulationen die Schnauze voll" |url=https://www.faz.net/aktuell/sport/fussball/fussballautor-leske-im-gespraech-von-manipulationen-die-schnauze-voll-1881858.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung]] |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170601132341/https://www.faz.net/aktuell/sport/fussball/fussballautor-leske-im-gespraech-von-manipulationen-die-schnauze-voll-1881858.html |archive-date=1 June 2017 |access-date=20 August 2019}}{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=149-151}} However, a previously unknown video recording of the match was published by [[Mitteldeutscher Rundfunk]] (MDR) in 2000. The video recording showed that the penalty was correctly awarded and that the sanctions against Stumpf were unjustified.{{cite news |last=Hahn |first=Anne |date=12 May 2022 |title=BFC Dynamo: Der Mythos des Schiebermeisters |url=https://www.zeit.de/sport/2022-05/bfc-dynamo-ddr-oberliga-zehn-meisterschaften |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Zeit]] |location=Hamburg |publisher=Zeit Online GmbH |access-date=2 June 2022}} In an interview in 2000, Stumpf said: "The people have never understood, how this Leipzig game was used by the highest officials in the party and government." The benefit of controlling important matches in Western Europe might have put indirect pressure on the referees to take preventive measures, in so-called preventive obedience.{{cite book |last=Leske |first=Hanns |date=2004 |title=Erich Mielke, die Stasi und das runde Leder: Der Einfluß der SED und des Ministeriums für Staatssicherheit auf den Fußballsport in der DDR |language=de |edition=1st |location=[[Göttingen]] |publisher=Verlag Die Werkstatt GmbH |pages=530–533 |isbn=978-3-89533-448-1 |author-link=Hanns Leske}}{{cite journal |last=Dennis |first=Mike |year=2007 |title=Behind the Wall: East German football between state and society |url=http://www.gfl-journal.de/2-2007/dennis.pdf |journal=German as a Foreign Language |issue=2 |volume=2007 |page=66 |access-date=21 August 2019 |issn=1470-9570 |archive-date=9 April 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409232204/http://www.gfl-journal.de/2-2007/dennis.pdf }}{{cite book |last1=Dennis |last2=LaPorte |first1=Mike |first2=Norman |date=2011 |title=State and Minorities in Communist East Germany |location=[[New York (state)|New York]] |edition=1st |publisher=Berghahn Books |page=134 |isbn=978-0-85745-195-8}}{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=150}}{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=226}} In order pursue an international career, a referee would need a travel permit, confirmed by the Stasi.{{cite book |last=Münkel |first=Daniela |year=2015 |title=State Security: A reader on the GDR secret police |url=https://www.bstu.de/assets/bstu/en/Downloads/E_bstu_lesebuch_englisch.pdf |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Stasi Records Agency]] |page=91 |access-date=21 August 2019 |isbn=978-3-942130-97-4}} It became known after the German reunification that several referees had also been [[Unofficial collaborator]]s (IM) of the Stasi.{{cite book |last1=Tomlinson |last2=Young |first1=Alan |first2=Christopher |date=2006 |title=German Football: History, Culture, Society |location=[[Abingdon-on-Thames]] |edition=1st |publisher=Routledge, Taylor & Francis Group |page=55 |isbn=0-415-35195-2}}{{cite book |last=Leske |first=Hanns |year=2012 |chapter=Schiedsrichter im Sold der Staatssicherheit |title=Fußball in der DDR: Kicken im Auftrag der SED |language=de |location=Erfurt |edition=2nd |publisher=Landeszentrale für politische Bildung Thüringen |isbn=978-3-937967-91-2}}{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|pp=148-149}} However, there is no evidence to show that referees were under direct instructions from the Stasi and no document has ever been found in the archives that gave the Stasi a mandate to bribe referees.{{sfn|Dennis|Grix|2012|p=150}} {{Quote box | quote = "I can imagine there was referee manipulation due to the immense pressure from the government and Ministry for State Security. That could have made some referees nervous and influenced their decisions. But we were the strongest team at the time. We didn't need their help." | author = [[Falko Götz]]{{cite news |last=Crossland |first=David |date=14 January 2016 |title=Dynamo Berlin: The soccer club 'owned' by the Stasi |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2016/01/13/football/dynamo-berlin-stasi-east-germany-football/index.html |work=[[CNN International]] |location=[[Atlanta]] |publisher=Cable News Network, Inc. |access-date=30 May 2019}} | source = | align = right | width = 25em }} The picture that the success of BFC Dynamo relied upon referee bias is dismissed by former BFC Dynamo players and coaches. Some of them admit that there might have been cases of referee bias, but they all insist that it was the thoroughness of their youth work and the quality of their play that earned them their titles.{{cite news |date=8 November 1999 |title=Andreas Thom über Dynamo und Stasi, Partys mit DDR-Prominenz und seinen Wechsel von Ost nach West |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/andreas-thom-ueber-dynamo-und-stasi-partys-mit-ddr-prominenz-und-seinen-wechsel-von-ost-nach-west/102972.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=2 September 2020 |quote=Und auf die Schiedsrichter gesetzt? Blödsinn. Zehnmal hintereinander Meister zu werden, das klingt vielleicht komisch, aber da steckt auch Arbeit und Können dahinter. Natürlich gab es auch mal Entscheidungen, über die wir selbst gestaunt haben.}}{{cite news |last=Lachmann |first=Michael |date=7 December 2016 |title=BFC-Idol Frank Terletzki: "Am schönsten waren immer unsere Siege gegen Union" |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/berlin-sport/bfc-dynamo-berlin/am-schoensten-waren-immer-unsere-siege-gegen-union |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=28 September 2020}} Former coach Jürgen Bogs has said: "You cannot postpone 26 matches in one season in the DDR-Oberliga. At that time we had the best football team".{{cite news |last=Jahn |first=Michael |date=10 April 2013 |title=Der große Fehler |url=https://www.fr.de/sport/sport-mix/grosse-fehler-11352133.html |work=[[Frankfurter Rundschau]] |language=de |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=Frankfurter Rundschau GmbH |access-date=21 September 2021 |quote='26 Spiele in einer Saison in der DDR-Oberliga kannst du nicht verschieben. Wir hatten zu dieser Zeit die fußballerisch beste Mannschaft.}} German author Steffen Karas claims that BFC Dynamo only scored nine match-deciding goals in the 86th minute or later, in the 218 matches it won or drew during its ten championship years. Only one of those goals came from a penalty.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=47}}{{refn|group=nb|That penalty was the [[Shame penalty of Leipzig|controversial penalty]] against 1. FC Lokomotive Leipzig in the 1985-86 DDR-Oberliga on 22 March 1986, which was later proven to be correct.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=47}}}} Supported by numerous training centers (TZ) of the [[SV Dynamo]] sports association, BFC Dynamo was able to filter the best talents through nationwide screening and train them in its youth academy. The youth academy had full-time trainers employed for every age group.{{cite news |last=Stolz |first=Sascha |date=7 August 2006 |title=Interview mit Jürgen Bogs |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2006_08_07_2.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=13 November 2021}} As the coach of the DDR-Oberliga team, Bogs worked with modern training methods, such as video evalutations, which was not yet common in East Germany. The club also applied heart rate and lactate measurements during training, which only came to the Bundesliga many years later.{{cite news |last=Krause |first=Thomas |date=19 January 2022 |title="Der BFC Dynamo wird immer mein Club sein" |url=https://www.nordkurier.de/sportnachrichten/der-bfc-dynamo-wird-immer-mein-club-sein-1946787001.html |work=[[Nordkurier]] |language=de |location=[[Neubrandenburg]] |publisher=Nordkurier Mediengruppe GmbH & Co. KG |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220119230017/https://www.nordkurier.de/sportnachrichten/der-bfc-dynamo-wird-immer-mein-club-sein-1946787001.html |archive-date=19 January 2022 |access-date=20 January 2022}} Former referee [[Bernd Heynemann]] has concluded: "The BFC is not ten times champions because the referees only whistled for Dynamo. They were already strong as a bear."{{cite news |last=Schäfer |first=Guido |date=11 October 2017 |title=Bernd Heynemann im Interview: "Wir brauchen kein Big Brother" |url=https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/bernd-heynemann-im-interview-wir-brauchen-kein-big-brother/ |language=de |newspaper=Sportbuzzer |location=Hannover |publisher=Sportbuzzer GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201103002011/https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/bernd-heynemann-im-interview-wir-brauchen-kein-big-brother/ |archive-date=3 November 2020 |access-date=30 October 2020 |quote=Der BFC ist nicht x-mal Meister geworden, weil die Schiris nur für Dynamo gepfiffen haben. Die waren schon bärenstark.}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1988-0406-032, BFC Dynamo - SG Dynamo Dresden 1-0.jpg|thumb|left|Andreas Thom during a match against SG Dynamo Dresden in 1988.]] BFC Dynamo recruited 20-year-old [[Thomas Doll]] from relegated [[F.C. Hansa Rostock]] in 1986.{{cite magazine |author= |date=August 1986 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201986%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201986-87.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1986 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=4 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=11 April 2023}} Doll and Andreas Thom would form one of the most effective attacking duos in East German football in the late 1980s. The [[1986-87 DDR-Oberliga|1986–87]] and [[1987-88 DDR-Oberliga|1987-88 seasons]] saw renewed competition in the DDR-Oberliga. BFC Dynamo eventually won its tenth consecutive league title in 1988. The 1987-88 DDR-Oberliga was won on goal difference in the final matchday.{{cite magazine |author= |date=25 May 1988 |title=25. Spieltag: Die Saison der dramatik hält uns weiter in Atem |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201988/FUWO%201988%2021.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1988 |issue=21 |page=3 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=3 August 2022}}{{cite magazine |last=Nöldner |first=Jürgen |date=31 May 1988 |title=Ein Tor - zwei Welten |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201988/FUWO%201988%2022.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1988 |issue=22 |page=4 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=12 June 2022}} Andreas Thom became the best goalscorer in 1987-88 DDR-Oberliga with 20 goals.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=132}} The team then defeated FC Carl Zeiss Jena in the [[1987-88 FDGB-Pokal|1988 FDGB-Pokal final]] and finally completed the [[Double (association football)|Double]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=132}} Thom was voted the [[Footballer of the Year (Germany)#Footballer of the Year for East Germany|1988 East German footballer of the year]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=132}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1989-0401-022, FDGB-Pokal, Finale, BFC Dynamo - FC Karl-Marx-Stadt 1-0.jpg|thumb|right|The team celebrates the victory in the 1989 FDGB-Pokal final at the [[Stadion der Weltjugend]].]] BFC Dynamo was drawn against West German champion [[SV Werder Bremen]] in the first round of the [[1988-89 European Cup]]. BFC Dynamo sensationally won the first leg 3–0 at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark.{{cite web |url=https://www.ndr.de/sport/fussball/sportmomente104_page-1.html |title=Werder und das "Wunder von der Weser" |last=Bellinger |first=Andreas |date=11 October 2018 |website=ndr.de |location=Hamburg |publisher=[[Norddeutscher Rundfunk]] |language=de |access-date=7 July 2020}} However, the team lost the return leg at the [[Weser-Stadion]] with 0–5. The return leg would become known as the second "Miracle on the Weser".{{cite news |last=Goldmann |first=Sven |date=10 October 2008 |title=Fußball-Historie: Die Wunde von der Weser |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/fussball-historie-die-wunde-von-der-weser/1343116.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=12 June 2022}} BFC Dynamo defeated FC Karl-Marx-Stadt in the [[1988-89 FDGB-Pokal|1989 FDGB-Pokal final]] and won its second consecutive cup title.{{cite magazine |last=Nöldner |first=Jürgen |date=4 April 1989 |title=Über die Logik des Sieges |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPokal/198889pokFH.html |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1989 |issue=14 |pages=3–4 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=21 June 2022}} SG Dynamo Dresden eventually broke the dominance of BFC Dynamo in the DDR-Oberliga in the [[1988-89 DDR-Oberliga|1988-89 season]]. BFC Dynamo finished the 1988-89 DDR-Oberliga as runner-up, and SG Dynamo Dresden became the new champion. Coach Bogs was replaced by Helmut Jäschke after the 1988–89 season.{{sfn|Karas|2022|pp=134-135}} As the cup winner, BFC Dynamo was set to play the new league champion SG Dynamo Dresden in the first edition of the [[DFV-Supercup]]. BFC Dynamo won the match 4-1 and became the first and, eventually, only winner of the DFV-Supercup in the history of East German football.{{sfn|Karas|2022|pp=134-135}} ===FC Berlin, decline and insolvency (1989–2004)=== {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo (1989–2004)}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1990-0223-013, Mannschaftsfoto FC Berlin.jpg|thumb|upright=1.15|left|The team of FC Berlin at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Stadion in 1990.]]The 1989–90 season was marked by the [[Peaceful Revolution|political change in East Germany]]. The [[Berlin Wall]] was opened on 9 November 1989 and people in East Berlin could now travel freely to West Berlin.{{cite web |url=https://www.stasi-unterlagen-archiv.de/informationen-zur-stasi/themen/beitrag/botschafter-im-trainingsanzug/ |title="Botschafter im Trainingsanzug" |website=stasi-unterlagen-archiv.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Stasi Records Agency]] |language=de |access-date=19 August 2021}} [[Andreas Thom]] was signed to [[Bayer Leverkusen]]. He left the team during the winter break 1989–1990 and became the first player in the DDR-Oberliga to be transferred to the West German [[Bundesliga]] after the fall of the Berlin Wall.{{cite news |last=Gartenschläger |first=Lars |date=9 November 2014 |title=Im Plattenbau machte Calmund mit Thom alles klar |url=https://www.welt.de/sport/fussball/bundesliga/bayer-leverkusen/article134144235/Im-Plattenbau-machte-Calmund-mit-Thom-alles-klar.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Welt]] |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=19 August 2021}} The [[Stasi]] was definitively dissolved on 13 January 1990 and thus BFC Dynamo lost a major sponsor. The East German Ministry of the Interior announced that it was only prepared to support the club until the end of the 1989–90 season and the fate of the club was uncertain.{{cite news |last=Hönel |first=Manfred |date=17 January 1990 |title=Wohin rollt der Ball bei Dynamo? |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1990_01_17.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Junge Welt]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Junge Welt GmbH |access-date=19 August 2021}}{{cite magazine |last=Nöldner |first=Jürgen |date=6 February 1990 |title=Mehr Sorgen um die Zukunft als um die Punkte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201990/FUWO%201990%2006.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag, Berlin |volume=1990 |issue=6 |page=6 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=19 August 2021}} BFC Dynamo was eventually rebranded as FC Berlin on 19 February 1990.{{cite magazine |date=27 February 1990 |title=Nicht nur Buchstabentausch |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201990/FUWO%201990%2009.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag, Berlin |volume=1990 |issue=9 |page=2 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=20 August 2021}}{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=317}} The team finished the [[1989-90 DDR-Oberliga]] in fourth place and failed for the first time in a long time to qualify for an UEFA competition. [[Thomas Doll]] and [[Frank Rohde]] left for [[Hamburger SV]] and [[Rainer Ernst]] for [[1. FC Kaiserslautern]] after the season.{{cite news |last=Lieske |first=Matti |date=10 May 1990 |title=Trauriger Abgesang |url=https://taz.de/!1768862/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=9 May 2022}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1990-1110-013, FC Berlin - HFC Chemie 0-0.jpg|thumb|right|FC Berlin forward [[Dirk Rehbein]] in a match against [[Hallescher FC|HFC Chemie]] in 1990.]] [[Jürgen Bogs]] returned as coach in 1990.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=140}} FC Berlin finished the [[1990-91 NOFV-Oberliga]] in 11th place, but qualified for the [[1990–91 NOFV-Oberliga#2. Bundesliga play-off|play-off for the 2. Bundesliga]]. The team just narrowly failed to qualify for the [[2. Bundesliga]].{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/05/fussball-letzte-saison-ddr-oberliga-vor-30-jahren-bfc-dynamo-fc-berlin.html |title=Letzte DDR-Oberliga-Saison von BFC Dynamo: Den Absprung verpasst |date=26 May 2021 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211029175404/https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/05/fussball-letzte-saison-ddr-oberliga-vor-30-jahren-bfc-dynamo-fc-berlin.html |archive-date=29 October 2021 |access-date=20 June 2021}} More players left the team after 1990–91 season, including [[Heiko Bonan]] for [[VfL Bochum]], [[Burkhard Reich]] for [[Karlsruher SC]] and [[Hendrik Herzog]] for [[FC Schalke 04]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=142}} The 1991–92 season was the first season when teams from East Germany and teams from [[West Germany]] played in the same league system. The [[NOFV-Oberliga]] was now at third tier in the [[German football league system]]. [[Defender (association football)#Sweeper (libero)|Sweeper]] [[Heiko Brestrich]] returned the team in 1991.{{cite magazine |author= |date=29 July 1991 |title=DIE FUSSBALL-BÖRSE |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201991/FUWO%201991%2031.pdf |journal= Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag GmbH |volume=1991 |issue=31 |page=11 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=22 April 2023}} The team was also joined by defender Jens Reckmann from the youth department the same year.{{cite news |last=Karkossa |first=Bernd |date=5 May 2003 |title=Back to the roots |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2003_05_05.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=6 October 2021}} Brestrich and Reckmann would be two of the most capped players of FC Berlin in the 1990s.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=197}} FC Berlin dominated the [[1991-92 NOFV-Oberliga]], but failed for the second season in a row to qualify for the 2. Bundesliga. FC Berlin lost 11 players after the 1991–92 season.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=144}} In total, the club lost two complete teams in the first year or two after the fall of the Berlin Wall: 22 players had left for the Bundesliga and 13 players for the 2. Bundesliga.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=144}} After failing to qualify for the 2. Bundesliga in 1991 and 1992, FC Berlin had to continue at amateur level. The club now had to rely heavily on its youth department to supply the team with new players.{{cite news |last=Wittich |first=Elke |date=11 May 1992 |title=License to kick |url=https://taz.de/!1670795/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=20 August 2021}} Coach Bogs resigned in September 1993 and was replaced by Helmut Koch.{{sfn|Karas|2022|pp=145-146}} FC Berlin managed to qualify for the new [[Regionalliga Nordost]] in 1994. The reinstated Regionalliga formed the new third tier. The [[1994-95 Regionalliga|1994-95 Regionalliga Nordost]] involved new [[Berlin derby|derbies]] against 1. FC Union Berlin and new matches against several other well-known opponents.{{cite web |url=https://www.turus.net/sport/fussball/10013-hochphasen-und-tristesse-beim-bfc-dynamo-foto-rueckblick-von-1994-bis-2018.html |title=Hochphasen und Tristesse beim BFC Dynamo: (Foto-)Rückblick von 1994 bis 2018 |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=7 December 2018 |work=turus.net |location=Essen |publisher=Karsten Höft |language=de |access-date=27 December 2021}} FC Berlin struggled in the 1994-95 Regionalliga Nordost but managed to retain its place in the league. The highlight of the [[1995-96 Regionalliga|1995-96 Regionaliga Nordost]] were then new duels with [[1. FC Dynamo Dresden]]. The two teams had not met since 1991.{{cite book |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=2015 |title=BFC Dynamo Fußballfibel |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=CULTURCON medien, Sole trader: Bernd Oeljeschläger |isbn=978-3-944068-38-1}}{{rp|116}} Due to a threat of relegation, coach Koch was dismissed in October 1995. He was succeeded by former player and youth coach [[Werner Voigt]].{{cite news |last=Burghause |first=Hans Günter |date=24 June 1996 |title=Saisonrückblick 1995/96: Ex-Trainer Werner Voigt kam nach der zwölften Runde als Notbremser |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1996_06_24.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=22 August 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=147}} Defender [[Mario Maek]] also returned to the team at the same time. FC Berlin organized one of the biggest youth football tournaments so far in the eastern part of the country in April 1996. As many as 30 youth teams from clubs such as [[Chelsea F.C.]], [[Feyenoord]], [[SK Rapid Wien]], [[FC Spartak Moscow]], [[FC Bayern München]] and [[Borussia Dortmund]] participated.{{cite news |last=Burghause |first=Hans Günter |date=28 March 1996 |title=Talente europäischer Top-Klubs beim FC Berlin |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/archiv/bayern-muenchen-rapid-wien-fc-chelsea-feyenoord-rotterdam-kommen-zum-fussball-turnier-nach-hohenschoenhausen-talente-europaeischer-top-klubs-beim-fc-berlin-li.745111 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=7 March 2024}} FC Berlin finished the [[1995-96 Regionalliga|1995-96 Regionaliga Nordost]] in 13th place. Club President Volkmar Wanski announced in November 1996 that the financial reserves had been used up; the millions of [[Deutsche Mark]] that the club had made from player transfers in the early 1990s were now gone.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=147}}{{cite news |last=Wittich |first=Elke |date=28 October 1998 |title=BFC ist FCB ist BFC |url=https://jungle.world/artikel/1998/44/bfc-ist-fcb-ist-bfc |work=[[Jungle World]] |language=de |volume=1988 |issue=44 |location=Berlin |publisher=Jungle World Verlags GmbH |access-date=22 August 2021}} Defender [[Jörn Lenz]] returned to the team during the winter break 1997–1998.{{cite news |date=26 January 1998 |title=Wie auf gepackten Koffern |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1998_01_26.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=23 August 2021}} Lenz would be a key player for several seasons to come.{{sfn|Karas|2022|pp=546, 553, 561, 568, 579, 587, 594, 602}} The successes in the Regionalliga did not materialize; FC Berlin remained a lower-table side. Coach Voigt eventually left for 1. FC Dynamo Dresden in March 1998.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=148}} Henry Häusler became new coach in 1998.{{cite news |last=Kepler |first=Wolf |date=June 1998 |title=Höhere Ansprüche |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1998_07_00.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |volume=1998 |issue=Fußballwoche-Sonderheft Sommer 1998/99 |access-date=23 August 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=149}} Among the key players on the team in the 1998–99 season were Heiko Brestrich, Jörn Lenz, Mario Kallnik and Mario Maek.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=546}} Coach Häusler was already dismissed in April 1999, due to repeated public criticism of the team.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=149}} The club took back its old name of BFC Dynamo on 8 May 1999.{{cite news |date=4 May 1999 |title=FC Berlin heißt wieder BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.spiegel.de/sport/fussball/regionalliga-fc-berlin-heisst-wieder-bfc-dynamo-a-20614.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Spiegel|Spiegel]] |publisher=Der Spiegel GmbH & Co. KG |location=Hamburg |access-date=23 August 2021}} BFC Dynamo defeated [[Berlin Turkspor 1965]] 4–1 in the final of the 1998-99 Berlin Cup and finally won its first [[Berlin Cup]] title.{{cite web |url=https://www.turus.net/sport/5580-motsche-laesst-den-bfc-dynamo-ins-pokalfinale-einziehen.html |title=Motsche lässt den BFC Dynamo ins Pokalfinale einziehen |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=8 January 2018 |work=turus.net |location=Essen |publisher=Karsten Höft |language=de |access-date=12 August 2022}} Brestrich scored two goals, [[Ayhan Gezen]] one goal and Maek one goal for BFC Dynamo in the final. Former [[FC Rot-Weiß Erfurt]]-player and coach Klaus Goldbach became new coach at the end of the season.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=149}} BFC Dynamo recruited goalkeeper [[Nico Thomaschewski]] from 1.
