FC Union Berlin in 1999.{{cite news |last=Stoltz |first=Saca |date=3 August 1999 |title=Routine soll dem BFC Dynamo helfen |url=https://www.welt.de/print-welt/article579379/Routine-soll-dem-BFC-Dynamo-helfen.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Welt]] |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=15 January 2023}}{{cite news |last=Göschel |first=Wolfram |date=3 August 1999 |title=Dynamo Berlin gibt Qualifikation für die dritte Liga als Saisonziel aus |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/dynamo-berlin-gibt-qualifikation-fuer-die-dritte-liga-als-saisonziel-aus/84712.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=23 August 2021}} BFC Dynamo suffered a period of crisis during the autumn of 1999; the club had run into financial difficulties, and the team had plummeted down the league after several matches without a single win.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=23 October 1999 |title=Angst vor der Versenkung |url=https://taz.de/!1265437/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=22 August 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=150}} Heiko Brestrich was sacked after a protest against coach Goldbach.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=150}} Brestrich had played 282 matches for the team between 1991 and 1999.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=197}} Eventually, Jürgen Bogs returned for his third stint as coach in December 1999.{{cite news |last=Färber |first=Michael |date=13 December 1999 |title=Zurück in die Zukunft |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1999_12_13.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=23 August 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=150}} Hans Reker became the new sporting director in January 2000. Through Reker, BFC Dynamo finally got a promising main sponsor in the form of the software company Lipro AG.{{cite news |last=Kepler |first=Wolf |date=7 February 2000 |title=Neuer Sponsor |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2000_02_07.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}}{{cite news |last=Becht-Zwetkov |first=Diana |date=8 March 2000 |title=Chance für Jung-Kicker |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2000_03_08.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} However, the struggle in the league continued and BFC Dynamo finished the 1999-2000 Regionalliga in 17th place. The team was thus relegated to [[NOFV-Oberliga Nord]]; for the first time, BFC Dynamo was a fourth-tier team.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=151}} After six years in office, Club President Wanski resigned in June 2000, in protest against Lipro AG's demand for influence.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=28 June 2000 |title=Der Sponsor regiert beim BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/archiv/praesident-wanski-tritt-nach-sechs-jahren-zurueck-der-sponsor-regiert-beim-bfc-dynamo-li.1093004 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=7 March 2024}} About a dozen new players were signed in the summer of 2000, including five Romanian players, four of whom were former national team players.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=10 July 2000 |title=Ronaldos Partner stürmt für den BFC |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2000_07_10.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=24 August 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=152}} [[Social democracy|Social democratic]] [[Social Democratic Party of Germany|SPD]]-politician Karin Halsch became the new club president in September 2000.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=28 September 2000 |title=Was glauben Sie – kann das gut gehen? – Der Ober-Dynamo ist jetzt eine Frau , BFC-Paukenschlag! |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/was-glauben-sie-kann-das-gut-gehen |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=29 August 2020}}{{refn|group=nb|Karin Halsch was known as Karin Seidel-Kalmutzki at the time.}} BFC Dynamo finished the [[2000–01 NOFV-Oberliga#Nord|2000–01 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]] in first place.{{cite news |last=Schlichting |first=Sbastian |date=1 June 2001 |title=Existenzangst |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_06_01.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} BFC Dynamo striker Denis Kozlov became the top scorer in the league with 29 goals. The team faced [[1. FC Magdeburg]] in the [[2000–01 NOFV-Oberliga#Promotion playoff|play-off for the Regionalliga Nord]]. However, two weeks before the first leg, it had become clear that the club was in major financial trouble.{{cite news |last=Höfgren |first=Ingmar |date=18 May 2001 |title=Geht BFC die Kohle aus? |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_05_18.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=28 June 2023}}{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=27 June 2001 |title=Nach dem Rücktritt der Präsidentin munkelt man beim BFC Dynamo von dubiosen Konten: Rette sich, wer kann |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_06_27.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=24 August 2021}}{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=27 June 2001 |title=Nach dem Rücktritt der Präsidentin munkelt man beim BFC Dynamo von dubiosen Konten: Rette sich, wer kann |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/nach-dem-ruecktritt-der-praesidentin-munkelt-man-beim-bfc-dynamo-von-dubiosen-konten-rette-sich-wer-kann-li.21053?pid=true |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=24 August 2021}} Players had not received their salaries for months and the club was behind on insurance payments. BFC Dynamo lost the play-off after a 5–2 defeat away in the second leg. Several players left the team immediately after the season.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=19 June 2001 |title=BFC Dynamo: Der Klub steht vor der Insolvenz |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/bfc-dynamo-der-klub-steht-vor-der-insolvenz/235294.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} The insurance company AOK eventually filed for insolvency against BFC Dynamo in June 2001.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=26 June 2001 |title=Wildwest: Präsidentin Seidel-Kalmutzki tritt zurück, vier Millionen Mark Schulden drücken |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_06_26.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} Shortly afterwards, Halsch resigned as club president and Reker took over as acting president.{{cite news |last=Völker |first=Markus |date=8 October 2001 |title="Eine fixe Idee" |url=https://taz.de/Eine-fixe-Idee/!1147749/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=30 August 2020}} The club's total debts were now estimated at 5.5 millions Deutsche Mark.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=12 July 2001 |title=Balanceakt am Rande der Insolvenz: Der BFC Dynamo hofft auf geduldige Gläubiger |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_07_12.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=1 October 2021}} BFC Dynamo tried to initiate a partnership with [[FC Dynamo Moscow]], but the plans led nowhere.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=23 August 2001 |title=Der Bruder hilft |url=https://taz.de/Der-Bruder-hilft/!1155268/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=1 October 2021}}{{cite news |last=Becht-Zwetkov |first=Diana |date=31 August 2001 |title=Hoffnung auf Rettung: BFC Dynamos heißer Tanz am Abgrund |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_08_31.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=2 October 2021}} BFC Dynamo needed 30,000 Deutsche Mark by 31 October 2001 to open insolvency proceedings, but the club did not have the money.{{cite news |date=26 October 2001 |title=Marsch gegen Zwangsabstieg |url=https://taz.de/Marsch-gegen-Zwangsabstieg/!1144475/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=2 October 2021}}{{cite news |last=Wolff |first=Matthias |title=Der BFC Dynamo geht in Insolvenz: Fans demonstrieren gegen den Fall in die Kreisliga C |date=23 October 2001 |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/archiv/fans-demonstrieren-gegen-den-fall-in-die-kreisliga-c-der-bfc-dynamo-geht-in-insolvenz-li.1346823 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=2 October 2021}}{{cite news |last=Meyer |first=Ulli |date=20 October 2001 |title=Der BFC Dynamo steht vor dem Aus: Dem DDR-Rekordmeister droht ab 1. November das Insolvenzverfahren |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_10_20.html |work=[[Märkische Allgemeine]] |language=de |location=Hannover |publisher=Verlagsgesellschaft Madsack GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=2 October 2021}} If insolvency proceedings could not be opened, the club would go bankcrupt. Supporters of BFC Dynamo staged a demonstration march from [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen]] to [[Rotes Rathaus]] to save the club. Also former players such as Hans-Jürgen Riediger, [[Rainer Troppa]], [[Waldemar Ksienzyk]] and [[Heiko Brestrich]], planned to participate.{{cite news |date=23 October 2001 |title=Der November rückt näher |url=https://taz.de/Der-November-rueckt-naeher/!1145091/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=2 October 2021}} A sponsor group around former club president Wanski eventually came forward at the last second and offered the money.{{cite news |date=27 October 2001 |title=BFC feiert Rettung, Vize Reker tritt ab |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/bfc-feiert-rettung-vize-reker-tritt-ab |language=de |work=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=2 October 2021}}{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=27 October 2001 |title=BFC Dynamo plant für die Verbandsliga: Maroder Klub hat angeblich neue Geldquelle aufgetan |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_10_27.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=2 October 2021}} BFC Dynamo, and then FC Berlin, was said to have made millions from player sales after ''[[Peaceful Revolution|Die Wende]]''.{{cite news |last=Holz |first=Jürgen |date=10 October 2001 |title=Wie tief steckt der BFC Dynamo in der Krise? |url=https://www.nd-aktuell.de/artikel/6217.wie-tief-steckt-der-bfc-dynamo-in-der-krise.html |language=de |work=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=2 October 2021}} The club was for a time considered the richest amateur club in Germany. But not all the money had gone to the club. Some had also gone to [[SV Dynamo]], the [[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|East German Football Association]] (DFV) and agents.{{cite thesis |last1=Farshi |last2=Hadi |first1=Sabbagh |first2=Mohammad |date=20 May 2011 |title=Deutsch-Deutsche Transfers: Der Wechsel von Thomas Doll vom BFC Dynamo zum HSV 1990 |location=Hamburg |publisher=[[Hochschule Mittweida]]|publication-place=[[Mittweida]]|url=https://monami.hs-mittweida.de/frontdoor/deliver/index/docId/1235/file/BA_Arbeit_Pruefungsexemplar_Hadi_Farshi.pdf |language=de |page=41 |access-date=19 August 2021}}{{cite magazine |last=Nöldner |first=Jürgen |date=19 December 1989 |title=Beim Geld setzte das Schweigen ein |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201989/FUWO%201989%2051.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1989 |issue=51 |page=2 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=29 June 2023}} The club's reputation as a former Stasi club made it difficult to win new sponsors.{{cite news |last=Jahn |first=Michael |date=10 May 1997 |title=Millionen eingenommen – nichts auf dem Konto |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1997_05_10.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=22 August 2021}}{{cite news |last=Stolz |first=Sascha |date=5 May 1999 |title=Zurück in die Zukunft:FC Berlin heißt wieder BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.welt.de/print-welt/article571078/Zurueck-in-die-Zukunft-FC-Berlin-heisst-wieder-BFC-Dynamo.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=2 October 2021}}{{cite news |last=Jahn |first=Michael |date=24 December 2001 |title=Dynamischer Ausverkauf |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/archiv/beim-einst-gut-betuchten-bfc-dynamo-regiert-nun-der-insolvenzverwalter-dynamischer-ausverkauf-li.1003222 |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=6 March 2024}} The club was also plagued by hooliganism which repeatedly made negative headlines.{{cite news |date=17 October 2000 |title=BFC Dynamo: Rot-Weinrot bei Mielkes Lieblingsklub |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/berlin/bfc-dynamo-rot-weinrot-bei-mielkes-lieblingsklub/172664.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} FC Berlin failed to qualify for the 2. Bundesliga and never progressed beyond third tier.{{cite news |last=Bardow |first=Dominik |date=17 January 2010 |title=Fußballer auf der Flucht |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/geschichte-fussballer-auf-der-flucht/1665774.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=20 August 2021}} Average attendance was only a couple of hundreds in the early 1990s. FC Berlin ran a large youth department which at one point cost 400,000 Deutsche Marks a year. At the end of 1996, the club had no money left in its bank accounts and Club President Volkmar Wanski would need to support the club with annual personal financial contributions. In 2000, the club finally got a promising main sponsor in the form of Lipro AG and made a bid to reach the third tier.{{cite news |last=Höfgen |first=Ingmar |date=November 2001 |title=Seit Anfang des Monats läuft gegen den BFC Dynamo ein Insolvenz-Verfahren. Damit muss der zehnmalige DDR-Meister nächste Saison in der 5. Liga neu anfangen. |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_11_00.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} However, the millions from Lipro AG later turned out to be loans. Insolvency proceedings were opened on 1 November 2001. BFC Dynamo was automatically relegated to the [[Berlin-Liga|Verbandsliga Berlin]] and had to continue under amateur conditions.{{cite news |last=Höfgen |first=Ingmar |date=2 November 2001 |title=Das Insolvenzverfahren wird heute eröffnet |url=https://www.kicker.de/das-insolvenzverfahren-wird-heute-eroeffnet-255479/artikel |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=28 November 2021}} Only three players from the former squad remained for the first mandatory friendly match, including new team captain Piotr Rowicki.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=5 November 2011 |title=Bogs will ein Konzept sehen |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_11_05_2.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=3 October 2021}} Jörn Lenz left for [[1. FC Lokomotive Leipzig|VfB Leipzig]] and Nico Thomaschewski for [[SV Babelsberg 03]].{{cite news |last=Holz |first=Jürgen |date=7 November 2001 |title="Bunte Truppe" will durchhalten |url=https://www.nd-aktuell.de/artikel/7669.bunte-truppe-will-durchhalten.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} Also coach Bogs left. Mario Maek took over as new coach on a voluntary basis assisted by goalkeeping coach [[Bodo Rudwaleit]]. Entrepreneur Mike Peters was elected as the new club president in May 2002.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=3 June 2002 |title=Neue Hoffnung mit neuem Präsidenten |url=https://www.nd-aktuell.de/artikel/18186.neue-hoffnung-mit-neuem-praesidenten.html |language=de |work=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=4 October 2021}} BFC Dynamo was estimated to have debts of around 2,2 million Euros.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=20 April 2002 |title=Die Ängste des BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/archiv/noch-droht-insolvenz-die-aengste-des-bfc-dynamo-li.972055 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=14 April 2024}} The preferential claims of about 200,000 Euros seemed insurmountable, but supporters negotiated with creditors and received numerous waivers, and also raised thousands of Euros themselves.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first= Matthias |date=31 May 2001 |title=Auferstehen aus Ruinen: Beim BFC Dynamo formiert sich ein neuer Vorstand |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2002_05_31.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=4 October 2021}}{{cite news |author= |date=2 June 2002 |title=Peters-Crew sorgt für frischen Wind |url=https://www.kicker.at/peters-crew-sorgt-fuer-frischen-wind-266644/artikel |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=26 January 2023}} In addition, the new presidium around Peters gave a large financial contribution to the insolvency plan. Peters also committed to funding a large part of the budget for the upcoming season. BFC Dynamo had to start over in the sixth-tier Verbandsliga Berlin in 2002. Nico Tomaschewski returned to the team in 2002.{{cite news |date=5 June 2002 |title=Das gab's noch nie Fans übernehmen die Macht im Klub |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/das-gabs-noch-nie-fans-uebernehmen-die-macht-im-klub |language=de |work=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=5 October 2021}}{{cite news |last=Stöckigt |first=Maxim |date=2 September 2002 |title=Der große Traum von Mba |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2002_09_02.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=5 October 2021}} The team was also joined by young defender Robert Rudwaleit from the reserve team the same year. Robert Rudwaleit was the son of Bodo Rudwaleit.{{cite news |last=Bläsig |first=Horst |date=20 July 2002 |title=Neuanfang in Liga fünf |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2002_07_20.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=5 October 2021}} BFC Dynamo finished its first season in the Verbandsliga Berlin in third place. Jörn Lenz then returned to the team in 2003.{{cite news |last=Bläsig |first=Horst |date=8 August 2003 |title=Weinrote Oberliga-träume |url=https://www.morgenpost.de/printarchiv/sport/article102390238/Weinrote-Oberliga-Traeume.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=15 January 2023}} BFC Dynamo also recruited forward Danny Kukulies from [[SC Pfullendorf]] the same year.{{cite news |last=Karkossa |first=Bernd |date=3 May 2004 |title=Fußballverrückter |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2004_05_03.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=11 October 2021}} The insolvency situation was complex. The club had 170 creditors and it was uncertain whether the insolvency proceedings would end successfully. BFC Dynamo eventually finished the 2003-04 Verbandsliga Berlin in first place and won promotion back to the [[NOFV-Oberliga Nord]]. The team won all 17 matches in the second half of the league season, which set new record in the Verbandsliga Berlin.{{cite news |date=23 May 2004 |title=Rekord: Dynamo gewinnt alle 17 Rückrundenspiele |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2004_05_23.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=14 October 2021}} Kukulies became the top scorer in the league with 32 goals.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=17 June 2004 |title=Neuer Trainer Backs will "möglichst weit nach oben" |url=https://www.kicker.de/neuer-trainer-backs-will-moeglichst-weit-nach-oben-300487/artikel |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=14 October 2021}} Finally, the insolvency proceedings also came to a positive conclusion after a meeting with the creditors at the [[Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf|Charlottenburg]] [[Amtsgericht|District Court]] on 8 June 2004.{{cite news |date=19 April 2004 |title=Sachstand zu Insolvenz |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2004_04_19_3.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=14 October 2021}} ===Consolidation (2004-2014)=== {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo (2004–present)}} Former player [[Christian Backs]] became the new coach in 2004. Key players on the team in the 2004–05 season were Robert Rudwaleit, Nico Thomaschewski, [[Dennis Kutrieb]], Jörn Lenz and Danny Kukulies.{{cite news |last=Rößner |first=Jörn |date=7 August 2004 |title=Sieben Berliner Klubs und ein Top-Favorit |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2004_08_07.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=15 December 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=587}} Coach Backs left for [[Berliner AK 07]] in April 2005, due to financial disagreements with the club.{{cite news |author= |date=21 April 2005 |title=Backs ab Sommer beim BAK |url=https://www.kicker.de/backs-ab-sommer-beim-bak-313962/artikel |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=16 December 2021}} Former [[1. FC Frankfurt|FC Vorwärts Berlin]] player [[Jürgen Piepenburg]] became the new coach in the summer of 2005.{{cite news |last=Gusko |first=Sven |date=9 May 2005 |title=Jürgen Piepenburg neuer BFC-Coach |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2005_05_09.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=16 December 2021}} The [[2005-06 NOFV-Oberliga|2005-06 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]] involved new derbies with 1. FC Union Berlin. BFC Dynamo lost the first meeting with 1. FC Union Berlin 8–0.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=595}} Coach Piepenberg was dismissed immediately after the match.{{cite news |last=Doneck |first=Karsten |date=23 August 2005 |title=BFC Dynamo entlässt den Trainer |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berlinsport/bfc-dynamo-entlaesst-den-trainer/635630.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=7 January 2022}} [[Israel]]i fashion brand JetLag became the new main sponsor at the beginning of 2006.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=8 January 2006 |title=Der BFC Dynamo zähmt sich selbst |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/der-bfc-dynamo-zaehmt-sich-selbst/673504.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=1 May 2024}} The return match against 1. FC Union Berlin was played at the Stadion im Sportforum in May 2006. The score was 1-1 when supporters of BFC Dynamo invaded the pitch to storm the away block.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=14 May 2006 |title=Fussballspiel wegen Randale abgebrochen |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/berlin/fussballspiel-wegen-randale-abgebrochen/710882.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=18 December 2021}} The match was abandoned and 1. FC Union Berlin was awarded a 2–0 victory. [[File:Clp 20060514 Sportforum Dynamostadion.jpg|thumb|right|The Stadion im Sportforum in May 2006.]] The riots in the match against 1. FC Union Berlin in May 2006 threw the club into a new financial crisis.{{cite magazine |last=Blaschke |first=Ronny |year=2006 |title=Nicht gesellschaftsfähig |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2006_11_00.html |magazine=[[11 Freunde]] |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |volume=2006 |issue=11 |access-date=24 July 2021}}{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=10 September 2006 |title=Auf verlorenem Posten: Der Oberligist BFC Dynamo befindet sich in einer schweren Krise und droht, weiter ins Abseits zu driften |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2006_09_09.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=18 December 2021}} The club's finances were eventually saved by the sponsor Infinity-Net Telekom GmbH. The company's owner Peter Meyer became the new strong man in the club. A power struggle developed between main sponsor Meyer and President Weinkauf.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=8 June 2007 |title=Machtkampf beim DDR-Rekordmeister |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2007_06_08.html |language=De |work=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=21 January 2023}} Weinkauf was eventually dismissed in a vote of no confidence at the annual meeting in June 2007.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=23 June 2007 |title=Acht Stunden – dann war der Präsident weg |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berlinsport/fussball-acht-stunden-dann-war-der-praesident-weg/966666.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=27 December 2021}} Meyer became the new chairman of the Economic Council and practically club manager. The [[Turkey|Turkish]] company Gökis Getränkegroßhandel became the new shirt sponsor for the 2007–08 season.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=26 July 2007 |title=BFC-PRÄSIDENT & Sponsor machen "rüber"… |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/bfc-praesident-sponsor-machen-rueber |language=de |work=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=27 December 2021}}{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=30 July 2007 |title="Wer pöbelt, der fliegt" |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/wer-poebelt-der-fliegt/1000058.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=28 December 2021}} The company's manager Gökhan Kazan also became member of the Economic Council. BFC Dynamo played a friendly match against Hertha BSC in July 2007. The match was played under the motto "Against violence and [[racism]]".{{cite news |last=Olaf |first=Sundermeyer |date=30 June 2007 |title=Mit Hooligans und Nazis gegen Rassismus |url=https://www.faz.net/aktuell/sport/fussball/berliner-fussballclub-dynamo-mit-hooligans-und-nazis-gegen-rassismus-1434107.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung]] |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150711194810/https://www.faz.net/aktuell/sport/fussball/berliner-fussballclub-dynamo-mit-hooligans-und-nazis-gegen-rassismus-1434107.html |archive-date=11 July 2015 |access-date=27 December 2021}}{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=31 July 2007 |title=BFC und Hertha setzen Signal: Berliner Testspiel gegen Gewalt und Rassismus |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2007_07_31.html |language=de |work=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=27 December 2021}} Before the match, Meyer publicly declared that "anyone who shouts [[Nazism|nazi]] slogans will be thrown out of the stadium".{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=30 July 2007 |title="Wer pöbelt, der fliegt" |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/wer-poebelt-der-fliegt/1000058.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=28 December 2021 |quote="Das betrifft Rechte und Hooligans? Das betrifft alle, die bei Fußballspielen stören. Wer Naziparolen brüllt, fliegt aus dem Stadion."}} BFC Dynamo recruited midfielder Christian Preiß in 2008.{{cite news |author= |date=n.d. |title=Christian Preiß - Leufbahn |url=https://www.kicker.de/christian-preiss/laufbahn |language=German |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=1 May 2024}} Norbert Uhlig was elected as the new club president in October 2008.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=13 October 2008 |title=Norbert Uhlig neuer Präsident |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2008_10_13.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=28 December 2021}} BFC Dynamo was undefeated in the first ten matches of the [[2008-09 NOFV-Oberliga|2008-09 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=617–621}} However, all hopes of promotion were dashed after a 2–4 loss against first-placed [[Tennis Borussia Berlin]] before the winter break.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=162}} BFC Dynamo finished the 2008-09 NOFV-Oberliga Nord as runner-up.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=617–621}} Christian Backs returned as coach in 2009.{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Matthias |date=25 March 2009 |title=Backs wird BFC-Trainer - Auftrag: Aufstieg in die Regionalliga |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2009_03_25.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 January 2022}} BFC Dynamo also recruited forward [[Nico Patschinski]] from 1. FC Union Berlin the same year.{{cite web |url=https://www.turus.net/sport/fussball/7792-der-bfc-dynamo-ist-wieder-da-r%C3%BCckblick-auf-die-vergangenen-20-jahre.html |title=Der BFC Dynamo ist wieder da: Rückblick auf die vergangenen 20 Jahre |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=15 April 2014 |work=turus.net |location=Essen |publisher=Karsten Höft |language=de |access-date=27 December 2021}} The team had a successful start to the [[2009-10 NOFV-Oberliga|2009-10 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]]. BFC Dynamo lost only one match in the first 14 matchdays, but failed to keep pace with first-placed [[FC Energie Cottbus|FC Energie Cottbus II]] in the second half of the league season.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=164}} Coach Backs was dismissed before Easter 2010 and former player [[Heiko Bonan]] took over as coach.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=12 April 2010 |title=Frischer Stoff für den Stammtisch |url=https://taz.de/Frischer-Stoff-fuer-den-Stammtisch/!452445/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=8 January 2022}} BFC Dynamo also finished the 2009-10 NOFV-Oberliga Nord as runner-up. The team reached the final of the 2009-10 Berlin Cup. BFC Dynamo lost the final 2–1 against Berliner AK 07. 100-150 supporters of BFC Dynamo stormed the pitch after the final whistle.{{cite news |date=2 June 2010 |title=BFC-Randale nach Sieg von Ankaraspor |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/bfc-randale-nach-sieg-von-ankaraspor |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=8 January 2022}}{{cite news |last=Stier |first=Sebastian |date=6 August 2011 |title=Wenn Hooligans weinen |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/bfc-dynamo-wenn-hooligans-weinen/4472276.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=8 January 2022}} Forward Matthias Steinborn from the youth department became a regular player in the team in 2010. The results in the [[2010-11 NOFV-Oberliga|2010-11 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]] were mediocre, but the team had more success in the Berlin Cup. BFC Dynamo defeated [[SFC Stern 1900]] 2–0 in the final of the 2010–11 Berlin Cup.{{cite news |last=Gustke |first=Axel |date=9 June 2011 |title=BFC Dynamo schlägt Stern 1900 im Finale |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berliner-landespokal-bfc-dynamo-schlaegt-stern-1900-im-finale/4267722.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=9 January 2022}} BFC Dynamo had thus won its first Berlin Cup title in 12 years and was qualified for the [[2011–12 DFB-Pokal]]; the victory in the cup was also worth 100,000 Euros in bonuses from the [[German Football Association]] (DFB).{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=587}} BFC Dynamo was drawn against [[1. FC Kaiserslautern]] in the first round of the 2011–12 DFB-Pokal. The match was played in front of 10,104 spectators at the [[Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark]]. BFC Dynamo lost the match 0–3.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=639}} Serious riots broke out among supporters of BFC Dynamo after the match. The club was subsequently fined 12,000 Euros by the DFB Sports Court.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=166}} BFC Dynamo saw a decline in the league and finished the [[2011-12 NOFV-Oberliga|2011-12 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]] in 13th place. The highly popular Turkish-born Volkan Uluc returned as coach in 2012. Uluc had previously coached BFC Dynamo from 2007 to 2009.{{cite news |last=Wllmann |first=Frank |date=8 August 2012 |title=Willmanns Kolumne: Der BFC auf dem Weg in die Unsterblichkeit? |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/der-bfc-auf-dem-weg-in-die-unsterblichkeit-6682945.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=13 September 2023}} BFC Dynamo conceded just one loss in the first 14 matchdays in the [[2012-13 NOFV-Oberliga|2012-13 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]] and was a top team in the league.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=645-650}} The team finished the season in third place in the league. BFC Dynamo then defeated [[SV Lichtenberg 47]] 1–0 in the final of the 2012–13 Berlin Cup in front of 6,381 spectators at the Friedrich-Ludig-Jahn-Sportpark.{{cite news |last=Gutske |first=Axel |date=12 June 2013 |title=BFC Dynamo schlägt Lichtenberg 1:0 |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berliner-pokal-finale-bfc-dynamo-schlaegt-lichtenberg-1-0/8342096.html |language=German |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=10 January 2022}} The attendance set a new record for a Berlin Cup final since [[German reunification]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=168}} BFC Dynamo recruited [[Senegal]]ese striker Djibril N'Diaye in 2013.{{cite news |last=Vossen |first=Lorenz |date=13 September 2013 |title=Die vielen Reisen des jungen BFC-Stürmers Dieter |url=https://www.morgenpost.de/sport/berlin-sport/article120030764/Die-vielen-Reisen-des-jungen-BFC-Stuermers-Dieter.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=10 January 2022}} N'Diaye quickly became a crowd favourite, known as "Dieter".{{cite news |last=Hettfleisch |first=Wolfgang |date=31 July 2013 |title=BFC Dynamo: Pflanze kontra Stiefel |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/sport-leidenschaft/bfc-dynamo-pflanze-kontra-stiefel-li.50387 |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201011212944/https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/sport-leidenschaft/bfc-dynamo-pflanze-kontra-stiefel-li.50387 |archive-date=11 October 2020 |access-date=2 October 2022}}{{cite news |last=Eisen |first=Martin |date=4 August 2015 |title=N'Diaye: Premiere und Wiederholung |url=https://www.kicker.de/ndiaye_premiere-und-wiederholung-632272/artikel |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=2 October 2022}} BFC Dynamo came to dominate the [[2013-14 NOFV-Oberliga|2013-14 NOFV-Oberliga Nord]]. The league title was secured on the 22nd matchday and the team would go through the entire league season undefeated. BFC Dynamo eventually finished the 2013-14 NOFV-Oberliga Nord 34 points ahead of second-placed [[Brandenburger SC Süd 05]].{{cite news |author= |date=12 April 2014 |title=BFC Dynamo steigt in Regionalliga auf |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/sport/bfc-dynamo-steigt-in-regionalliga-auf |language=de |work=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=9 January 2022}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=652–657}} Christian Preiß had scored 15 goals and N'Diaye 12 goals in the league.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=652-657}} BFC Dynamo had thus finally won promotion to the [[Regionalliga Nordost]] after 10 years in the NOFV-Oberliga Nord. ===Regionalliga Nordost (2014-present)=== {{Main|History of Berliner FC Dynamo (2004–present)}} [[File:2018-08-19 BFC Dynamo vs. 1. FC Köln (DFB-Pokal) by Sandro Halank–351.jpg|thumb|right|The mascot of BFC Dynamo "Teddy" in 2018.]] The 2014–15 season saw the return of BFC Dynamo to live television.{{cite web |url=https://www.fc-carlzeiss-jena.de/news/club-profis/details/hinweise_fuer_die_besucher_des_spiels_fcc_bfc_dynamo.html |title=HINWEISE FÜR DIE BESUCHER DES SPIELS FCC - BFC DYNAMO |author= |date=31 July 2014 |website=fc-carlzeiss-jena.de |location=Jena |publisher=[[FC Carl Zeiss Jena|FC Carl Zeiss Jena Fußball Spielbetriebs GmbH]] |language=de |access-date=8 June 2023}} [[Thomas Stratos]] became new coach in November 2014. BFC Dynamo defeated [[SV Tasmania Berlin]] 2–1 in the 2014–15 Berlin Cup final in front of 6,914 spectators at [[Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark]] and won their fourth Berlin Cup title.{{cite news |last=Bröhl |first=Stefan |date=20 May 2015 |title=Der BFC Dynamo gewinnt 1:0 gegen Tasmania |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berliner-pokalfinale-der-bfc-dynamo-gewinnt-1-0-gegen-tasmania/11805284.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=15 January 2022}} The attendance set a new record for a Berlin Cup final since German reunification.{{cite news |last1=Miller |last2=Bunkus |first1=René |first2=Mathias |date=20 May 2015 |title=Berliner Pokal: BFC im Pokalglück |url=http://www.berliner-kurier.de/bfc-dynamo/berliner-pokal-bfc-im-pokalglueck,27904404,30748822.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150717102419/http://www.berliner-kurier.de/bfc-dynamo/berliner-pokal-bfc-im-pokalglueck,27904404,30748822.html |archive-date=17 July 2015 |access-date=15 January 2022}} BFC Dynamo recruited a number of players with [[3. Liga]] experience in 2015, such as [[Brazil]]ian midfielder [[Thiago Rockenbach]], forward [[Dennis Srbeny]] and goalkeeper [[Bernhard Hendl]]. The team was also joined by midfielder [[Kai Pröger]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=172}} The club celebrated its 50th anniversary on 15 January 2016. The anniversary was celebrated with around 1,000 guests in the Loewe Saal in the locality of [[Moabit]].{{cite news |last=Lange |first=Jörn |date=14 January 2016 |title=Auf die Party des BFC Dynamo hat nicht jeder Lust |url=https://www.morgenpost.de/sport/berlin-sport/article206919539/Auf-die-Party-des-BFC-Dynamo-hat-nicht-jeder-Lust.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=15 January 2022}}{{cite news |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=21 January 2016 |title=Mit der Narrenkappe beim Jubiläum |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/50-jahre-1-fc-union-und-bfc-dynamo-mit-der-narrenkappe-beim-jubilaeum/12864472.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=15 January 2022}} Among the guests were former players and coaches such as [[Peter Rohde (footballer)|Peter Rohde]], [[Frank Terletzki]], [[Wolf-Rüdiger Netz]], [[Jürgen Bogs]], [[Artur Ullrich]], [[Bernd Schulz]], [[Frank Rohde]], [[Andreas Thom]] and [[Thomas Doll]].{{cite news |last=Gartenschläger |first=Lars |date=15 January 2016 |title=Der tiefe Fall des verhassten Stasi-Klubs |url=https://www.welt.de/sport/fussball/article151020667/Der-tiefe-Fall-des-verhassten-Stasi-Klubs.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Welt]] |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=15 January 2022}} Midfielder Joey Breitfeld from the youth department made his debut for BFC Dynamo in the [[Regionalliga Nordost]] in February 2016.{{cite web |url=https://bfc.com/news.php?id=84&cat_id=&p=59&search= |title=BFC-Talent verlängert bis 2018! |author= |date=24 February 2016 |website=bfc.com |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Fussballclub Dynamo e.V. |language=de |access-date=10 June 2023}} BFC Dynamo finished the [[2015-16 Regionalliga|2015-16 Regionalliga Nordost]] in fourth place. [[René Rydlewicz]] became the new coach in 2016. BFC Dynamo reached the final of the 2016-17 Berlin Cup. The team defeated FC Viktoria 1889 Berlin 3–1 in the final, after two goals by Pröger in extra time.{{cite news |last=Graeber |first=Julian |date=25 May 2017 |title=BFC Dynamo gewinnt den Berliner Pokal |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/3-1-gegen-viktoria-89-bfc-dynamo-gewinnt-den-berliner-pokal/19854158.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=16 January 2022}} BFC Dynamo recruited [[Azerbaijan]]i striker and [[Azerbaijan national football team|national team player]] [[Rufat Dadashov]] as well as midfielder Philip Schulz in 2017.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=175}} The team drew [[FC Schalke 04]] in the first round of the [[2017-18 DFB-Pokal]]. BFC Dynamo lost the match 0–2 in front of 14,114 spectators at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark. The attendance was the highest for BFC Dynamo since the match between BFC Dynamo and [[AS Monaco]] in the [[1989–90 European Cup Winners' Cup]] in November 1989.{{cite news |last=Hermanns |first=Stefan |date=15 August 2017 |title=DFB-Pokal: Die Berliner unterliegen Schalke 0:2 Klassentreffen beim BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/dfb-pokal-die-berliner-unterliegen-schalke-0-2-klassentreffen-beim-bfc-dynamo/20188438.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=16 January 2022}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=175}} BFC Dynamo finished the [[2017-18 Regionalliga|2017-18 Regionalliga Nordost]] in 4th place. Dadashov became the top scorer in the league with 25 goals in 25 games.{{cite web |url=https://www.fussball.de/newsdetail/bfc-dynamo-wer-wird-der-neue-dadashov/-/article-id/188943#!/ |title=BFC Dynamo: Wer wird der neue Dadashov? |author= |date=22 June 2018 |website=fussball.de |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=DFB GmbH |language=de |access-date=16 January 2022}} The team again reached the final of the Berlin Cup. BFC Dynamo defeated Berliner SC 2–1 in the final of the 2017–18 Berlin Cup to claim its second consecutive Berlin Cup title.{{cite news |last=Wiesner |first=Steven |date=21 May 2018 |title=BFC Dynamo schlägt Berliner SC 2:1 |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berliner-landespokal-bfc-dynamo-schlaegt-berliner-sc-2-1/22587154.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=17 January 2022}} Dadashov scored both goals for BFC Dynamo in the final.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=689}} BFC Dynamo recruited defender [[Chris Reher]] in 2018. BFC Dynamo played [[1. FC Köln]] in the first round of the [[2018-19 DFB-Pokal]]. The match was played at the [[Olympiastadion (Berlin)|Olympiastadion]].{{cite news |last=Hermanns |first=Stefan |date=19 August 2018 |title=BFC Dynamo verliert 1:9 gegen den 1. FC Köln |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/dfb-pokal-im-olympiastadion-bfcdynamo-verliert-1-9-gegen-den-1-fckoeln/22929792.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=17 January 2022}} The match was attended by 14,357 spectators, which was a new record for BFC Dynamo since the fall of the [[Berlin Wall]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=177}} [[Christian Benbennek]] became the new coach in 2019.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=179}} The [[2019-20 Regionalliga|2019-20 Regionalliga Nordost]] was suspended due to the outbreak of the [[COVID-19 pandemic in Germany|COVID-19 pandemic]].{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/thema/2020/coronavirus/beitraege/fussball-berlin-brandenburg-nordostdeutscher-verband-nofv-unterbrechung.html |title=Auch Regionalliga Nordost setzt Spielbetrieb aus |author= |date=13 March 2020 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=19 January 2022}} BFC Dynamo arranged a virtual match against the fictional team FC Corona on 18 April 2020 to raise money for the club. The virtual match was played at the no longer existing [[Stadion der Weltjugend]].{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/thema/2020/coronavirus/beitraege_neu/2020/04/fussball-regionalliga-bfc-dynamo-virtuelles-benefizspiel-spenden.html |title=BFC Dynamo sammelt rund 70.000 Euro mit virtuellem Kick |author= |date=18 April 2020 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |access-date=19 January 2022}} The club sold a total of 50,000 tickets for the match.{{cite news |author= |date=15 April 2020 |title=Virtuell Geld verdienen im Fußball |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/klubs-trotzen-der-coronakrise-virtuell-geld-verdienen-im-fussball/25741554.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=20 January 2022}} The team was joined by midfielder [[Alexander Siebeck]] in 2020.{{cite web |url=https://bfc.com/news.php?id=1367&cat_id=0&p=55&search= |title=Neuzugang: BFC Dynamo begrüßt Alexander Siebeck |author= |date=25 June 2020 |website=bfc.com |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Fussballclub Dynamo e.V. |language=de |access-date=17 June 2023}} Also the [[2020-21 Regionalliga|2020-21 Regionalliga Nordost]] was suspended due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/thema/2020/coronavirus/beitraege_neu/2020/06/fussball-regionalliga-nofv-saisonabbruch-aufstieg-energie-cottbus-altglienicke.html |title=NOFV beschließt Regionalliga-Abbruch |author= |date=5 June 2020 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |access-date=20 January 2022}}{{cite news |author= |date=5 June 2020 |title=NOFV ernennt Lok Leipzig zum Regionalliga-Meister |url=https://www.kicker.de/nofv_ernennt_lok_leipzig_zum_regionalliga_meister-776932/artikel |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=19 January 2022}} BFC Dynamo defeated [[Berliner AK 07]] 2–1 in the final of the 2019-20 Berlin Cup to claim its seventh Berlin Cup title.{{cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/05/berlin-fussball-landespokal-finale-finaltag-amateure-bfc-dynamo-berliner-ak.html |title=BFC Dynamo gewinnt Berliner Landespokal |date=29 May 2021 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |access-date=29 May 2021 |archive-date=29 May 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210529135100/https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/05/berlin-fussball-landespokal-finale-finaltag-amateure-bfc-dynamo-berliner-ak.html}} [[File:2018-08-19 BFC Dynamo vs. 1. FC Köln (DFB-Pokal) by Sandro Halank–101.jpg|thumb|400px|right|Chris Reher with the ball during the match between BFC Dynamo and 1. FC Köln in the [[2018-19 DFB-Pokal]].]] BFC Dynamo recruited experienced forward [[Christian Beck (footballer)|Christian Beck]] in 2021.{{cite web |url=https://www.mdr.de/sport/fussball_rl/christian-beck-wechsel-fc-magdeburg-bfc-dynamo-berlin-100.html |title=FCM-Legende Beck heuert beim BFC Dynamo an |date=15 June 2021 |website=mdr.de |location=Leipzig |publisher=[[Mitteldeutscher Rundfunk]] |language=German |access-date=11 July 2021}} Key players on the team in the 2021–22 season were Christian Beck, [[Dmitri Stajila]], Chris Reher, Alexander Siebeck, [[Michael Blum (footballer)|Michael Blum]], Andreas Pollasch, Joey Breitfeld, [[Darryl Geurts]], Andor Bolyki, [[Niklas Brandt]] and Philip Schulz. BFC Dynamo drew [[VfB Stuttgart]] in the first round of the [[2021-22 DFB-Pokal]]. The match was played at the Stadion im Sportforum. It was the first DFB-Pokal match at the Stadion im Sportforum since FC Berlin played [[SC Freiburg]] at the stadium in the [[1991–92 DFB-Pokal]].{{cite web |url=https://www.turus.net/sport/fussball/10933-bfc-dynamo-nach-30-jahren-wieder-dfb-pokal-im-sportforum-hohenschoenhausen.html |title=BFC Dynamo: Nach 30 Jahren wieder DFB-Pokal im Sportforum Hohenschönhausen|last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=5 August 2021 |website=turus.net |location=[[Essen]] |publisher=Karsten Höft |language=de |access-date=20 August 2021}} BFC Dynamo lost the match 0–6. BFC Dynamo had great success in the [[2021-22 Regionalliga|2021-22 Regionalliga Nordost]] and became ''[[Herbstmeister]]''.{{cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/11/fussball-regionalliga-nordost-berlin-bak-bfc-dynamo-spielbericht.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211201015659/https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2021/11/fussball-regionalliga-nordost-berlin-bak-bfc-dynamo-spielbericht.html |archive-date=1 December 2021 |title=BFC Dynamo krönt sich zum Herbstmeister |author= |date=30 November 2021 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=23 January 2022 }} The club saw the biggest increase in membership of any club in Berlin in 2021, apart from [[Hertha BSC]] and [[1. FC Union Berlin]]; membership increased by 51 percent in 2021.{{refn|group=nb|The number of club members was 2,148 by 30 June 2022.{{cite news |last=Sculze |first=Lukas |date=18 January 2023 |title=REPORTAGE: DIESE 15 OSTDEUTSCHEN FUSSBALLVEREINE HABEN DIE MEISTEN MITGLIEDER! |url=https://www.tag24.de/sport/fussball/reportage-diese-ostdeutschen-fussballvereine-haben-die-meisten-mitglieder-2708208 |language=German |newspaper=Tag24 ([[:de:Tag24|de]]) |location=[[Dresden]] |publisher=TAG24 NEWS Deutschland GmbH |access-date=8 October 2023 }} That compares with membership numbers as low as 150 to 200 in the early 1990s.}} BFC Dynamo finished the 2021–22 Regionalliga Nordost in first place and had finally claimed its first ever [[Regionalliga]] title.{{cite news |author= |date=15 May 2022 |title=Nach 4:2-Sieg gegen die VSG Altglienicke: Der BFC Dynamo ist Meister der Regionalliga Nordost - |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/der-bfc-dynamo-ist-meister-der-regionalliga-nordost-5144809.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=24 September 2022}} Christian Beck became the top scorer in the league with 23 goals.{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Mathias |date=20 June 2022 |title=Signalwirkung: Torjäger Christian Beck verlängert beim BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/sport-leidenschaft/signalwirkung-torjaeger-christian-beck-verlaengert-beim-bfc-dynamo-li.238428 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=24 September 2022}} BFC Dynamo faced [[VfB Oldenburg]] from the [[Regionalliga Nord]] in the [[2021–22 Regionalliga#Promotion play-offs|play-off for the 3. Liga]]. BFC Dynamo eventually lost the play-off on goal difference, having lost the first leg at home and won the second leg away.{{cite news |author= |date=28 May 2022 |title=BFC Dynamo steht nach 0:2 mit dem Rücken zur Wand: Doppelpacker Zietarski bringt Oldenburg der 3. Liga ganz nah |url=https://www.kicker.de/bfc-dynamo-gegen-oldenburg-2022-liga-relegation-4769833/spielbericht |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=23 September 2022}}{{cite news |author= |date=4 June 2022 |title=Zurück im Profifußball: VfB Oldenburg steigt in die 3. Liga auf |url=https://www.kicker.de/oldenburg-gegen-bfc-dynamo-2022-liga-relegation-4769834/spielbericht |language=de |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=24 September 2022}}{{cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2022/06/fussball-berlin-bfc-dynamo-vfb-oldenburg-aufstieg-dritte-liga-regionalliga-nordost.html |title=BFC Dynamo verpasst Aufstieg in die 3. Liga knapp |author= |date=4 June 2022 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |access-date=7 June 2022 |archive-date=6 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220606193804/https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2022/06/fussball-berlin-bfc-dynamo-vfb-oldenburg-aufstieg-dritte-liga-regionalliga-nordost.html }} [[Heiner Backhaus]] became new coach for the 2022-23 season.{{cite news |author= |date=9 June 2022 |title=Überraschung! Ex-Union-Profi Heiner Backhaus wird neuer Trainer beim BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/ueberraschung-ex-union-profi-heiner-backhaus-wird-neuer-trainer-beim-bfc-dynamo-li.234496 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=10 November 2024 }} BFC Dynamo had a diffucult first half of the season.{{cite news |last=Fritzsche |first=Matthias |date=18 December 2022 |title=Ganz im Sinn von Heiner Backhaus: BFC-Party mit Demut |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/ganz-in-heiners-sinn-bfc-party-mit-demut-li.299095 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=9 November 2024 }} One of the few highlights was a 4-1 win at home over [[FC Energie Cottbus]] on 13 November 2022.{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2022/11/fussball-regionalliga-nordost-bfc-dynamo-energie-cottbus-tebe-altglienicke-babelsberg-chemnitz.html |title=Cottbus verpasst Tabellenführung beim BFC Dynamo klar |last=Jacobs |first=Flynn |date=13 November 2022 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=10 March 2024 }}{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Mathias |date=13 November 2022 |title=BFC Dynamo begeistert gegen Cottbus mit dem Energie-Einsparprogramm und Wollitz bekommt einiges zu hören und zu lesen! |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/bfc-dynamo-begeistert-gegen-cottbus-mit-dem-energie-sparprogramm-und-wollitz-bekommt-einiges-zu-hoeren-und-zu-lesen-li.286588 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=10 March 2024 }} BFC Dynamo climbed the table after the winter break. The team eventually finished the [[2022-23 Regionalliga|2022-23 Regionalliga Nordost]] in sixth place. Christian Beck ended his career at BFC Dynamo after the season. He had been the team's top goalscorer, as well as a top goalscorer in the [[Regionalliga Nordost]], for two consecutive seasons.{{cite news |author= |date=n.d. |title=Regionalliga Nordost - Torjäger 2021/22 - 38. Spieltag |url=https://www.kicker.de/regionalliga-nordost/torjaeger/2021-22 |language=German |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 November 2024 }}{{cite news |author= |date=n.d. |title=Regionalliga Nordost - Torjäger 2022/23 - 34. Spieltag |url=https://www.kicker.de/regionalliga-nordost/torjaeger/2022-23 |language=German |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 November 2024 }} BFC Dynamo recruited numerous new players for the 2023-24 season. One of them was striker Rufat Dadashov, who returned to the club.{{cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2023/09/fussball-berlin-bfc-dynamo-regionalliga-nordost-interview-stuermer-rufat-dadashov.html |title="Für uns als Mannschaft kam die Trainer-Entlassung so plötzlich wie für alle anderen" |last=Bürgener |first=Jonas |date=11 September 2023 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=23 October 2024 }} Chris Reher became the new team captain 2023-24 season.{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Mathias|date=24 July 2024 |title=Chris Reher wird neuer Kapitän des BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/chris-reher-wird-neuer-kapitaen-der-dynamos-li.372416 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=19 October 2024 }} The team got a relatively good start to the league season with ten points in the first five matches.{{cite news |last=Schulze |first=Lukas |date=9 August 2024 |title=EX-DYNAMO BACKHAUS REUMÜTIG: "ICH KÖNNTE ZU JEDEM KLUB ZURÜCKGEHEN - AUSSER ZUM BFC |url=https://www.tag24.de/sport/fussball/regionalliga/ex-dynamo-backhaus-reumuetig-ich-koennte-zu-jedem-klub-zurueckgehen-ausser-zum-bfc-3307023 |language=German |newspaper=Tag24 ([[:de:Tag24|de]]) |location=[[Dresden]] |publisher=TAG24 NEWS Deutschland GmbH |access-date=20 October 2023 }} On 2 September 2024, however, the club went out and announced that coach Backhaus had been released from his duties with immediate effect "due to behavior that is detrimental to the club".{{cite news |last=Wiezorek |first=Peter |date=2 September 2024 |title=Beben beim BFC: Dynamo stellt Trainer Heiner Backhaus frei |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/beben-beim-bfc-dynamo-stellt-trainer-heiner-backhaus-frei-li.385070 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=20 October 2024 }} Backhaus had declared interest in becoming the new coach of [[TSV Alemannia Aachen]].{{cite news |last=Bühler |first=Karin |date=14 September 2023 |title=BFC-Sportdirektor Angelo Vier über Backhaus: "Uns hat das Thema brutal erwischt" |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/sport-leidenschaft/fussball-regionalliga-interview-bfc-dynamo-sportdirektor-angelo-vier-ueber-ex-trainer-heiner-backhaus-uns-hat-das-thema-brutal-erwischt-li.388972 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231003191824/https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/sport-leidenschaft/fussball-regionalliga-interview-bfc-dynamo-sportdirektor-angelo-vier-ueber-ex-trainer-heiner-backhaus-uns-hat-das-thema-brutal-erwischt-li.388972 |archive-date=3 October 2023 |access-date=20 October 2024 |url-status=live }} Berlin-native Dirk Kunert took over as the new head coach after Backhaus.{{cite news |last=Wiezorek |first=Peter |date=12 September 2023 |title=Amtlich! Dirk Kunert (55) ist neuer Cheftrainer des BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/amtlich-dirk-kunert-55-ist-neuer-cheftrainer-des-bfc-dynamo-li.388132 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=20 October 2024 }} BFC Dynamo was in second place in the league before the winter break, after a very successful autumn. After defeating Berliner AK 07 2–0 in the replay of the match from the 17th matchday on 27 February 2024, the team could retroactively title themselves ''[[Herbstmeister]]'' in the [[2023–24 Regionalliga|2023–24 Regionalliga Nordost]].{{cite news |author= |date=27 February 2024 |title=BAK glücklos, BFC effizient: Dynamo in Schlagdistanz zu Greifswald |url=https://www.kicker.de/berliner-ak-gegen-bfc-dynamo-2024-regionalliga-nordost-4864796/spielbericht |language=German |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=26 October 2024 }} BFC Dynamo defeated [[1. FC Lokomotive Leipzig]] 4-0 at home on 9 March 2024.{{cite news |author= |date=9 March 2024 |title=Nach Blitz-Gelb-Rot: BFC Dynamo schlägt Lok Leipzig deutlic |url=https://www.kicker.de/bfc-dynamo-gegen-lok-leipzig-2024-regionalliga-nordost-4864869/spielbericht |language=German |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=27 October 2024 }} It was the team's biggest win against 1. FC Lokomotive Leipzig since the [[1983-84 DDR-Oberliga]].{{cite news |author= |date=n.d. |title=BFC Dynamo gegen 1. FC Lok Leipzig - Alle spiele |url=https://www.kicker.de/bfc-dynamo-gegen-1-fc-lok-leipzig |language=German |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=27 October 2024 }} Midfielder Joey Breitfeld made his 200th competitive appearance for BFC Dynamo in the league match against [[VSG Altglienicke]] on 23 March 2024.{{cite news |last=Wiezorek |first=Peter |date=24 March 2024 |title=Breites Grinsen nach Breitfeld-Gala: BFC Dynamo lässt Wände wackeln |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/breites-grinsen-nach-breitfeld-gala-bfc-dynamo-laesst-waende-wackeln-li.2199704 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=28 October 2024 }} BFC Dynamo reported on 10 April 2024 that the club had achieved a new attendance record since ''[[Peaceful Revolution|Die Wende]]'' in the [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen]] during the 2023–24 season: the club had thus achieved its highest attendance figures since 1990. BFC Dynamo lost several important points in the second half of the 2023-24 Regionalliga Nordost and missed out on several chances to move to the top of the table.{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2024/03/berlin-brandenburg-sport-fussbal-regionalliga-nordost-babelsberg-bfc-dynamo-hertha-altglienicke.html |title=BFC Dynamo patzt in Babelsberg und verpasst Sprung an Tabellenspitze |last=Richter |first=Patrick |date=2 March 2024 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=27 October 2024 }}{{cite news |author= |date=13 April 2024 |title=Verdiente Pleite: BFC Dynamo unterliegt bei Viktoria Berlin nach Führung |url=https://www.kicker.de/vikt-berlin-gegen-bfc-dynamo-2024-regionalliga-nordost-4864904/spielbericht |language=German |work=[[Kicker (sports magazine)|Kicker]] |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=2 November 2024 }} The team eventually finished the 2023-24 Regionalliga Nordost in fourth place. BFC Dynamo played a friendly match against [[AS Monaco]] at the Stadion im Sportforum on 25 May 2024. AS Monaco was represented by a selection of young players, several of whom already had [[Ligue 1]] experience.{{cite news |last=Wiezorek |first=Peter |date=24 May 2024 |title=Sondertrikot, Wimpel, alte Helden: Der BFC Dynamo fordert AS Monaco |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/sondertrikot-wimpel-alte-helden-der-bfc-dynamo-fordert-as-monaco-li.2218290 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 November 2024 }} The match was played in memory of the encounter between the two clubs 35 years ago in the [[1989-90 European Cup Winners' Cup]].{{cite news |last=Wiezorek |first=Peter |date=5 March 2024 |title=Ein Hauch von Europacup: Der BFC Dynamo spielt gegen AS Monaco |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/ein-hauch-von-europacup-der-bfc-dynamo-spielt-gegen-as-monaco-li.2193748 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=27 October 2024 }} BFC Dynamo won the match 4-2.{{cite news |last=Wiezorek |first=Peter |date=26 May 2024 |title=4:2 gegen AS Monaco: BFC Dynamo beeindruckt seinen neuen Trainer |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/42-gegen-as-monaco-bfc-dynamo-beeindruckt-seinen-neuen-trainer-li.2218650 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 November 2024 }} ==Colours and crest== The colours of BFC Dynamo are [[Wine (color)#Claret|claret]] and white.{{cite web |url=https://www.dfb.de/datencenter/vereine/bfc-dynamo-e-v |title=BFC Dynamo e. V. |website=dfb.de |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=Deutscher Fußball-Bund e.V. |language=de |access-date=1 November 2020}} The colours were inherited from [[SC Dynamo Berlin]] and followed the claret colour scheme of [[SV Dynamo]]. BFC Dynamo has been playing in claret and white since the club's founding, with the exception of a period in the 1990s. The BFC Dynamo home kit has traditionally been a claret shirt, paired with claret or white shorts and socks. The team is occasionally nicknamed "die Weinroten", which means "the Clarets".{{cite news |last=Baingo |first=Andreas |date=20 July 2020 |title=BFC Dynamo: Raus aus der Schmuddelecke |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/sport/bfc-dynamo-raus-aus-der-schmuddelecke-li.93885 |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=11 October 2020}}{{cite news |last=Lachmann |first=Michael |date=7 August 2015 |title=0:2! BFC Dynamo scheitert auch im vierten Versuch |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/berlin-sport/mehr-berlin-sport/02-bfc-dynamo-scheitert-auch-im-vierten-versuch |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} The club was rebranded as FC Berlin on 19 February 1990. A pure white was set as the new match colour with immediate effect.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=137}} FC Berlin then adopted a red and white colour scheme.{{cite news |last=Willmamn |first=Frank |date=27 May 2022 |title=BFC Dynamo: Der Resthauch des Bösen |url=https://www.zeit.de/sport/2022-05/bfc-dynamo-regionalliga-aufstieg |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Zeit]] |location=Hamburg |publisher=Zeit Online GmbH |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20230405220540/https://www.zeit.de/sport/2022-05/bfc-dynamo-regionalliga-aufstieg |archive-date=5 April 2023 |access-date=12 June 2022}} In the eyes of the supporters, the red and white kit looked a lot like 1. FC Union Berlin. The club played in red and white home kits for most of the FC Berlin era, but wore a black and red striped home shirt, paired with black shorts and socks from the [[1996–97 Regionalliga|1996–97 season]] through the [[1998–99 Regionalliga|1998–99 season]]. The club eventually decided on 3 May 1999 to return to its original club name and consequently also later returned to its traditional colour scheme.{{cite news |last=Quednau |first=Frank |date=5 May 1999 |title=Zurück in die Zukunft |url=https://www.welt.de/print-welt/article571072/Zurueck-in-die-Zukunft.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} The BFC Dynamo away kit has traditionally been a white shirt, paired with claret or white shorts and socks. However, a variety of away kits have been used at different times. The team occasionally used a green alternative shirt during the 1960s and early 1970s.{{cite magazine |author= |date=6 August 1968 |title=Club, BSG, SG, Abschneiden, Spieleraufgebot, Zugänge, Abgänge, Trainger, Bisherige Erfolfe, Leistungskollektiv: Berliner FC Dynamo |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201968/FUWO%201968%20%2033.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1968 |issue=33 |pages=8–9 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=4 February 2023}}{{cite magazine |author= |date=August 1975 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201975%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201975-76.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1975 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=6 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=11 April 2022}}{{refn|group=nb|The green alternative shirt was used on several occasions as an alternative home shirt, such as in SC Dynamo Berlin's home matches against [[Chemnitzer FC|SC Motor Karl-Marx-Stadt]] on 2 September 1962 and [[SV 08 Steinach|BSG Motor Steinach]] on 29 November 1964, or BFC Dynamo's home matches against [[FC Erzgebirge Aue|BSG Wismut Aue]] on 13 November 1966, [[SV Babelsberg 03|BSG Motor Babelsberg]] on 10 September 1967 and [[Hallescher FC|HFC Chemie]] on 30 October 1971.{{cite magazine |author= |date=4 September 1962 |title=Der Kampf um Punkte... |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201962/FUWO%201962%2036.pdf |magazine=[[:de:Die neue Fußballwoche|Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo)]] |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[:de:Sportverlag Berlin|Sportverlag]] |volume=1962 |issue=36 |page=4 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=19 January 2025 }}{{cite magazine |author= |date=1 December 1964 |title=Kampf um Punkte... |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201964/FUWO%201964%2048.pdf |magazine=[[:de:Die neue Fußballwoche|Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo)]] |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1964 |issue=48 |page=4 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=19 January 2025 }}{{cite magazine |last=Buchspieß |first=Dieter |date=15 November 1966 |title=Trotz des 4:1 Vorsprungs noch gefährdet |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201966/FUWO%201966%2046.pdf |magazine=[[:de:Die neue Fußballwoche|Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo)]] |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1966 |issue=46 |page=4 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=19 January 2025 }}{{cite magazine |last=Winkel |first=Günter |date=12 September 1967 |title=Dynamo-Spiel wurde zur Farce |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201967/FUWO%201967%2037.pdf |magazine=[[:de:Die neue Fußballwoche|Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo)]] |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1967 |issue=37 |page=7 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=19 January 2025 }}{{cite magazine |last=Burghause |first=Hans-Günter |date= |title=Das Übel begann im Mittfeld |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201971/FUWO%201971%2044.pdf |magazine=[[:de:Die neue Fußballwoche|Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo)]] |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1971 |issue=44 |page=7 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=19 January 2025 }}}} Green was the colour of the [[Volkspolizei]], which was the official sponsor of BFC Dynamo during the East German era. The green alternative shirt was then abandoned in favor of the white shirt in the mid-1970s.{{cite magazine |author= |date=August 1976 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201976%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201976-77.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1976 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=6 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=11 April 2022}} BFC Dynamo would, however, once again play in a green shirt in the away match against [[AS Monaco]] in the [[1989–90 European Cup Winners' Cup]].{{cite magazine |last=Thiemann |first=Klaus |date=24 October 1989 |title=Courage, Disziplin |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201989/FUWO%201989%2045.pdf |magazine=[[:de:Die neue Fußballwoche|Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo)]] |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1989 |issue=43 |page=8 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=18 January 2025 }} In the 1985–86 season, the team began using a red and white diagonally striped alternate shirt.{{cite magazine |author= |date=August 1985 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201985%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201985-86.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1985 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=4 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=8 July 2023}} The diagonally striped alternative shirt was used for the rest of the 1980s.{{cite magazine |last=Thiemann |first=Klaus |date=August 1989 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201989%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201989-90.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1989 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=5 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=21 June 2022}} The crest of BFC Dynamo was unveiled at the club's founding ceremony in the [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen#Dynamo-Sporthalle|Dynamo-Sporthalle]] on 15 January 1966. The crest had been chosen by midfielder [[Waldemar Mühlbächer]] from several proposals.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=109}} The crest of BFC Dynamo during the East German era featured the lettering "BFC" in red and yellow and a stylized "D" for SV Dynamo on a white background, surrounded by a yellow [[wreath]].{{cite news |last=Heinke |first=Lothar |date=30 January 2001 |title=BFC Dynamo: Der Fußballverein muss für die Verwendung des eigenen Logos zahlen |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/berlin/bfc-dynamo-der-fussballverein-muss-fuer-die-verwendung-des-eigenen-logos-zahlen/198638.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} BFC Dynamo abandoned its East German crest when the club was rebranded as FC Berlin on 19 February 1990.{{cite news |last=Kühn |first=Alexander |date=2 August 2015 |title=Deutschlands unbeliebtester Club erwacht |url=https://www.bazonline.ch/sport/fussball/deutschlands-unbeliebtester-club-erwacht/story/12910242 |language=de |newspaper=[[Basler Zeitung]] |location=[[Basel]] |publisher=Tamedia Basler Zeitung AG |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150804073149/https://www.bazonline.ch/sport/fussball/deutschlands-unbeliebtester-club-erwacht/story/12910242 |archive-date=4 August 2015 |access-date=9 May 2021}}{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=20 November 2000 |title=Berliner Sport: Emblem-Problem – Der BFC Dynamo steht im Rechtsstreit um sein Vereinslogo |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berliner-sport-emblem-problem-der-bfc-dynamo-steht-im-rechtsstreit-um-sein-vereinslogo/180400.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}} The club used two different crests during the FC Berlin era. The first crest featured a stylized image of the roof of the [[Brandenburg Gate]] with the lettering "FCB" underneath and the club name "Fussballclub Berlin" in capital letters at the bottom, in white on a red background. It was only briefly used at the beginning of the FC Berlin era in 1990.{{rp|102–103}} The second crest featured a stylized image of a football with the Brandenburg Gate in front, the lettering "FCB" at the top and the club name "FC Berlin" at the bottom, in red on a white background. This crest was used from the spring of 1990 until the end of the FC Berlin era.{{rp|102–103}}{{cite web |url=https://www.turus.net/sport/fussball/8413-vor-20-jahren-fc-berlin-und-1-fc-dynamo-dresden-trennen-sich-3-4-vor-2-000-zuschauern.html |title=Vor 20 Jahren: FC Berlin und 1. FC Dynamo Dresden trennen sich 3:4 vor 2.000 Zuschauern |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=9 September 2015 |website=turus.net |location=[[Essen]] |publisher=Karsten Höft |language=de |access-date=16 August 2021}} BFC Dynamo reclaimed its East German crest when the club returned to its original club name on 3 May 1999. But the club was no longer in legal possession of the crest. The club had neglected to seek legal protection for its East German crest after German reunification. The neglect was likely due to managerial inexperience. Protection of trademarks was neither necessary nor common in East Germany. The crest was now owned by Peter Klaus-Dieter Mager, commonly known as "Pepe". Pepe Mager was a famous fan of [[Hertha BSC]] and a fan merchandise dealer.{{cite news |last=Leue |first=Gunnar |date=26 September 1998 |title="Der BFC lebt" dank Hertha-Mitglied |url=https://taz.de/Der-BFC-lebt-dank-Hertha-Mitglied/!1323458/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=1 November 2020}} The club tried to recover the crest from Mager though court action, without success. The ownership of the crest was instead passed on to Rayk Bernt and his company RA-BE Immobilien- und Handelsgesellschaft mbH.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=14 June 2007 |title=Teure Marke Dynamo |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/bfc-teure-marke-dynamo/871884.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=27 December 2021}}{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=20 June 2007 |title=D-Day bei Dynamo |url=https://www.neues-deutschland.de/artikel/111510.d-day-bei-dynamo.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=12 June 2009 |title=BFC Dynamo: Umstrittenes neues Logo |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berliner-fussball-bfc-dynamo-umstrittenes-neues-logo/1534064.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} [[File:BFC Dynamo - 2009.svg|thumb|right|The crest used by BFC Dynamo from 2009 to 2023.]] BFC Dynamo continued to use the disputed crest on its kits and webpage. But the club would have to ask the owner of the crest every time it wanted to have a pennant made and was unable to exploit the commercial value of the crest for its own benefit.{{cite web |url=https://www.turus.net/sport/4111-das-neue-logo-fuer-den-bfc-dynamo.html |title=Das neue Logo für den BFC Dynamo |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=18 June 2009 |work=turus.net |location=[[Essen]] |publisher=Karsten Höft |language=de |access-date=9 January 2022}} The legal situation around the crest would also have caused problems in the event of an advance to the Regionalliga, as the [[German Football Association|German football Association (DFB)]] required clubs to own their crests.{{cite news |last=Hasselmann |first=Markus |date=12 June 2009 |title=Genial daneben |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/genial-daneben/376854 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co.
