KG |access-date=1 November 2020}} In order establish independence, the club finally decided to adopt a new crest in 2009.{{cite news |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=13 February 2015 |title=BFC Dynamo: Peter Meyer: "Wir haben aus Fehlern gelernt" |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/bfc-dynamo-peter-meyer-wir-haben-aus-fehlern-gelernt/11367190.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=15 December 2021}} The new crest abandoned the traditional stylized "D" and the lettering "BFC", as they would have met legal obstacles. The new crest featured a black [[Coat of arms of Berlin|Berlin bear]] on claret and white stripes, together with the club name and the founding year. The first version of the new crest sparked controversy. The word "fußball" in the club name had been written in [[Capitalization|lower case]] with a double "s" instead of the graphene "[[ß]]". This was contrary to German spelling rules, where it is only permissible to write "fußball" with a double "s" when the word is written in upper case. Club President Norbert Uhlig ensured that there was absolutely no ulterior motive behind the spelling and claimed that the word had always been spelled like that on club pennants and scarfs. The Chairman of the Economic Council Peter Meyer later claimed that the spelling was a deliberate marketing ploy, in order to have new crest immediately known across Germany. A second version of the crest was soon made public, where the club name was written in upper case. The new crest was used by BFC Dynamo from the 2009–10 season. BFC Dynamo finally managed to win back the traditional crest in 2022, through the Chairman of the Economic Council Peter Meyer. Meyer had acquired the rights to the crest through one of his companies.{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Mathias |date=16 January 2023 |title=Hammer in Hohenschönhausen: Geheimnis gelüftet! Der BFC Dynamo holt sich sein DDR-Rekordmeister-Logo zurück |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/geheimnis-gelueftet-das-bfc-logo-kehrt-nach-hause-zu-den-dynamos-zurueck-li.307654 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=16 January 2023}} From the 2023–24 season, BFC Dynamo is once again playing with its traditional crest. The traditional crest was displayed for the first time since its reintroduction in a friendly match against Hertha BSC in front of more than 10,000 spectators at the [[Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark]] on 7 July 2023.{{cite news |last=Bühler |first=Karin |date=6 July 2023 |title=BFC gegen BSC: Dynamo empfängt Hertha zum Test und darf die Einnahmen behalten |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/sport-leidenschaft/hertha-bsc-gegen-bfc-dynamo-regionalligist-empfaengt-und-darf-die-einnahmen-behalten-li.366277 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=8 July 2023}}{{cite news |author= |date=7 July 2023 |title=Derby: Hertha BSC schlägt BFC Dynamo im Testspiel mit 2:0 |url=https://www.morgenpost.de/berlin/article238889165/hertha-bsc-testspiel-bfc-dynamo-2-0-testspiel-friedich-ludwig-jahn-sportpark-derby.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=8 July 2023}} ===Ownership of the traditional crest=== Many clubs in East Germany rushed to drop their East German names during the [[Peaceful revolution]]. BFC Dynamo was among the clubs to do so, in an attempt to distance the club from the [[Stasi]].{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=317}} The club was rebranded as FC Berlin on 19 February 1990 and consequently abandoned its East German crest. Pepe Mager was a famous fan of Hertha BSC and fan merchandise dealer. Mager had organized away trips for the fans Hertha in the early 1960s and was one of the founders of the notorious supporter group "Hertha-Frösche". He now sold his own fan merchandise from a mobile stand outside the [[Olympiastadion (Berlin)|Olympiastadion]].{{cite news |last=Blüchert |first=Alex |date=30 April 2019 |title=Herthas Fan-Original "Pepe" Mager mit 80 Jahren verstorben |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/berlin-sport/hertha-bsc/herthas-fan-original-pepe-mager-mit-80-jahren-verstorben |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite news |last=Dinkelaker |first=Max |date=16 April 2020 |title=Die Berliner Ostkurve |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/flammen-im-interzonenzug/555774?position=seiteninhalt&seite=1#seiteninhalt |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=1 November 2020}} Mager inquired with the register of associations in [[Charlottenburg]] in 1991 about all deleted names of East German clubs. He immediately found BFC Dynamo and saw business opportunities. Mager claimed that he first secured the former crest of BFC Dynamo for 80 [[Deutsche Mark]] in 1992. The name FC Berlin never became popular with the fans.{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=317}} Fans continued to identify themselves with the former name and crest. An overwhelming majority voted for the club to take back its original club name at the club's general meeting on 3 May 1999. Of the 135 present, 125 voted in favor, three against and seven abstained. BFC Dynamo thus reclaimed its East German crest, but the rights to the crest now belonged to Mager. Mager had registered the crest in his name at the [[Deutsches Patent- und Markenamt|German Patent and Trademark Office]] on 13 May 1997.{{cite news |last=Baingo |first=Andreas |date=9 June 1999 |title=Pepe Mager schockte Dynamo: BFC-Rechte bald in Holland? Pepe Mager setzt den BFC Dynamo unter Druck. |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1999_06_09.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} BFC Dynamo contacted Mager for a co-operation, but an agreement could not be reached. Mager held the opinion that the club should buy its merchandise from him, or simply buy the rights to the crest. He later informed the club that he had received interest from foreign buyers and offered the club to buy the rights. He claimed that the crest was worth 200,000 Deutsche Mark. BFC Dynamo on the other hand claimed that the crest should legally belong to the club. The club sued Mager in court on 20 November 2000, but eventually lost the case. The club decided to suspend the legal dispute with Mager in the summer of 2001 and instead wanted to find a solution outside court.{{cite news |last=Becht-Zwetkov |first=Diana |date=9 July 2001 |title=Trotz Geldnot: BFC-Kicker reisen ins Trainingslager |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_07_09.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=9 August 2021}} Mager was repeatedly exposed to minor threats from the environment around BFC Dynamo and eventually sold the crest to Rayk Bernt and his company RA-BE Immobilien- und Handelsgesellschaft mbH for a price of 50,000 Deutsche Mark in June 2002.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=12 June 2009 |title=Fussball mit ss |url=https://www.pnn.de/sport/ueberregional/von-matthias-koch-fussball-mit-ss/22242564.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210729112112/https://www.pnn.de/sport/ueberregional/von-matthias-koch-fussball-mit-ss/22242564.html |archive-date=29 July 2021 |language=de |newspaper=[[Potsdamer Neueste Nachrichten]] |location=[[Potsdam]] |publisher=Potsdamer Zeitungsverlagsgesellschaft mbH & Co. KG |access-date=1 November 2020 }} Bernt was a close associate of André Sommer.{{cite news |last=Rüttenhauer |first=Andreas |date=23 March 2005 |title=Okkupation eines Vereins |url=https://taz.de/!557234/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=1 November 2020}} Bernt and Sommer had assisted the club at the [[History of Berliner FC Dynamo (1989–2004)#Insolvency (2001–2002)|opening of the insolvency proceedings in 2001–2002]]. Both were long time fans of BFC Dynamo.{{cite news |last=Holz |first=Jürgen |date=28 November 2001 |title=Zwielichtige Gestalten geben Ton an |url=https://www.neues-deutschland.de/artikel/8765.zwielichtige-gestalten-geben-ton-an.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} But the duo was controversial for their connections to [[Hells Angels]]. Bernt and Sommer were almost as restrictive towards the club when it came to the crest as Mager had been. Bernt organized the production of fan merchandise in his own regime. The club would have to ask his company every time it wanted to have a pennant made. Bernt and Sommer usually agreed, manufactured the pennant and then sold it at their own fan merchandise stand at the stadium. BFC Dynamo continued to use the crest and would at times be given ten percent of the revenues from their sales. The club eventually offered 5,000 Euros for the crest, but was turned down. Sponsor Peter Meyer was also said to have offered 150,000 Euros for the buyback.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=10 June 2007 |title=Beim BFC Dynamo will eine Investorengruppe einsteigen |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2007_06_09.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=27 December 2021}} Bernt demanded a seven-digit sum, according to former Club President Mario Weinkauf. The lawyer representing RA-BE Immobilien- und Handelsgesellschaft mbH allegedly claimed the crest was worth around 600,000 Euros at the time.{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Mathias |date=5 April 2005 |title=Der lange Kampf ums Vereins-Logo: Noch steckt keiner zurück |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2006_04_05.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=4 November 2021}} [[File:BFCDynamoCrestSportforum2.jpg|thumb|right|The traditional crest of BFC Dynamo at the back of the main stand of the Stadion im Sportforum in 2023.]] President Weinkauf planned to recover the rights to the former crest with the help of Thomas Thiel and the company Treasure AG before the general meeting on 23 June 2007. Thiel was a co-owner of Treasure AG, which was intended as a new major sponsor. Bernt sold parts of the rights to the former crest to Thiel.{{cite web |url=https://register.dpma.de/DPMAregister/marke/register/306680289/DE |title=DPMAregister – Marken – Registerauskunft – Registernummer: 30668028 – Verfahrensansicht: Teilübergang (Position: 8) |website=dpma.de |location=[[Munich]] |publisher=[[Deutsches Patent- und Markenamt|German Patent and Trade Mark Office]] |language=de |access-date=29 May 2021}} The price was allegedly a six-digit sum. According to the plan, the club would be given the rights of use to the crest. The profits would thus go to the club. The club would pay a symbolic sum of 1 Euro per month for the rights of use. BFC Dynamo would then have a [[right of first refusal]] after the ten-year contract had expired and thus have the opportunity to eventually acquire ownership of the crest.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=18 June 2007 |title=Personalpoker bei Dynamo |url=https://taz.de/!268669/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=27 December 2021}} However, Weinkauf was ultimately rejected by club members in a vote of no-confidence at the general meeting on 23 June 2007. Weinkauf would then be contacted by the former president of [[Tennis Borussia Berlin]] Peter Antony. Treasure AG became a sponsor of Tennis Borussia Berlin instead and Weinkauf would later become president of the club.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=3 August 2007 |title=Oberligafußball ist Millionen wert |url=https://taz.de/!251806/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=27 December 2021}} Thiel sold his rights to the crest back to Bernt and his company BFC Dynamo Vermarktungsgesellschaft m.b.H in 2009.{{cite web |url=https://register.dpma.de/DPMAregister/marke/register/306680289/DE |title=DPMAregister – Marken – Registerauskunft – Registernummer: 30668028 – Verfahrensansicht: Umschreibung – Rechtsübergang (Position: 9) |website=dpma.de |location=[[Munich]] |publisher=[[Deutsches Patent- und Markenamt|German Patent and Trade Mark Office]] |language=de |access-date=29 May 2021}} The rights to the old crest where subsequently controlled again by the company RA-BE Immobilien- und Handelsgesellschaft mbH.{{cite web |url=https://register.dpma.de/DPMAregister/marke/register/397129262/DE |title=DPMAregister – Marken – Registerauskunft – Registernummer: 39712926 |website=dpma.de |location=[[Munich]] |publisher=[[Deutsches Patent- und Markenamt|German Patent and Trade Mark Office]] |language=de |access-date=23 February 2021}}{{cite web |url=https://register.dpma.de/DPMAregister/marke/register/306680289/DE |title=DPMAregister – Marken – Registerauskunft – Registernummer: 30668028 |website=dpma.de |location=[[Munich]] |publisher=[[Deutsches Patent- und Markenamt|German Patent and Trade Mark Office]] |language=de |access-date=23 February 2021}} The company is controlled by Bernt, who sold occasional items with the former crest at his own webpage. However, RA-BE Immobilien- und Handelsgesellschaft mbH transferred its rights to company AXXON AG in 2022.{{cite web |url=https://register.dpma.de/DPMAregister/marke/register/397129262/DE |title=DPMAregister – Marken – Registerauskunft – Registernummer: 39712926 – Verfahrensansicht: Umschreibung – Rechtsübergang (Position: 12) |website=dpma.de |location=[[Munich]] |publisher=[[Deutsches Patent- und Markenamt|German Patent and Trade Mark Office]] |language=de |access-date=7 January 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://register.dpma.de/DPMAregister/marke/register/306680289/DE |title=DPMAregister – Marken – Registerauskunft – Registernummer 30668028 – Verfahrensansicht: Umschreibung – Rechtsübergang (Position: 12) |website=dpma.de |location=[[Munich]] |publisher=[[Deutsches Patent- und Markenamt|German Patent and Trade Mark Office]] |language=de |access-date=7 January 2023}} In connection with the club's 57th anniversary in 2023, the Chairman of the Economic Council Peter Meyer revealed in an exclusive interview with [[Berliner Kurier]] that he had acquired the rights to the crest for the club through one of his companies. After more than 13 years, the traditional crest was finally back with the club. According to Berliner Kurier and [[Mitteldeutscher Rundfunk]] (MDR), a six-digit sum is said to have become due.{{cite web |url=https://www.mdr.de/sport/fussball_rl/bfc-dynamo-plant-rueckkehr-zu-altem-logo-102.html |title=BFC Dynamo plant Rückkehr zu altem Logo |author= |date=17 January 2023 |website=mdr.de |location=Leipzig |publisher=[[Mitteldeutscher Rundfunk]] |language=German |access-date=8 July 2023}} ===Championship star=== The [[Deutsche Fußball Liga|German Football League]] (DFL) introduced a system of [[Bundesliga#Honours|championship stars]] in the 2004–05 season. The system was meant to honor the most successful teams in the Bundesliga by allowing teams to display stars on their shirts for the championships they have won. The system awarded one star for three titles, two stars for five titles, and three stars for ten titles.{{cite news |last=Jahn |first=Micharl |date=10 August 2004 |title=Der BFC Dynamo greift nach den Sternen – Der zehnmalige DDR-Fußballmeister sucht nach Anerkennung |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2004_08_10.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=1 November 2020}} However, the system only counted titles won in the [[Bundesliga]] since the [[1963-64 Bundesliga|1963–64 season]].{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=11 October 2004 |title=BFC Dynamo startet Krieg der Sterne |url=https://taz.de/BFC-Dynamo-startet-Krieg-der-Sterne/!689350/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=11 October 2004}}{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=18 March 2005 |title=BFC Dynamo greift nach den Sternen |url=https://www.neues-deutschland.de/artikel/69122.bfc-dynamo-greift-nach-den-sternen.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.dw.com/en/east-germanys-star-quality-in-question/a-1581269 |title=East Germany's Star Quality in Question |last=Kluempers |first=John |date=13 May 2005 |website=Deutsche Welle |location=[[Bonn]] |access-date=1 November 2020}} BFC Dynamo submitted an application to the DFL and the DFB on 9 August 2004 to receive three stars for its ten [[List of East German football champions|titles]] in the [[DDR-Oberliga]]. The club asked for equal rights and argued that the [[German Football Association]] (DFB) had absorbed the [[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|German Football Association of the GDR]] (DFV) with all its statistics, international matches and goal scorers.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=10 August 2004 |title=Verrückt? BFC will genauso viele Sterne wie die Bayern – DDR-Rekordmeister fordert Gleichbehandlung von DFL |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2004_08_10_2.html |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite news |last=Rosentritt |first=Michael |date=10 August 2004 |title=Sport "Drei goldene Sterne stehen uns zu" Präsident Weinkauf über Dynamos DFB-Antrag |url=https://m.tagesspiegel.de/sport/drei-goldene-sterne-stehen-uns-zu-praesident-weinkauf-ueber-dynamos-dfb-antrag/538060.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} BFC Dynamo received support from Dynamo Dresden and 1. FC Magdeburg in its attempts to achieve recognition for East German titles. The DFL responded that it was not the responsible body, but the DFB remained silent for a long time. The DFB eventually declared itself responsible and recommended BFC Dynamo to submit a formal application for a new title symbol in accordance with a relevant paragraph. BFC Dynamo commissioned a law firm in [[Mitte (locality)|Mitte]] in January 2005 and sent a new letter to the DFB. The DFB announced that the application from BFC Dynamo was going to be negotiated in a meeting with the DFB presidium. The meeting with the DFB presidium on 18 March 2005 agreed that all titles won in East Germany, as well all others titles won in Germany since the first recognized championship in 1903, should qualify for stars.{{cite news |last=Gödecke |first=Christian |date=21 March 2005 |title=BFC Dynamo zieht mit Bayern gleich |url=https://www.spiegel.de/sport/fussball/krieg-der-sterne-bfc-dynamo-zieht-mit-bayern-gleich-a-347604.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Spiegel (website)|Spiegel]] |publisher=Der Spiegel GmbH & Co. KG |location=Hamburg |access-date=1 November 2020}} However, the decision was subject to approval by the DFL.{{cite news |last=Kölmel |first=Michael |date=28 March 2005 |title=Krieg der Sterne: Der BFC Dynamo läuft ohne Genehmigung mit seinen Meisterinsignien auf |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2005_03_26_3.html |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=20 January 2022}} No final decision had yet been made by the DFB presidium.{{cite news |author= |date=23 May 2005 |title=Ehrungen: BFC Dynamo wartet auf seine drei Meistersterne |url=https://www.mz.de/varia/ehrungen-bfc-dynamo-wartet-auf-seine-drei-meistersterne-2815272 |language=German |newspaper=Mitteldeutsche Zeitung |location=[[Halle (Saale)|Halle]] |publisher=Mediengruppe Mitteldeutsche Zeitung GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=20 January 2022}}{{cite news |last=Hönicke |first=Christian |date=26 March 2005 |title=Sternstunden im Sportforum |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/sternstunden-im-sportforum/595640.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite news |date=26 March 2005 |title=Trikot-Zoff: BFC Dynamo greift nach den Sternen |url=https://www.morgenpost.de/printarchiv/sport/article104211621/Trikot-Zoff-BFC-Dynamo-greift-nach-den-Sternen.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite news |date=26 March 2005 |title=Trikot-Zoff: BFC Dynamo greift nach den Sternen |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2005_03_26_2.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} [[File:Star full.svg|thumb|100px|right|BFC Dynamo is allowed to wear one star inscribed with the number ten for its ten East German championships.]]However, BFC Dynamo took matters in its own hands and unilaterally emblazoned its shirts with three stars. The team displayed the three stars for the first time in the match against [[FC Energie Cottbus|FC Energie Cottbus II]] in the [[NOFV-Oberliga Nord]] on 25 March 2005. The claim by BFC Dynamo was controversial because the club had been the favorite club of [[Erich Mielke]] and had had a connection to the [[Stasi]] during the East German era.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=5 April 2005 |title=Der dreiste Griff nach den drei Sternen |url=https://www.faz.net/aktuell/sport/mehr-sport/fussball-der-dreiste-griff-nach-den-drei-sternen-1233321.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung]] |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite news |last=Geisler |first=Sven |date=9 August 2013 |title=Verpfiffen |url=https://www.saechsische.de/plus/verpfiffen-2636378.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Sächsische Zeitung]] |location=[[Dresden]] |publisher=Sächsische Zeitung GmbH |access-date=27 October 2020 |quote=Laut einer internen Analyse der Saison 1984/85 gab es in acht von 26 Spielen klare Fehlentscheidungen, die den Berlinern mindestens acht Punkte brachten. So gewinnen sie mit sechs Zählern Vorsprung auf Dynamo Dresden und Lok Leipzig zum siebenten Mal in Folge den Titel.}} Critics in the DFB environment pointed to politically influenced championships in East Germany. BFC Dynamo had been sponsored by the [[Stasi]] and had enjoyed advantages. The club had privileged access to talents and a permanent training camp at Uckley in [[Königs Wusterhausen]]. However, also other clubs in East Germany had enjoyed similar advantages, which put the DFB in a difficult situation. Also former East German referee and [[Christian Democratic Union of Germany|CDU]] parliamentarian [[Bernd Heynemann]] spoke out for recognition of all East German titles. The DFL rejected the application from the DFB and recommended the DFB to only honor clubs that were champions in the Bundesliga.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=7 April 2005 |title=Griff nach den Sternen |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2005_04_07.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=1 November 2020}} However, the DFB chose to not follow the recommendation. The DFB presidium instead decided on a compromise solution on 19 July 2005 and adopted a new regulation for the 2005–06 season which gave all clubs the right to wear one single star for the championships they have won in the former East Germany and in Germany since 1903. Clubs were also allowed to indicate the number of championships they have won in the center of the star.{{cite web |url=https://www.dfb.de/news/detail/dfb-laesst-trikot-sterne-fuer-gewonnene-ddr-meisterschaften-zu-4782/ |title=DFB lässt Trikot-Sterne für gewonnene DDR-Meisterschaften zu |date=30 July 2005 |website=dfb.de |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=Deutscher Fußball-Bund e.V. |language=de |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite news |date=31 July 2005 |title=Oberligist BFC Dynamo bekommt einen Meisterstern |url=https://rp-online.de/sport/fussball/oberligist-bfc-dynamo-bekommt-einen-meisterstern_aid-17068601 |language=de |newspaper=[[Rheinische Post]] |location=[[Düsseldorf]] |publisher=RP Digital GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.turus.net/sport/3634-dfb.html |title=RW Erfurt und 1. FC Magdeburg erhalten Meisterstern |last=Schmahld |first=Ralf |date=20 February 2009 |website=turus.net |location=[[Essen]] |publisher=Karsten Höft |language=de |access-date=1 November 2020}} The regulation only applies to clubs playing in a league under the DFB umbrella. It does not apply to clubs playing in the 2. Bundesliga and Bundesliga, which are organized by the DFL. The new regulation meant that BFC Dynamo was finally allowed to emblazon its shirts with a championship star. The regulation also affected other former East German teams including Dynamo Dresden with its eight titles, [[1. FC Frankfurt]] with its six titles and Magdeburg with its three titles in the Oberliga. BFC Dynamo has since then used the championship star in accordance with DFB graphic standards, displaying one single star inscribed with the number ten for its ten East German titles.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=5 November 2005 |title=Zoff um Sterne hat ein Ende |url=https://www.neues-deutschland.de/artikel/80632.zoff-um-sterne-hat-ein-ende.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=1 November 2020}} ==Stadiums== {{Main|Sportforum Hohenschönhausen#Football stadium|l1 = Stadion im Sportforum}} The long-time home and training facility of BFC Dynamo is the [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen]] in [[Alt-Hohenschönhausen]] in [[Berlin]]. The sports complex is the location of the club offices and the clubhouse. It is also the base of the youth teams. More than 20 youth teams of BFC Dynamo regularly train at the facilities.{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2022/09/sportforum-hohenschoenhausen-berlin-fussball-stadion-bfc-dynamo.html |title=Sportforum Hohenschönhausen wird erneuert - aber nicht drittliga-tauglich |last=Witte |first=Lukas |date= 21 September 2022|website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=27 February 2023}} The Sportforum Hohenschönhausen is considered the spiritual home of the club. The Sportforum Hohenschönhausen was also known as the Dynamo-Sportforum during the East German era.{{cite magazine |author= |date=19 September 1961 |title=Visitenkarte der Oberliga: Rekordinternationaler ist Dynamos Kapitän |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201961/FUWO%201961%2038.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag ([[:de:Sportverlag Berlin|de]]) |volume=1961 |issue=38 |page=5 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=14 October 2022}} The sports complex was built as a training center for elite sport and was home to [[Sports club (East Germany)|sports club]] [[SC Dynamo Berlin]], with its many departments and squads.{{cite web |url=https://www.visitberlin.de/en/sportforum-hohenschonhausen |title=Sportforum Hohenschönhausen |website=visitberlin.de |location=Berlin |publisher=Berlin Tourismus & Kongress GmbH |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url= https://espazium.s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/files/2020-06/0.00_auslobung.pdf |title= Wettbewerb: Sportforum Berlin – Berlin Lichtenberg – Auslobung |date=5 June 2020 |website= espazium.ch |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Senate of Berlin|Senatsverwaltung für Stadtentwicklung und Wohnen]] |language=de |access-date=18 September 2020}} Development began in 1954 and expansion continued into the 1980s.{{cite web |url=http://www.museum-lichtenberg.de/index.php/angebote/archiv/74-ausstellungsarchiv/95-diktat-und-erfolg-2 |title="Diktat und Erfolg" Ausstellung über das Sportforum Hohenschönhausen 1954–1990 |date=5 December 2008 |website=museum-lichtenberg.de |location=Berlin |publisher=Museum Lichtenberg im Stadthaus |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}} The Sportforum is still unique as of today.{{cite news |last=Vetter |first=Claus |date=2 February 2014 |title=Sportforum Hohenschönhausen: Medaillen aus Wellblech |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/serie-olympisches-berlin-4-sportforum-hohenschoenhausen-medaillen-aus-wellblech/9419604.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=19 September 2020}} The sports complex covers an area of 45 to 50 [[hectare]]s and comprises 35 sports facilities as of 2020.{{cite web |url=http://www.museum-lichtenberg.de/images/publikationen/Tour_01_Hohenschnhausen_02.pdf |title=Lichtenberg entdecken – 1 Hohenschönhausen – Rad- und Fußtouren durch den Bezirk |website=museum-lichtenberg.de |publisher=Museum Lichtenberg im Stadthaus |location=Berlin |page=11 |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.berlin.de/sen/inneres/sport/sportstaetten/sportforum/ |title=Sportforum Berlin |website=www.berlin.de |location=[[Berlin]] |publisher=[[State of Berlin]] |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020 |archive-date=25 December 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181225030931/https://www.berlin.de/sen/inneres/sport/sportstaetten/sportforum/ }} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-67567-0002, SC-Dynamo - Turbine Erfurt 2-0.jpg|thumb|right|A match between SC Dynamo Berlin and [[FC Rot-Weiß Erfurt|SC Turbine Erfurt]] at the Walter-Ulbricht-Stadion in 1959.]]SC Dynamo Berlin played its first season at the large [[Walter-Ulbricht-Stadion]] in [[Mitte (locality)|Mitte]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=61–65}} The team moved its home matches to the football stadium in the Dynamo-Sportforum for the short transitional [[1955 DDR-Oberliga|1955 season]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=65–68}} SC Dynamo Berlin then returned to the Walter-Ulbricht-Stadion for the 1956 season.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=61–65}} The team would play at the Walter-Ublricht-Stadion for the rest of the 1950s.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=61–65}} SC Dynamo Berlin eventually moved its home matches permanently to the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum after the construction of the [[Berlin wall]] began on 13 August 1961.{{harvnb|Karas|2022|pp=65–68}} The football stadium in the Dynamo-Sportforum held a capacity of 10,000 spectators at the beginning of the 1961–62 season. The team drew average attendances between 3,000 and 6,000 spectators in the DDR-Oberliga at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum in the 1960s.{{refn|Average league attendances from the 1961–62 season to the 1968–69 season:{{cite web |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/attendance/gdr-oberliga-1961-1962/1/ |title=GDR » Oberliga 1961/1962 » Attendance » Home matches |website=worldfootball.net |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/attendance/gdr-oberliga-1962-1963/1/ |title=GDR » Oberliga 1962/1963 » Attendance » Home matches |website=worldfootball.net |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/attendance/gdr-oberliga-1963-1964/1// |title=GDR » Oberliga 1963/1964 » Attendance » Home matches |website=worldfootball.net |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/attendance/gdr-oberliga-1964-1965/1/ |title=GDR » Oberliga 1964/1965 » Attendance » Home matches |website=worldfootball.net |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/attendance/gdr-oberliga-1965-1966/1/ |title=GDR » Oberliga 1965/1966 » Attendance » Home matches |website=worldfootball.net |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/attendance/gdr-oberliga-1966-1967/1/ |title=GDR » Oberliga 1966/1967 » Attendance » Home matches |website=worldfootball.net |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/attendance/gdr-oberliga-1968-1969/1/ |title=GDR » Oberliga 1968/1969 » Attendance » Home matches |website=worldfootball.net |location=[[Munster]] |publisher=HEIM:SPIEL Medien GmbH & Co. KG |language=de |access-date=19 September 2020}}}}{{harvnb|Karas|2022|pp=238-238}} The highlights were matches against local rival [[1. FC Frankfurt|ASK Vorwärts Berlin]] and the various top teams during the period. The capacity of the football stadium in the Dynamo-Sportforum was gradually expanded during the 1960s.{{refn|group=nb|The capacity was 12,000 at the beginning of the 1966–67 season and 14,000 at the beginning of the 1968–69 season.{{cite magazine |date=2 August 1966 |title=Klub, BSG, SG: Berliner FC Dynamo |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201966/FUWO%201966%2031.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1966 |issue=31 |pages=8–9}}{{cite magazine |date=6 August 1968 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201968/FUWO%201968%20%2032.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1968 |issue=32 |page=6 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=8 June 2022}}}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-E0416-0009-001, BFC Dynamo Berlin - SC Chemie Leipzig 2-0.jpg|thumb|left|A match between BFC Dynamo and [[BSG Chemie Leipzig (1950)|BSG Chemie Leipzig]] at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum in 1966.]]BFC Dynamo began playing occasional matches that required floodlights at the larger [[Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark]] in [[Prenzlauer Berg]] from November 1968. The Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark was the home ground of FC Vorwärts Berlin at the time. However, the stadium became vacant when [[1. FC Frankfurt|FC Vorwärts Berlin]] was relocated to [[Frankfurt (Oder)|Frankfurt an der Oder]] on 31 July 1971.{{cite web |url=https://www.fcfrankfurt.de/verein/geschichte-2/ |title=Geschichte: 1971–1991: Frankfurter Epoche im DDR-Fußball |author= |date=n.d. |website=fcfrankfurt.de |location=[[Frankfurt an der Oder]] |publisher=1. FC Frankfurt (Oder) E.V. e. V. |language=de |access-date=13 April 2022}} BFC Dynamo played its home matches in the [[1971-72 European Cup Winners' Cup]] at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark.{{harvnb|Karas|2022|pp=68–70}} The matches against [[Åtvidabergs FF]] in the quarter-finals on 22 March 1972 and [[FC Dynamo Moscow]] in the semi-finals on 5 April 1972 were each attended by 30,000 spectators.{{cite magazine |last=Simon |first=Günter |date=28 March 1972 |title=Einen großen Kampf geliefert! |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201972/FUWO%201972%2013.pdf |journal=Neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1972 |issue=13 |page=8 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=12 March 2023}}{{cite magazine |last=Simon |first=Günter |date=11 April 1972 |title=Eine neue Tempo-Qualiät! |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201972/FUWO%201972%2015.pdf |journal=Neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1972 |issue=15 |pages=5–6 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=12 March 2023}} The team also played two home matches in the [[1971-72 DDR-Oberliga]] at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=358}} However, more matches at the stadium were not possible after the summer of 1972, as the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark was then undergoing extensive renovation for the upcoming [[10th World Festival of Youth and Students]].{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=61–65}} BFC Dynamo was qualified for the [[1972-73 UEFA Cup]]. However, neither the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark nor the Walter-Ulbricht-Stadion were available for the upcoming [[UEFA Cup]] matches. Both were undergoing extensive renovation for the 10th World Festival of Youth and Students. Instead, the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum underwent a complete transformation in just five weeks between the end of July 1972 and September 1972.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=61–65}} The capacity of the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum was now expanded to 20,000 spectators.{{cite magazine |date=August 1971 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201971%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201971-72.pdf |magazine=[[Deutsches Sportecho]]/Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |volume=1971 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=13 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=8 June 2022}}{{cite magazine |date=September 1972 |title=Visitenkarte |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%20Sonderausgabe/FUWO%201972%20Sonderausgabe.%20Saison%201972-73.pdf |magazine=Neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1972 |issue=Sonderausgabe |page=6 |issn=0323-6420 |access-date=8 June 2022}} BFC Dynamo played all home matches in the 1972-73 UEFA Cup at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum. The attendance of 20,000 spectators during the match against [[Liverpool F.C.|Liverpool]] on 29 November 1972 is still a record attendance for the stadium.{{cite news |last=Schlehahn |first=Britt |date=17 May 2020 |title=Vor dem Abriss nochmal BFC Dynamo gegen BSG Chemie – Corona verhindert letztes Halali |url=https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/vor-dem-abriss-nochmal-bfc-dynamo-gegen-bsg-chemie-corona-verhindert-letztes-hal/ |language=de |newspaper=Sportbuzzer |location=[[Hannover]] |publisher=Sportbuzzer GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230222215046/https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/vor-dem-abriss-nochmal-bfc-dynamo-gegen-bsg-chemie-corona-verhindert-letztes-hal/ |archive-date=22 February 2023 |access-date=31 July 2020}} BFC Dynamo remained at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum for a couple more seasons. The team saw rising attendance numbers at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum during the 1970s.{{sfn|Karas|2022|pp=238-238}} An average of 12,000 people attended the last six matches of BFC Dynamo at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum in the second half of the 1973–74 season. The match between BFC Dynamo and 1. FC Magdeburg in the [[1974-75 DDR-Oberliga]] at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum on 8 March 1975 was attended by a whole 19,000 spectators.{{cite magazine |last=Binkowski |first=Manfred |date=11 March 1975 |title=19 000 kamen auf ihre Kosten |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201975/FUWO%201975%2010.pdf |magazine=Die neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=197 |issue=10 |page=5 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=24 January 2023}} [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-R0221-0019, BFC Dynamo - SG Dynamo Dresden 3-4.jpg|thumb|right|A match between BFC Dynamo and SG Dynamo Dresden in front of 25,000 spectators at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark in 1976.{{cite magazine |last=Pfitzner |first=Joachim |date=24 February 1976 |title=Beide 'Dynamos' auf vollen Touren |url=https://www.fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201976/FUWO%201976%2009.pdf |journal=Neue Fußballwoche |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV der DDR]] |volume=1976 |issue=8 |page=5 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=11 April 2022}}]] BFC Dynamo eventually moved its home matches to the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark for the 1975–76 season, due to upcoming repair work at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum. The move was meant to be temporary, but eventually became permanent. The Dynamo-Sportforum would primarily serve as a training facility from then and the football stadium would be used mostly by the reserve team BFC Dynamo II. The Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark held a capacity of 30,00 spectators in the 1975–76 season The average home attendance of 16,538 spectators for BFC Dynamo at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Stadion in the [[1975-76 DDR-Oberliga]] is the highest average league attendance in club history.{{cite news |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=13 April 2020 |title=Nordwall und Gegengerade |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/nordwall-und-gegengerade/1769170 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210429165526/https://11freunde.de/artikel/nordwall-und-gegengerade/1769170 |archive-date=29 April 2021 |access-date=20 September 2020}} BFC Dynamo celebrated nine of its ten [[List of East German football champions|DDR-Oberliga titles]] in the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Stadium. The team also played most of its home matches in the [[UEFA competitions]] at the stadium. BFC Dynamo hosted teams such as [[Shakhtar Donetsk]], [[Red Star Belgrade]], [[Nottingham Forest]], [[Hamburger SV]], [[Aston Villa]], [[AS Roma]] [[FC Aberdeen]] at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Stadium in the 1970s and 1980. However, all matches in the [[Berlin derby|derby]] against [[1. FC Union Berlin]] were played at the neutral [[Stadion der Weltjugend]] from the 1976–77 season for security reasons.{{cite news |last=Jahn |first=Michael |date=22 April 2000 |title=Am Sonnabend treffen die zu DDR-Zeiten so ungleichen Stadtrivalen im Stadion an der Alten Försterei aufeinander: Zum letzten Mal: Union contra BFC |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/am-sonnabend-treffen-die-zu-ddr-zeiten-so-ungleichen-stadtrivalen-im-stadion-an-der-alten-foersterei-aufeinander-zum-letzten-mal-union-contra-bfc-li.6730 |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210623045450/https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/am-sonnabend-treffen-die-zu-ddr-zeiten-so-ungleichen-stadtrivalen-im-stadion-an-der-alten-foersterei-aufeinander-zum-letzten-mal-union-contra-bfc-li.6730 |archive-date=23 June 2021 |access-date=5 April 2021}}{{cite news |last=Bläsig |first=Horst |date=21 August 2005 |title=Lokalderby unter Polizeischutz |url=https://www.welt.de/print-wams/article131250/Lokalderby-unter-Polizeischutz.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=19 September 2020 |quote=Ausgetragen wurden die Derbys seit den 70er Jahren bis zur Wende im Stadion der Weltjugend an der Chausseestraße. Anfangs wegen des großen Zuschauerandrangs, später auch wegen der besseren Kontrollmöglichkeit. Das enge Stadion von Union am Rande der Wuhlheide war den DDR-Sicherheitsorganen als vermeintlicher Hort von Jugendprotest, Rowdytum und Zersetzung ohnehin ein Dorn im Auge.}}{{cite book |last1=Tomlinson |last2=Young |first1=Alan |first2=Christopher |date=2006 |title=German Football: History, Culture, Society |location=[[Abingdon-on-Thames]] |edition=1st |publisher=Routledge, Taylor & Francis Group |pages=53–54 |isbn=0-415-35195-2}}{{cite book |last1=Dennis |last2=LaPorte |first1=Mike |first2=Norman |date=2011 |title=State and Minorities in Communist East Germany |location=[[New York (state)|New York]] |edition= 1st |publisher=Berghahn Books |page=132 |isbn=978-0-85745-195-8}} A permanent training camp for BFC Dynamo was built in Uckley in the Zernsdorf district of [[Königs Wusterhausen]] in [[Bezirk Potsdam]] at the end of the 1960s.{{cite news |last=Morgenstern |first=Thomas |date=12 August 2005 |title=Feuer im alten BFC-Trainingscamp |url=https://www.neues-deutschland.de/artikel/980898.feuer-im-alten-bfc-trainingscamp.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=19 September 2020}} It was located in the woods and completely sealed off from the surroundings.{{cite news |last=Mohr |first=Franziska |date=5 May 2015 |title=Neues Heim mitten im Wald |url=https://www.maz-online.de/Lokales/Dahme-Spreewald/Neues-Heim-mitten-im-Wald |language=de |newspaper=[[Märkische Allgemeine]] |location=[[Hannover]] |publisher=Verlagsgesellschaft Madsack GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=19 September 2020}} The training camp covered an area of around 10 hectares. The complex was equipped with a boarding school, several football pitches, a sports hall, a swimming pool, a fitness area and a sauna.{{cite news |date=2 February 2005 |title=Sportzentrum im Nordkreis hat sich gemausert |url=https://www.lr-online.de/lausitz/luebben/sportzentrum-im-nordkreis-hat-sich-gemausert-37469980.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Lausitzer Rundschau]] |location=[[Cottbus]] |publisher=Lausitzer VerlagsService GmbH |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite news |last1=von Alten |last2=Pfaffenzeller |first1=Saara |first2=Martin |date=11 August 2015 |title=Gebäude neben geplanter Asylunterkunft brennt |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/berlin/polizei-justiz/brandstiftung-in-koenigs-wusterhausen-gebaeude-neben-geplanter-asylunterkunft-brennt/12171550.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=19 September 2020}}{{cite news |last=Galley |first=Eckhard |date=6 December 1989 |title=Zernsdorfer können nun das BFC-Objekt nutzen – Noch einmal eine Runde mit Journalisten in Uckley |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1989_12_06.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH}}{{cite news |last=Dankert |first=René |date=9 November 2019 |title=Wie Ex-DDR-Nationalspieler Burkhard Reich in einer Nacht- und Nebelaktion zum KSC kam |url=https://bnn.de/karlsruhe/vom-stasi-club-ging-es-fuer-reich-in-einer-nacht-und-nebelaktion-zum-ksc |language=de |newspaper=[[Badische Neueste Nachrichten]] |location=[[Karlsruhe]] |publisher=Badische Neueste Nachrichten Badendruck GmbH |access-date=19 September 2020}} The team would gather in Uckley days before its European matches. The players would have access to catering facilities, a [[sauna]], a nearby lake, a bowling alley, a cinema and pinball machines, among other things. [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1987-0717-036, Mannschaftsfoto BFC Dynamo.jpg|thumb|left|The team of BFC Dynamo in front of the new grandstand of the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark in 1987.]]BFC Dynamo moved its home matches temporary to the Dynamo-Sportforum for the 1986–87 season, as the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark was undrgoing redevelopment during the season for the upcoming 750th anniversary of Berlin. The team also played its home matches in the [[1986-87 European Cup]] at the Dynamo-Stadion im Sportforum. The team then returned to the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark for the 1987–88 season.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=463}} The Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark now had a new four storey [[grandstand]] and new floodlight masts. The current grandstand and the [[floodlight]]s of the stadium dates from this time.{{cite web |url=https://entwicklungsstadt.de/fussballherz-von-prenzlauer-berg-der-friedrich-ludwig-jahnsportpark/ |title=FUSSBALLHERZ VON PRENZLAUER BERG: DER FRIEDRICH-LUDWIG-JAHNSPORTPARK |last=Leffler |first=Björn |date=10 November 2023 |website=entwicklungsstadt.de |location=Berlin |publisher=MEDIA Group Berlin (Sole trader: Wolfgang Leffler) |language=de |access-date=24 March 2024}} The club was rebranded as FC Berlin after ''[[Peaceful revolution|Die Wende]]''. FC Berlin moved permanently to the [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen#Football stadium|Stadion im Sportforum]] at the beginning of the 1992–93 season. The team would remain in the Sportforum Hohenschönhausen for many seasons to come. The capacity of the Stadion im Sportforum had been reduced to about 12,000 spectators by 1992. FC Berlin only drew an average of a couple of hundred spectators per match at the Stadion im Sportforum in the early and mid-90s. The highlights were the matches against 1. FC Union Berlin. FC Berlin under Club President Volkmar Wanski announced plans in April 1998 to buy and modernize the stadium.{{cite news |last=Rößling |first=Ingo |date=19 April 1998 |title=FC Berlin will Stadion im Sportforum kaufen |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1998_04_19.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Morgenpost]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Morgenpost GmbH}} However, the plans never materialized. The team saw rising attendance numbers at the Stadion im Sportforum at the end of the 1990s. Active supporters of BFC Dynamo were traditionally found at the northern curved end, popularly known as the Nordwall stand. 4,220 spectators watched the match between BFC Dynamo and Union Berlin at the Stadion im Sportforum on 23 November 1999.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=554}} Supporters of BFC Dynamo installed new bucket seats on the main stand and built a new clubhouse next to the main stand of the Stadion im Sportforum in 2001–2003.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |title=Mielkes liebste Kicker: Der BFC Dynamo ist unbeliebt und pleite – doch seine Fans machen den alten DDR-Verein wieder flott |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2002_06_14.html |date=14 June 2022 |language=de |volume=2002 |issue=14 |newspaper=Zitty ([[:de:Zitty|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Zitty Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 June 2021}} The Stadion im Sportforum was then equipped with a 25-metre player tunnel and [[Poly(methyl methacrylate)|plexiglass]]-clad coaching benches in November 2004.{{cite news |author= |date=16 November 2004 |title=BFC "tunnelt" die Konkurrenz |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/bfc-tunnelt-die-konkurrenz |language=German |work=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=15 November 2021}} BFC Dynamo under Club President Mario Weinkauf announced new plans in April 2006 for a modern football stadium in the Sportforum Hohenschönhausen. The club now wanted to build a new modern stadium for 10,000–15,000 spectators.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=4 April 2006 |title=BFC Dynamo plant neues Stadion! |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/bfc-dynamo-plant-neues-stadion |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=20 September 2020}} However, these plans did not materialize either. The Stadion im Sportforum was closed at the end of the 2005–06 season following the riots during the match between BFC Dynamo and Union Berlin on 13 May 2006.{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=23 May 2006 |title=Sportgericht bestraft BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/sportgericht-bestraft-bfc-dynamo/713892.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=13 November 2021}} BFC Dynamo temporarily had to move to the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark.{{cite news |last=Wilheim |first=Raimund |date=4 August 2006 |title=Ein Abbruch und die Folgeschäden: Verstärkter BFC Dynamo geht nicht ohne Sorgen in die kommende Saison |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2006_08_04.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=18 December 2021}} The stadium was then refurbished in 2006–2007 to increase safety and meet certain requirements from the NOFV. The refurbishment included a new fence.{{cite news |last1=Görke |last2=Goldmann |first1=André |first2=Sven |date=7 March 2008 |title= Tribünen in Trümmern |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berlinsport/fussballstadien-tribuenen-in-truemmern/1182166.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=14 November 2021}} [[File:BFC Dynamo - SV Babelsberg April 2017.jpg|thumb|right|A match between BFC Dynamo and [[SV Babelsberg 03]] at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportkark in 2017.]]BFC Dynamo won promotion to the [[Regionalliga Nordost]] at the end of the 2013–14 season. The team moved permanently to the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark for the 2014–15 season, due to increased media and spectator interest following its promotion.{{cite news |last=Bardow |first=Dominik |date=11 August 2014 |title=Wende im Gelände |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berlinsport/was-wird-aus-dem-jahnsportpark-wende-im-gelaende/10312264.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=26 February 2023}} The [[2014-15 Regionalliga|2014-15 Regionalliga Nordost]] meant matches against well-known opponents such as 1. FC Magdeburg and [[FC Carl Zeiss Jena]]. The more central location of the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark was seen by the club as an opportunity to attract more spectators. The match between BFC Dynamo and 1. FC Magdeburg on 8 November 2014 was attended by 5,103 spectators.{{cite web |url=https://www.dfb.de/datencenter/regionalliga-nordost/2014-2015/13-spieltag/bfc-dynamo-1-fc-magdeburg-2212472 |title=BFC Dynamo - 1. FC Magdeburg 0:1, Regionalliga Nordost, 2014/2015, 13. Spieltag |author= |date=n.d. |website=dfb.de |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=Deutscher Fußball-Bund e.V. |language=German |access-date=10 March 2023}} Active supporters of BFC Dynamo have traditionally been found on the main stand, and on the side opposite the main stand ({{langx|de|die Gegengerade}}) of the Friedrich Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark. The match between BFC Dynamo and [[FC Schalke 04]] in the first round of the [[2018-19 DFB-Pokal]] at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark on 17 August 2017 was watched by 14,117 spectators. The attendance was then the highest attendance for BFC Dynamo in a single match since the fall of the Berlin wall.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=68–70}} The average league attendance of BFC Dynamo in the [[2017-18 Regionalliga|2017-18 Regionalliga Nordost]] would also be the highest average league attendance to date for BFC Dynamo since the [[1990–91 NOFV-Oberliga|1990-91 season]]. [[File:StadionimSportforum202313.jpg|thumb|left|The Stadion im Sportforum in 2023.]] BFC Dynamo had to play a number of matches at the Stadion im Sportforum at the end of the 2018–19 season due to safety issues relating to the dilapidated floodlights at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark.{{cite news |last=Brandbeck |first=Leonard |date=4 May 2019 |title=Absturzgefahr – Das Licht bleibt aus |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/flutlichtprobleme-im-jahn-sportpark-absturzgefahr-das-licht-bleibt-aus/24284912.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=20 September 2020}} The move was greeted by some supporters as a move to the true home of the club.{{cite news |date=8 May 2019 |title=BFC Dynamo-Fans freuen sich auf Rückkehr in die Heimat |url=https://www.faszination-fankurve.de/index.php?head=BFC-Dynamo-Fans-freuen-sich-auf-Rueckkehr-in-die-Heimat&folder=sites&site=news_detail&news_id=20261 |language=de |newspaper=Faszination Fankurve |location=[[Brühl (Rhineland)|Brühl]] |publisher=Johannes Mäling |access-date=20 September 2020}} The club was then set to return to the Sportforum in the [[2020–21 Regionalliga|2020–21 season]] as the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark was planned to be demolished for a complete redevelopment. The team was allowed to continue play in the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark until 31 December 2020.{{cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2020/08/berlin-fussball-jahn-sportpark-betriebserlaubnis.html |title=Zum letzten Mal vor dem geplanten Abriss: Betriebserlaubnis für Jahn-Sportpark erteilt |date=5 August 2020 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |access-date=20 September 2020 |archive-date=28 December 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201228174832/https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2020/08/berlin-fussball-jahn-sportpark-betriebserlaubnis.html }} BFC Dynamo then officially announced on 21 March 2021 that the club was going to move back to the Sportforum Hohenschönhausen for the next season.{{cite web |url=https://bfc.com/news.php?id=1548&cat_id=3#ontitle |title=Sportforum: Der BFC Dynamo kehrt zurück |date=21 March 2021 |website=bfc.com |location=[[Berlin]] |publisher=Berliner Fussball Club Dynamo e.V. |language=de |access-date=21 March 2021}} [[File:Modell Jahn Sportpark Berlin 2023.jpg|thumb|right|upright|An illustration of the new large stadium in the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark seen from the [[Mauerpark]].]] The Stadion im Sportforum was equipped with a floodlight system in April 2021.{{cite web |url=https://www.fupa.net/berichte/vsg-altglienicke-sportforum-berlin-bekommt-flutlicht-2761625.html |title=Sportforum Berlin bekommt Flutlicht |date=19 March 2021 |website=fupa.net |location=Berlin |publisher=19 Group GmbH |language=German |access-date=20 March 2021}}{{cite news |date=18 May 2021 |title=Neues aus dem Sportforum: BFC Dynamo baut an seiner Zukunft |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc-dynamo-baut-an-seiner-zukunft-li.159697 |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=18 June 2023}} The club organized a work effort in the summer of 2021 to get the stadium in shape for the [[2021-22 Regionalliga|upcoming Regionalliga season]]. {{cite news |date=4 June 2021 |title=Arbeitseinsatz im Sportforum: Kampf dem Wildwuchs! BFC braucht seine tollen Fans |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/kampf-dem-wildwuchs-bfc-braucht-sein-tollen-fans-li.163247 |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 June 2021}} Supporters of BFC Dynamo gathered and cleared sections of the old stadium from weeds.{{cite news |last=Wiezorek |first=Peter |date=5 June 2021 |title=Tolle Aktion im Sportforum: Einsatz im Stadion: Dynamo ruft, die Fans kommen |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/einsatz-im-stadion-dynamo-ruft-die-fans-kommen-li.163344 |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=6 June 2021}} Members of the interest group IG BFC'er also restored the iconic manual scoreboard above the curved end towards the Weißenseer Weg in time for the first home match of the 2021–22 season against [[Energie Cottbus]] on 28 July 2021.{{cite web |url=https://bfc.com/news.php?id=1680&cat_id=3#ontitle |title=Sportforum: Anzeigetafel erstrahlt in neuem Glanz |date=28 July 2021 |website=bfc.com |location=[[Berlin]] |publisher=Berliner Fussball Club Dynamo e.V. |language=de |access-date=1 August 2021}} The attendance for BFC Dynamo at the Stadion im Sportforum in the [[2021-22 Regionalliga|2021-22 Regionalliga Nordost]] was almost tripled compared to the last comparable league season before the [[COVID-19 pandemic in Germany|COVID-19 pandemic]]. 3,219 people watched the match between BFC Dynamo and FC Carl Zeiss Jena on 10 April 2022.{{cite news |last=Ludweig |first=Alxander |date=27 May 2022 |title=Endlich in den Westen? |url=https://www.nd-aktuell.de/artikel/1164116.bfc-dynamo-endlich-in-den-westen.html |language=German |work=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=27 February 2023}} In April 2024, BFC Dynamo reported that the club had achieved its highest attendance figures since ''Die Wende'' in the Sportforum Hohenschönhausen during the 2023–24 season, thus setting a new attendance record since 1990.{{Cite Instagram |author=[[BFC Dynamo|Berliner Fussballclub Dynamo e.V]] |user=bfcdynamo_official |postid=C5luD5KCAf2 |date=10 April 2024 |title=Rekord geknackt |language=de |location=Berlin |access-date=24 July 2024 |link=https://www.instagram.com/bfcdynamo_official/p/C5luD5KCAf2/ }} ===Future stadium=== The Sportforum Hohenschönhausen is the location of the club offices and the club house, which serves as a meeting point for supporters and parents.{{cite news |last=Lachmann |first=Michael |date=2 January 2022 |title=So plant der BFC den Aufstieg in die 3. Liga! |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/berlin-sport/so-plant-der-bfc-den-aufstieg-in-die-3-liga |language=German |work=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=26 February 2023 }}{{cite news |author= |date=15 February 2022 |title="Ein Verein braucht eine feste Heimat" |url=https://www.faszination-fankurve.de/news/34493/ein-verein-braucht-eine-feste-heimat |language=German |newspaper=Faszination Fankurve |location=[[Brühl (Rhineland)|Brühl]] |publisher=Faszination Fankurve, Sole trader: Johannes Mäling |access-date=27 February 2023 }} The Stadion im Sportforum stands as the center of club life.{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Mathias |date=23 September 2023 |title=BFC kämpft gegen Windmühlen: Sportausschuss des Abgeordnetenhauses zimmert nächsten Sargnagel in Dynamos Heimat |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/sportausschuss-des-abgeordnetenhaueses-zimmert-naechsten-sargnagel-in-dynamos-heimat-li.270163 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=27 February 2023 }} But the stadium does not meet the requirements for matches in the [[3. Liga]]. Among other things, the stadium lacks enough seating, [[under-soil heating]] and a sufficiently powerful floodlight system.{{cite news |last=Klages |first=Robert |date=1 March 2022 |title=Kein Drittliga-Stadion in Berlin-Lichtenberg: BFC Dynamo bei Aufstieg ohne Heimspielstätte? |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/berlin/bfc-dynamo-bei-aufstieg-ohne-heimspielstatte-5420857.html |language=German |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=27 February 2023 }}{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2022/09/sportforum-hohenschoenhausen-berlin-fussball-stadion-bfc-dynamo.html |title=Sportforum Hohenschönhausen wird erneuert – aber nicht drittliga-tauglich |last=Witte |first=Lukas |date=20 September 2022 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=27 February 2023}} The club and the supporters have long campaigned for an adaptation of the stadium to the requirements for the [[3. Liga]].{{cite news |last=Bunkus |first=Mathias |date=3 April 2023 |title=BFC Dynamo jubelt: Happy End nach monatelangem Kampf – die Zukunft des Sportforums ist gerettet! |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/bfc-dynamo-jubelt-happy-end-nach-monatelangem-kampf-die-zukunft-des-sportforums-ist-gerettet-li.334345 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=10 April 2023 }} The question became particularly relevant during the [[2021–22 Regionalliga|2021–22 season]], when BFC Dynamo was on the way to possibly qualify for the 3. Liga. Plans for an adaptation of the Stadion im Sportforum to the requirements for the 3. Liga were eventually agreed in the coalition agreement between the [[Christian Democratic Union of Germany|CDU]] and the [[Social Democratic Party of Germany|SPD]] for the new government coalition after the [[2023 Berlin state election]].{{ cite web |url=https://www.tag24.de/sport/fussball/bundesliga/dritte-liga/nach-langem-kampf-sportforum-des-bfc-dynamo-wird-fuer-die-3-liga-modernisiert-2795326 |title=NACH LANGEM KAMPF: STADION DES BFC DYNAMO SOLL FÜR DIE 3. LIGA MODERNISIERT WERDEN! |last=Schulze |first=Lukas |date=3 April 2023 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=de |access-date=10 April 2023 }}{{cite news |last=Klages |first=Robert |date=11 April 2023 |title=Sport |url=https://leute.tagesspiegel.de/lichtenberg/sportler/2023/04/11/268552/ |language=German |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=17 October 2023 }} According to the agreement, the Senate of Berlin will invest a total of 4 million [[Euro]]s in the stadium in the coming years.{{ cite web |url=https://www.rbb24.de/sport/beitrag/2023/04/sportmetropole-berlin-koalition-spd-cdu-plaene-ueberblick-.html |title=Das plant der designierte Senat für die Sportmetropole Berlin |last=Westhoff |first=Shea |date=22 April 2023 |website=www.rbb24.de |location=Berlin |publisher=[[Rundfunk Berlin-Brandenburg]] |language=German |access-date=17 October 2024 }} The Senate of Berlin commissioned a feasibility studie on a redevelopment of the football stadium in Sportforum Hohenschönhausen in January 2024.{{cite web |url=https://goolazo.berlin/wie-geht-es-weiter-mit-den-stadionplaenen-des-bfc-dynamo/ |title=Wie geht es weiter mit den Stadionplänen des BFC Dynamo? |last=Leffler |first=Wolfgang |date=30 September 2024 |website=goolazo.berlin |location=Berlin |publisher=BerliMedia GmbH |language=de |access-date=1 October 2024 |quote=}} The study aims to investigate the feasibility of a multifunctional ball sports stadium, suitable for 3. Liga football, with a capacity for approximately 10,000 spectators, in the Sportforum Hohenschönhausen.{{cite web |url=https://proprojekt.de/de/projekte/beda-rfs-pla-nung-mit-mach-bar-keits-s-tu-die-sport-fo-rum-berlin/ |title=Bedarfsplanung mit Machbarkeitsstudie - Sporforum Berlin |author= |date=n.d. |website=proprojekt.de |location=Frankfurt am Main |publisher=PROPROJEKT Planungsmanagement & Projektberatung GmbH |language=de |access-date=16 October 2024 }} The large stadium in the [[Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark]], on the other hand, has been under concrete plans for a complete redevelopment for several years. The stadium will be demolished and replaced by a new modern stadium.{{cite news |last=Rada |first=Uwe |date=15 December 2022 |title=Es bleibt nur ein rotes Band |url=https://taz.de/Siegerentwurf-fuer-den-Jahnsportpark/!5899394/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=29 April 2024}} The new large stadium in the Fredrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark is designed as an [[Inclusion (disability rights)|inclusive]] sports facility. The stadium will hold 20,000 spectators and meet the requirements for play in the 3. Liga and [[2. Bundesliga]].{{cite news |last1=Biekler |first1=Sabine |last2=Loy |first2=Thomas |date=22 January 2015 |title=Olympia: 150 statt 30 Millionen Euro für Jahn-Sportpark |url=http://www.tagesspiegel.de/berlin/was-kosten-die-spiele-in-berlin-olympia-150-statt-30-millionen-euro-fuer-jahn-sportpark/11262114.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=20 September 2020}}{{cite news |date=10 June 2019 |title=Berliner Jahn-Stadion soll bis zum Jahr 2023 durch einen Neubau ersetzt werden |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/mensch-metropole/berliner-jahn-stadion-soll-bis-zum-jahr-2023-durch-einen-neubau-ersetzt-werden-li.31593 |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=20 September 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://www.fupa.net/news/jahn-sportpark-masterplan-vor-dem-abriss-kommt-die-vsg-altglienicke-2920570 |title=Jahn-Sportpark-Masterplan: vor dem Abriss kommt die VSG Altglienicke |date=22 January 2023 |website=fupa.net |location=Berlin |publisher=19 Group GmbH |language=de |access-date=12 February 2023}} BFC Dynamo will be able to play matches at the new stadium. The demolition of the large stadium in the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark began on 8 October 2024.{{cite news |last=Läkamp |first=Jannik |date=8 October 2024 |title=Jahn-Sportpark in Berlin: Tribünen-Abriss gestartet – Bagger am Stadion |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/news/jahn-sportpark-tribuenen-abriss-gestartet-bagger-rollen-im-stadion-li.2260823 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=12 October 2024 }} The new stadium is planned to be opened towards the end of 2027.{{cite news |author= |date=7 February 2023 |title=Umbau des Jahnsportparks: Ausstellung zeigt Wettbewerbsentwürfe |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/berlin/umbau-des-jahn-sportparks-ausstellungen-zeigen-wettbewerbsentwuerfe-li.315063 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |access-date=29 April 2024}} ==Supporters and rivalries== {{Main|Berliner FC Dynamo supporters}} ===History=== BFC Dynamo initially had modest support, but with its growing successes in the 1970s, the club began to attract young fans, primarily from the central areas around the [[Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark]], such as [[Prenzlauer Berg]] and [[Mitte (locality)|Mitte]]. Many came from [[working class]] families in Prenzlauer Berg.{{cite news |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=23 April 2014 |title=Willmanns Kolumne: Die Feierbiester des BFC Dynamo sind zurück |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/willmanns-kolumne-die-feierbiester-des-bfc-dynamo-sind-zurueck/9794266.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=20 March 2021}} The supporter scene became a focal point for various subcultures in the late 1970s and beginning of the 1980s.{{cite news |last=Bertram |first=Marco |date=10 December 2015 |title=Buchreihe "Fußballfibeln": Ich wohne in der Zone! – Die BFC-Fans der 1980er Jahre |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/buchreihe-fussballfibeln-ich-wohne-in-der-zone-die-bfc-fans-der-1980er-jahre/12704126.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=26 April 2020}}{{cite news |last=Wurm |first=Philip |date=4 March 2016 |title=Der große Fußball-Traum des BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.zitty.de/der-grosse-fussball-traum-des-bfc-dynamo/ |language=de |newspaper=Zitty ([[:de:Zitty|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=GCM Go City Media GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190201063338/https://www.zitty.de/der-grosse-fussball-traum-des-bfc-dynamo/ |archive-date=1 February 2019 |access-date=21 October 2020}} There were [[Punk subculture|punks]], rockers, hippies and a few early [[skinhead]]s.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=30 August 2007 |title=Schläger hinter Stacheldraht |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/bfc-dynamo-und-1-fc-union-zu-ddr-zeiten-schlaeger-hinter-stacheldraht/1028396.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=27 April 2020}}{{cite news |last=Schwermer |first=Alina |date=15 May 2019 |title=Der Ost-Ost-Konflikt |url=https://taz.de/Berliner-Fussball-Klubs/!5593199/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=15 October 2023}} Some were [[Left-wing politics|left-leaning]] and others were [[Right-wing politics|right-leaning]]. {{Quote box | quote = "We provoked with chants and slogans ... We were right, left, punk, hippie, skinhead. We were direct and provocative, kind and evil, in love, or drunk. Cool words were always well received. Right or left, I don't want to classify one. We were all in our fan group against the [[East Germany|GDR]], rebellion!" | author = – A fan of BFC Dynamo in the 1980s{{cite book |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=2013 |title=Stadionpartisanen nachgeladen. Fans und Hooligans in der DDR. |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=nofb-shop.de, Sole trader: Stephan Trosien |page=39 |isbn=978-3-00-039788-2 |quote='Wir provozierten meist Gesängen und Spruchparolen, wie zum Beispiel: "Hundert Meter im Quadrat, Mauer, Miene, Stacheldraht, jetz wißt ihr wo ich wohne, ja ich wohne in der Zone". Oder: "Einmar wird es anders sein, dann sperren wir die Bullen ein." Wir waren rechts, links, Punk, Hippie, Skinhead. Wir waren direkt und provozierend, lieb und böse, verliebt oder besoffen. Coole Sprüche kamen immer gut an. Rechts oder links, will ich keinen einordnen. Wir waren in unserer Fangruppe alle gegen die DDR, Rebellion!'}} | source = | align = right | width = 25em }} Young people were gradually attracted by the provocative image of the club. One supporter recalled that the 1980s "were my greatest years, as we always had glorious success in provoking other fans".{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=225}} The supporter scene of BFC Dynamo was acclaimed as creative and humorous. One fan of BFC Dynamo said: "Our goal is to always do something that nobody expects!"{{cite news |last=Hahn |first=Anne |date=31 August 2007 |title=Hooligans in der DDR: Feuerwerk am hellichten Tag |url=https://www.freitag.de/autoren/der-freitag/feuerwerk-am-hellichten-tag |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Freitag]] |location=Berlin |publisher=der Freitag Mediengesellschaft mbh & Co. KG |access-date=1 May 2020}} [[Western Bloc|The West]] had a great influence on the supporter scene and fashion played a big role.{{cite book |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=2013 |title=Stadionpartisanen nachgeladen. Fans und Hooligans in der DDR. |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=nofb-shop.de, Sole trader: Stephan Trosien |page=39 |isbn=978-3-00-039788-2}}{{cite book |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=2013 |title=Stadionpartisanen nachgeladen. Fans und Hooligans in der DDR. |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=nofb-shop.de, Sole trader: Stephan Trosien |page=132 |isbn=978-3-00-039788-2}}{{cite book |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=2013 |title=Stadionpartisanen nachgeladen. Fans und Hooligans in der DDR. |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=nofb-shop.de, Sole trader: Stephan Trosien |page=176 |isbn=978-3-00-039788-2}} Football supporters in East Berlin shared a sense of superiority over their counterparts in the [[Administrative divisions of East Germany|regional districts]].{{cite thesis |last=Köhler |first=Michael |date=2011 |title=Hooliganismus in der DDR – Eingriff des Staates gegen die BFC Dynamo-Hooliganszene |degree=Bachelor |language=de |location=[[Berlin]] |publication-place=[[Mittweida]] |publisher=[[Hochschule Mittweida]] |url=https://monami.hs-mittweida.de/frontdoor/deliver/index/docId/1338/file/Binder1.pdf |pages=15–18 |access-date=20 August 2022}} This was also the case with the supporters of 1. FC Union Berlin, but notably with the supporters of BFC Dynamo.{{cite news |last=Sperling |first=Peer |date=12 November 2003 |title=Geschichten vom Serienmeister |url=https://www.nd-aktuell.de/artikel/44320.geschichten-vom-serienmeister.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=17 July 2021}}{{cite book |last1=Dennis |last2=LaPorte |first1=Mike |first2=Norman |date=2011 |title=State and Minorities in Communist East Germany |location=[[New York (state)|New York]] |edition=1st |publisher=Berghahn Books |pages=131–132 |isbn=978-0-85745-195-8}} Football-related violence spread in East Germany in the 1970s.{{cite book |last1=Tomlinson |last2=Young |first1=Alan |first2=Christopher |date=2006 |title=German Football: History, Culture, Society |location=[[Abingdon-on-Thames]] |edition=1st |publisher=Routledge, Taylor & Francis Group |page=56 |isbn=0-415-35195-2}} The hatred of opposing fans welded the supporters of BFC Dynamo together.{{cite news |last=Boßdorf |first=Hagen |date=13 April 1991 |title=Das prügelnde Mysterium |url=https://taz.de/!1723903/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=10 May 2020}} The supporters of BFC Dynamo responded to the hostile environment and learned to compensate their smaller numbers by being more aggressive and better organized.{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=234}}{{cite web |url=https://www.fupa.net/berichte/fc-energie-cottbus-das-engagement-gegen-rechts-ist-nicht-imm-2241924.html |title="Das Engagement gegen Rechts ist nicht immer störungsfrei" |last=Bock |first=Sven |date=6 November 2018 |website=fupa.net |location=[[Cottbus]] |publisher=Lausitzer VerlagsService GmbH |language=de |access-date=1 May 2020}} They would eventually gain a reputation for being particularly organized and violent.{{cite book |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=2013 |title=Stadionpartisanen nachgeladen. Fans und Hooligans in der DDR. |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=nofb-shop.de, Sole trader: Stephan Trosien |pages=175–176 |isbn=978-3-00-039788-2}} The development in the supporter scene caught the attention of the authorities. The Stasi would try to control the supporter scene with a broad catalogue of repressive measures. Numerous supporters of BFC Dynamo were sentenced to long and short prison terms in the 1980s. All football fan clubs in East Germany had to undergo registration. In the 1986–87 season, BFC Dynamo had 17 unauthoritzed fan clubs and 15 registered fan clubs (with a total of 32 fan clubs). As a comparison, at Union Berlin, 61 out of 70 fan clubs (87 per cent of all fan clubs) were registered in the 1986–87 season.{{cite book |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=2013 |title=Stadionpartisanen nachgeladen. Fans und Hooligans in der DDR. |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=nofb-shop.de, Sole trader: Stephan Trosien |page=343 |isbn=978-3-00-039788-2 }} Unauthorized fan clubs were those that were unregistered or did not meet [[Deutscher Fußball-Verband der DDR|DFV]] guidelines.{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=196}} Registered fan clubs, on the other hand, were those that were willing to cooperate with the authorities. More and more supporters of BFC Dynamo adopted skinhead fashion in the early 1980s. Skinhead fashion was now considered the most provocative outfit. From the mid-1980s, the supporter scene was increasingly associated with skinhead fashion and far-right tendencies.{{cite thesis |last=Köhler |first=Michael |date=2011 |title=Hooliganismus in der DDR – Eingriff des Staates gegen die BFC Dynamo-Hooliganszene |degree=Bachelor |language=de |location=[[Berlin]] |publication-place=[[Mittweida]] |publisher=[[Hochschule Mittweida]] |url=https://monami.hs-mittweida.de/frontdoor/deliver/index/docId/1338/file/Binder1.pdf |page=20 |access-date=6 October 2023}}{{refn|group=nb|In August 1985, 14 members of the supporter group Analen Jungs assaulted people in [[Dresden]] and sang fascist songs such as "My father was an [[Schutzstaffel|SS]]-soldier". They were arrested, and nine received prison sentences.{{cite book |last1=Tomlinson |last2=Young |first1=Alan |first2=Christopher |date=2006 |title=German Football: History, Culture, Society |location=[[Abingdon-on-Thames]] |edition=1st |publisher=Routledge, Taylor & Francis Group |page=61 |isbn=0-415-35195-2}}}} Right-wing slogans and [[Fascism|fascist]] chants were regarded as particularly challenging forms of provocations, as [[anti-fascism]] was state doctrine and [[nazism]] officially did not exist in East Germany.{{cite book |last1=Dennis |last2=LaPorte |first1=Mike |first2=Norman |date=2011 |title=State and Minorities in Communist East Germany |location=[[New York (state)|New York]] |edition= 1st |publisher=Berghahn Books |page=140 |isbn=978-0-85745-195-8}} One supporter of BFC Dynamo said: "The scene wasn't right-wing, we did describe ourselves as right-wing, but that was more of a pure provocation, none of us really knew anything about politics.
