But to [[Hitler salute|raise your arm]] in front of the cops was a real kick, for some [[Volkspolizei|Vopos's]], their whole world collapsed".{{cite book |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=2013 |title=Stadionpartisanen nachgeladen. Fans und Hooligans in der DDR. |edition=1st |location=Berlin |publisher=nofb-shop.de, Sole trader: Stephan Trosien |pages=75–76 |isbn=978-3-00-039788-2 |quote=Rechts war die Szene nicht, wir ham uns zwar als rechts bezeichnet, doch war das eher pure Provokation, über Politik wusste keiner groß Bescheid. Aber vor den Bullen den Arm zu heben, war schon ein Ding, da ist bei einigen Vopos's ne Welt zusammengebrochen.}} Supporters of BFC Dynamo radicalized in the 1980s.{{cite news |last=Blaschke |first=Ronny |date=1 August 2007 |title=Fußball im Osten: Radikalisierung der Fans |url=https://www.spiegel.de/sport/fussball/fussball-im-osten-radikalisierung-der-fans-a-495717.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Spiegel (website)|Der Spiegel]] |publisher=Der Spiegel GmbH & Co. KG |location=Hamburg |access-date=16 August 2022}} The first East German [[Football hooliganism|hooligan]] group developed from the supporter scene of BFC Dynamo in the 1980s. The development was partly a response to the increasing state repression against the supporter scene; the more violence the Stasi used, the more radicalized supporters became. The hooligans of BFC Dynamo described the East German "[[bourgeoisie]]", who waved the [[Flag of East Germany|East German flag]] at the [[Socialist Unity Party of Germany|party]] conference, but gave [[the finger]] to state power in the stadium, as their enemy.{{cite magazine |last=Blaschke |first=Ronny |date=26 March 2007 |title=Kehrseite des Sommermärchens- FUSSBALLKRAWALLE: Der BFC Dynamo - ein Beispiel dafür, wie Rechtsradikale in Ostclubs Gewalt säen und Macht ernte |url=https://webarchiv.bundestag.de/archive/2010/0427/dasparlament/2007/13/Thema/14425160.html |language=de |magazine=DasParlament |location=Berlin |publisher=[[German Bundestag]] |volume=2007 |issue=13 |page= |access-date=22 August 2024 |quote=Als ihr Feinbild bezeichneten sie jene "Spießer", die zum Parteitag die DDR-Flagge schwenkten, aber im Stadion der Staatsmacht den Finger zeigten. Schon in den Achtzigern führte das zu Ausschreitungen.}} The [[1987-88 FDGB-Pokal]] final between BFC Dynamo and [[FC Carl Zeiss Jena]] saw some of the most serious violence ever witnessed at a football match in East Germany. Around 300 supporters of BFC Dynamo tried to invade the pitch at the victory ceremony, causing extensive damage.{{sfn|McDougall|2014|p=211}} They were only stopped by forces from the [[Volkspolizei]] and the [[Felix Dzerzhinsky Guards Regiment|Stasi Guards Regiment "Felix E. Dzerzhinsky"]].{{cite web |url=https://www.hagalil.com/2019/05/antisemitismus-ddr/ |title=Neonazismus, Rassismus und Antisemitismus in der DDR |author= |date=29 May 2019 |website=hagalil.com |location=München |publisher=Dr. Andrea Livnat (ViSdP ) |language=de |access-date=29 September 2023 |quote=Ca. 300 Berliner Fans versuchten gewaltsam auf das Spielfeld zu gelangen, was "Sicherungskräfte des Wachregimentes des MfS und der Volkspolizei" verhindern konnten.}} An organized hooligan scene that was unique in East Germany would eventually develop at BFC Dynamo in the late 1980s. [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1990-1110-014, FC Berlin - HFC Chemie 0-0.jpg|right|thumb|300px|Supporters of FC Berlin commemorate Mike Polley at the Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark on 10 November 1990.]]A wave of violence swept through the football stadiums of East Germany in 1990. One of the largest hooligan scenes in Germany was formed around FC Berlin. Expensive sportswear was now the new fashion in the supporter scene; brands such as [[Adidas]], [[Iceberg (fashion house)|Iceberg]], [[Diesel (brand)|Diesel]] and [[Ray Ban]] became popular.{{cite news |last=Boßdorf |first=Hagen |date=12 November 1990 |title=Die intelligenten Idioten |url=https://taz.de/!1744536/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=10 May 2020}}{{rp|88–89}}{{cite news |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=26 May 2016 |title=Hooligans in der DDR: "Danach zoger wir brandschatzend durch Ostberlin" |url=https://www.zeit.de/sport/2016-05/hooligans-ddr-bfc-dynamo-berlin |language=de |newspaper=Zeit Online |location=Hamburg |publisher=Zeit Online GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230406161431/https://www.zeit.de/sport/2016-05/hooligans-ddr-bfc-dynamo-berlin |archive-date=6 April 2023 |access-date=24 August 2021 |url-status=live }} 18-year-old FC Berlin supporter Mike Polley was shot dead by police during riots in connection with the away match against [[FC Sachsen Leipzig]] on 3 November 1990.{{cite news |last=Fuge |first=Jens |date=3 November 2013 |title=Der Tag, an dem Mike Polley starb |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/der-tag-an-dem-mike-polley-starb/460400 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=23 May 2020}} The police had fired between 50 and 100 shots in about a minute.{{cite news |last=Kohlhuber |first=Sören |date=4 November 2015 |title=Der letzte deutsche Fußballtote |url=https://www.zeit.de/sport/2015-11/mike-polley-bfc-hooligans |language=de |newspaper=Zeit Online |location=Hamburg |publisher=Zeit Online GmbH |access-date=29 August 2022}} After the shootings, the riots continued in central [[Leipzig]] with great devastation; up to 31 shops were smashed and looted.{{cite news |last=Schwarz |first=Stefan |date=5 November 1990 |title=Tödliche Schüsse nach Krawallen |url=https://taz.de/!1745374/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=23 May 2020}} Supporters of FC Berlin organized a funeral march for Polley with 1,000 participants in Prenzlauer Berg on 10 November 1990.{{cite news |author= |date=12 November 1990 |title= Hooligans trauern friedlich in Berlin |url= https://taz.de/Hooligans-trauern-friedlich-in-Berlin/!1744481/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=3 October 2023}} An investigation against ten police officers was opened after the shootings, but closed in April 1992.{{cite news |last=Skorupinski |first=Dierek |date=12 February 2016 |title=Tod eines Fußballfans |url=https://www.freitag.de/autoren/footballuprising/tod-eines-fussballfans|language=de |newspaper=[[Der Freitag]] |location=Berlin |publisher=der Freitag Mediengesellschaft mbh & Co. KG |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210225134810/https://www.freitag.de/autoren/footballuprising/tod-eines-fussballfans |archive-date=25 February 2021 |access-date=23 May 2020}} The exact circumstances around the death of Polley was never clarified.{{cite news |last=Puppe |first=Mattias |date=2 November 2010 |title=Ausnahmezustand in Leutzsch: Als in Leipzig ein Fußballfan erschossen wurde |url=https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/ausnahmezustand-in-leutzsch-vor-20-jahren-wurde-in-leipzig-ein-hooligan-erschoss/|language=de |newspaper=Sportbuzzer |location=Hannover |publisher=Sportbuzzer GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210227155713/https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/ausnahmezustand-in-leutzsch-vor-20-jahren-wurde-in-leipzig-ein-hooligan-erschoss/ |archive-date=27 February 2021 |access-date=23 May 2020}}{{cite news |date=29 March 2018 |title=Gedenken an Mike Polley: BFC plant Fanmarsch zum Gastspiel bei Chemie Leipzig |url=https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/gedenken-an-mike-polley-bfc-plant-fanmarsch-zum-gastspiel-bei-chemie-leipzig/ |language=de |newspaper=Sportbuzzer |location=Hannover |publisher=Sportbuzzer GmbH |access-date=23 May 2020 |archive-date=9 April 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220409095937/https://www.sportbuzzer.de/artikel/gedenken-an-mike-polley-bfc-plant-fanmarsch-zum-gastspiel-bei-chemie-leipzig/ }} Stadium attendance at FC Berlin collapsed in 1990. Ordinary supporters disappeared and only young supporters remained. The violent faction of FC Berlin came to shape the entire 1990–91 season. Serious riots broke out in [[Rostock]] in connection with the match between [[FC Hansa Rostock]] and FC Berlin on 16 March 1991. A group of 500–600 supporters of FC Berlin had travelled to the match with a special train. Supporters of FC Berlin devastated a shopping street in central Rostock and clashed with the police. 21 people, including nine police officers, were injured in the turmoil.{{cite news |author= |date=20 March 1991 |title=Gomolka: Stasi steckt hinter Rostocker Randalierern |url=https://taz.de/Gomolka-Stasi-steckt-hinter-Rostocker-Randalierern/!1727288/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=22 April 2023}}{{cite news |date=18 March 1991 |title=Hooligans wüteten in Rostock: Plünderungen und Straßenschlachten |url=https://www.neues-deutschland.de/artikel/299797.pluenderungen-und-strassenschlachten.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=20 August 2021}} The hooligan scene of FC Berlin at the beginning of the 1990s was considered the most notorious for years in Germany. Hooligans of FC Berlin were subsequently involved in numerous fights in stadiums, woods and meadows. In the years after German reunification, the club's eternal outsider image attracted people from the underground. Playing for meager crowds in regional leagues, the club eventually became a meeting place for individuals from Berlin's [[far-right politics|far-right]], hooligan and criminal underground. The FC Berlin mob remained by far the largest in the [[New states of Germany]] still in the mid-1990s. The hooligan scene around FC Berlin counted 500 people in 1996.{{cite news |last=Landwehr |first=Bernhard |date=17 May 1996 |title=Dynamos abgewickelte Kinder |url=https://www.zeit.de/1996/21/Dynamos_abgewickelte_Kinder |language=German |issue=21/1996 |newspaper=[[Die Zeit]] |location=Hamburg |publisher=Zeit Online GmbH |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170121123107/http://www.zeit.de/1996/21/Dynamos_abgewickelte_Kinder/seite-2 |archive-date=21 January 2017 |access-date=9 September 2023}} More than 400 hooligans from FC Berlin attended the away match against [[SG Dynamo Dresden|1. FC Dynamo Dresden]] on 16 March 1996. Hooligans from FC Berlin rioted in central Dresden before the match. It took a large police effort with 580 officers to bring the riots under control.{{cite news |author= |date=18 March 1996 |title=Randale in der City - 1:0 im Stadion |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/archiv/den-fc-berlin-trennten-120-sekunden-vom-punktgewinn-in-dresden-union-verlor-randale-in-der-city-1-0-im-stadion-li.470383 |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=6 March 2024}} There were several outbreaks of violent hooliganism among supporters of BFC Dynamo in the late 1990s and the beginning of the 2000s.{{cite news |date=12 May 1999 |title=Dynamo-Fans laufen Amok |url=https://www.spiegel.de/sport/fussball/anderes-inland-dynamo-fans-laufen-amok-a-22044.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Der Spiegel (website)|Spiegel]] |publisher=Der Spiegel GmbH & Co. KG |location=Hamburg |access-date=4 June 2021}}{{cite news |last=Schnedelbach |first=Lutz |date=26 March 2001 |title=Krawalle nach Fußballspiel |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_03_26.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=13 October 2023}}{{cite news |author= |date=13 February 2005 |title=Verletzte bei Krawall im Stadion |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/themen/brandenburg/verletzte-bei-krawall-im-stadion/584678.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=13 October 2023}}{{cite news |last=Hasselmann |first=Jörg |date=7 August 2005 |title=Aufwärmphase für dieHooligans |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/berlin/aufwaermphase-fuer-diehooligans/631124.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=13 October 2023}}{{cite news |date=15 May 2006 |title=BFC-Hooligans, Krawalle beim Derby gegen Union |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/bfc-hooligans-krawalle-beim-derby-gegen-union |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=13 October 2023}} The period also saw several controversial police operations against BFC Dynamo supporters, including the raid on the Jeton discothèque in [[Friedrichshain]] after a football fan tournament in the [[Sportforum Hohenschönhausen]] in August 2005, with 39 people injured, and the violent intervention against BFC Dynamo supporters during the away match against [[Tennis Borussia Berlin]] in December 2008, with 58 persons injured, including seven police officers.{{cite news |last1=Behrendt |last2=Lier |last3=Schlichting |first1=Michael |first2=Axel |first3=Sebastian |date=23 August 2005 |title=Vorwürfe nach Polizeieinsatz |url=https://www.welt.de/print-welt/article160584/Vorwuerfe-nach-Polizeieinsatz.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Welt]] |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=13 October 2023}}{{cite news |last=Plutonia |first=Plarre |date=30 August 2005 |title=Polizeipräsident verteidigt Razzia |url=https://taz.de/!553429/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=13 October 2023}}{{cite news |last=Buntrock |first=Tanja |date=9 December 2008 |title=58 Festnahmen bei TeBe gegen BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/ausschreitungen-58-festnahmen-bei-tebe-gegen-bfc-dynamo/1391156.html |language=German |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=13 October 2023}} ===Contemporary supporter scene=== The contemporary supporter scene of BFC Dynamo contains various categories of supporters, ranging from older supporters to younger ultras. Older supporters constitute an essential part of the supporter scene. Many are active in the supporter group 79er.{{cite news |last=Raack |first=Axel |date=11 February 2011 |title='Stadtderby mit Schwachköpfen' |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/stadtderby-mit-schwachk%C3%B6pfen/574982 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=29 November 2020}} The supporter scene played an important part in saving the club from [[bankruptcy]] in 2001. Supporters organized collections, made donations, threw parties and travelled as far as [[Austria]] and [[Switzerland]] to convince creditors to accept smaller pay-offs in order to save the cub. The insolvency crisis remains a defining moment for older supporters. For a long time, the supporter scene arranged an annual Mike-Polley-Gedenkturnier, which was a football fan tournament in memory of Mike Polley.{{cite news |date=3 November 2018 |title=Vor 28 Jahren wurde Mike Polley erschossen |url=https://www.faszination-fankurve.de/index.php?head=Vor-28-Jahren-wurde-Mike-Polley-erschossen&folder=sites&site=news_detail&news_id=19244 |language=de |newspaper=Faszination Fankurve |location=[[Brühl (Rhineland)|Brühl]] |publisher=Faszination Fankurve, Sole trader: Johannes Mäling |access-date=24 May 2020}} A march in memory of Polley in [[Leipzig]] in 2018 was attended by 850 supporters of BFC Dynamo.{{cite news |date=15 April 2018 |title=850 BFC-Fans gedenken Mike Polley |url=https://www.welt.de/regionales/berlin/article175469768/850-BFC-Fans-gedenken-Mike-Polley.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=23 May 2020}} New groups of younger [[Ultras|ultra]]-oriented supporters have emerged since the 2000s. Supporter group Fraktion H was founded in 2006 by younger supporters who wanted to create more atmosphere in the stadium. A minor ultras scene then emerged with the founding of Ultras BFC in 2011. The ultras of BFC Dynamo have initiated campaigns such as "[[Political colour#Brown|Brown]] is not Claret" and have also engaged in football tournaments for [[refugee]]s.{{cite news |last=Scwermer |first=Alina |date=25 May 2016 |title=Rassismus beim BFC Dynamo: Ultralangsam aus der rechten Ecke |url=https://taz.de/Rassismus-beim-BFC-Dynamo/!5348199/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=24 May 2020}} The club has encouraged the new groups of younger supporters and club management has taken a stand against [[racism]] and [[right-wing extremism]].{{cite news |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=26 September 2012 |title=Willmanns Kolumne: Arthur aus Marzahn -|url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/willmanns-kolumne-arthur-aus-marzahn/7185612.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=24 May 2020}}{{cite news |last=Bock |first=Andreas |date=31 March 2015 |title='Das hat eine neue Qualität' |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/das-hat-eine-neue-qualit%C3%A4t/587703 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=24 May 2020}} The statutes of BFC Dynamo as of 2024 state that the club "feels strongly committed to the idea of [[anti-racism]]".{{cite web |url=https://bfc.com/pdf/Satzung_BFC_Dynamo.pdf |title=Satzung des BFC DYNAMO e.V. (Stand 29.08.2018) - § 2 Zweck und Aufgabe des Vereins |website=bfc.com |location=[[Berlin]] |publisher=Berliner Fussball Club Dynamo e.V. |language=de |access-date=4 August 2024 |quote=3. Zweck des Vereins sind die körperliche Ertüchtigung und sportliche Forderung seiner Mitglieder. die Pflege von Sportgemeinschaft sowie die Beaufsichtigung und Anlei- tung. insbesondere der Jugend, bei sportlichen Übungen. Der Verein ist politisch und weltanschaulich neutral. Er fühlt sich in hohem Maße dem Gedanken des Fair Play und des Antirassismus verbunden.}} BFC Dynamo engages in active fan work and has taken measures to control violent elements, to exclude known violators and to distance itself from radical supporters.{{cite news |last=Raack |first=Axel |date=2 August 2011 |title='Ich schäme mich für unsere Fans' |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/ich-sch%C3%A4me-mich-f%C3%BCr-unsere-fans/576540 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=29 November 2020}}{{cite news |last=Willmann |first=Frank |date=12 November 2014 |title=Willmanns Kolumne: Gästefazit: Nachttischlampen und dürre Weiber |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/gastefazit-nachttischlampen-und-durre-weiber-3598660.html |language=German |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=9 August 2024 |quote=Der BFC ist intern gegen das Mitglied des Fanclubs "Black Boys Dynamo" vorgegangen, die Fanvereinigung hat sich öffentlich distanziert, ein positives Zeichen des Clubs und seines Fanbeauftragten.}} Far-right symbols and slogans are not tolerated by the club. The Chairman of the Economic Council Peter Meyer stated publicly in connection with a friendly match against [[Hertha BSC]] in 2007 that the club did not want people who cannot follow the rules and that "anyone who shouts nazi slogans will be thrown out of the stadium". A large number of stadium bans has been issued by the club since the 2000s.{{cite news |date=21 August 2007 |title=BFC DYNAMO: Nach dem drohenden Spielverbot schlägt Fan-Beauftragter Rainer Lüdtke im B.Z.-Interview Alarm |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/bfc-dynamo-nach-dem-drohenden-spielverbot-schlaegt-fan-beauftragter-rainer-luedtke-im-b-z-interview-alarm |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=24 May 2020}} No riots has occurred since 2011. BFC Dynamo is affiliated with Fanprojekt Berlin. The contemporary supporter scene of BFC Dynamo scene includes groups such as 79er, Mythos BFC, Fraktion H, Piefkes, Riot Sport, Black Boys Dynamo, Bärenbande, Gegengerade, Hipstercrew, Sektion Süddeutschland, Banda Invicta and Kollektiv Brandenburg.{{cite news |date=15 August 2018 |title=BFC Dynamo-Fans treffen sich am Alexanderplatz |url=https://archiv.faszination-fankurve.de/index.php?head=BFC-Dynamo-Fans-treffen-sich-am-Alexanderplatz&folder=sites&site=news_detail&news_id=18718 |language=de |newspaper=Faszination Fankurve |location=[[Brühl (Rhineland)|Brühl]] |publisher=Faszination Fankurve, Sole trader: Johannes Mäling |access-date=24 May 2020}}{{cite book |last=Glaser |first=Joakim |date=2015 |title=Fotboll från Mielke till Merkel – Kontinuitet, brott och förändring i supporterkultur i östra Tyskland |trans-title=Football from Mielke to Merkel |edition=1st |language=sv |location=[[Malmö]] |publisher=Arx Förlag AB |page=98 |isbn=978-91-87043-61-1}}{{cite news |author= |date=29 July 2023 |title="Auf in ein neues Kapitel!": Neue aktive Gruppe beim BFC Dynamo |url=https://www.faszination-fankurve.de/news/61146/auf-in-ein-neues-kapitel-neue-aktive-gruppe-beim-bfc-dynamo |language=German |newspaper=Faszination Fankurve |location=[[Brühl (Rhineland)|Brühl]] |publisher=Faszination Fankurve, Sole trader: Johannes Mäling |access-date=2 August 2023}} Gegengerade is a [[Left-wing politics|left wing-oriented]] supporter group. A number of supporters of FC Berlin were members of the "[[Anti-fascism|Anti-Fascist]] Football Fan Initiative" (AFFI) back in 1993.{{cite news |last=Rada |first=Uwe |date=4 January 1993 |title="Lust und Energie" statt Rassismus" |url=https://taz.de/quotLust-und-Energiequot-statt-Rassismus/!1637017/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=22 April 2023}}{{cite news |last=Busch |first=Ralf |date=16 January 1993 |title=Fußballfans |url=https://taz.de/Fussballfans/!1634921/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=22 April 2023}}{{cite news |author= |date=10 December 1993 |title=AFFIS auf? |url=https://www.nd-aktuell.de/artikel/457877.affis-auf.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=2 October 2023}}{{cite news |last=Krauss |first=Martin |date=11 April 1994 |title=Fußball nicht den Rechten überlassen |url=https://taz.de/!1568068/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=2 October 2023}} Supporters of BFC Dynamo have occasionally displayed a banner in the stadium that reads "There Is A Light That Never Goes Out", with a reference to 1980s British cult band [[The Smiths]].{{cite news |last=Thiede |first=Jens Patrick |date=3 October 2008 |title=Stasi-nazi-ossi-hooligan-image på prøve: Dynamos evige arv og tavse transformation |url=https://www.tipsbladet.dk/artikel/stasi-nazi-ossi-hooligan-image-paa-proeve-dynamos-evige-arv-og-tavse-transformation |work=[[Tipsbladet]] |language=da |location=[[Copenhagen]] |publisher=Tipsbladet ApS |access-date=6 February 2023}}{{cite news |last=Sweetman |first=Jacob |date=15 June 2011 |title=Jacob Sweetman: That joke isn't funny anymore |url=https://www.the-berliner.com/berlin/that-joke-isnt-funny-anymore/ |work=[[The Berliner (magazine)|The Berliner]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Tip Berlin Media Group GmbH |access-date=24 September 2024 }} The BFC Dynamo supporter scene traditionally organizes an annual fan tournament in the Sportforum Hohenschönhausen. The ninth edition in May 2024 was won by a multicultural team with players from [[Syria]], [[Palestine (region)|Palestine]], [[Asia]] and Germany, who had been invited to the tournament by the supporter scene.{{cite web |url=https://www.bfc.com/news.php?id=2676&cat_id=3#ontitle |title=Herrentag: Multikulturelle Auswahl gewinnt traditionelles Fanturnier des BFC Dynamo |author= |date=10 May 2024 |website=bfc.com |location=[[Berlin]] |publisher=Berliner Fussballclub Dynamo e.V. |language=de |access-date=10 May 2024}} One of the most well-known books in Germany about the supporter scene of BFC Dynamo is "Der BFC war schuld am Mauerbau" by author and BFC Dynamo fan Andreas Gläser ([[:de:Andreas Gläser|de]]).{{cite book |last1=Tomilson |last2=Young |first1=Alan |first2=Christopher |date=2006 |title=German Football: History, Culture, Society |location=[[Abingdon-on-Thames]] |edition=1st |publisher=Routledge, Taylor & Francis Group |page=39 |isbn=0-415-35195-2}} The book was first published in 2002 and describes the supporter scene from the late 1970s and forward. Gläser grew up in [[Prenzlauer Berg]] and became a supporter of BFC Dynamo in te 1970s.{{cite news |last=Gläser |first=Andreas |date=21 August 2005 |title=Willkommen in der Zone |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/willkommen-in-der-zone/635018.html |language=German |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=7 July 2021}} The book "Stadionpartisanen - Fans und Hooligans in der DDR", by authors Anne Hahn and Frank Willmann ([[:de:Frank Willmann (Autor)|de]]), first published in 2007, also contains extensive interviews with BFC Dynamo supporters from the late 1970s and forward. The book "Riot Boys!" by Jochen Schramm, published posthumously in 1995, depicts the supporter scene of BFC Dynamo in the early 1980s and contains stories of violent away trips.{{cite news |last=Vogelsang |first=Lucas |date=20 November 2008 |title=Ultras Kutten Hooligans |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/ultras-kutten-hooligans/371851 |language=German |work=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=26 September 2021}}{{cite web |url=https://www.buchfreund.de/de/d/e/9783930559220/riot-boys?bookId=99196637 |title=Riot Boys! |author= |date=n.d. |website=buchfreund.de |location=[[Würzburg]] |publisher=w+h GmbH |language=de |access-date=1 October 2023}} BFC Dynamo, its reputation and supporter scene, was the theme of stage play "Dynamoland" by Gudrun Herrbold. The play was set up in 2007 and involved young football players from BFC Dynamo as well as Andreas Gläser and Sven Friedrich, who is the owner of the clothing store Hoolywood and a BFC Dynamo fan.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=20 October 2007 |title=Nazikinder! Stasi-Schweine! |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/nazikinder-stasi-schweine/361335 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=8 August 2020}} {{cite news |last=Lang-Lendorff |first=Antje |date=21 October 2007 |title=Montagsinterview: "Dieser Hass hat mich erschreckt" |url=https://taz.de/Montagsinterview/!5193048/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=26 April 2020}}{{cite news |last=Lüdeke |first=Steffen |date=23 October 2007 |title= Im Osten Stasi-Schweine, im Westen Neonazis |url= https://www.welt.de/sport/article1292128/Im-Osten-Stasi-Schweine-im-Westen-Neonazis.html |language=de |newspaper=Die Welt |location=Berlin |publisher=WeltN24 GmbH |access-date=15 March 2024}} {{cite news |last=Lorenz |first=Mirko |date=4 July 2016 |title=Wie die orangene Bomberjacke ihr Nazi-Image ablegte |url=https://www.vice.com/de/article/wie-die-orangene-bomberjacke-ihr-nazi-image/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Vice Media|Vice]] |location=Berlin |publisher=VICE Media GmbH |access-date=8 July 2020}} In an interview with football magazine ''11 Freunde'' about the play, Friedrich stated that he doesn't want to have anything to do with Nazis. Gläser clarified that they both come from the "alternative spectrum".{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=20 October 2007 |title=Nazikinder! Sasi-Schweine! |url=https://11freunde.de/artikel/nazikinder-stasi-schweine/361335 |language=de |newspaper=[[11 Freunde]] |location=Berlin |publisher=11FREUNDE Verlag GmbH & Co. KG |access-date=15 March 2024 |quote= Mit Nazis will er nichts zu tun haben, sagt er. "Der kommt eher, so wie ich, aus dem alternativen Spektrum", sagt Schriftsteller Gläser, der seinen Klub gern mit einer "Indierockband" vergleicht. Auch das ist der BFC.}} The clothing store Hoolywood on [[Schönhauser Allee]] in Prenzlauer Berg was founded at the beginning of the 1990s and became a store for [[Left-wing politics|left-wing]] subculture.{{cite news |last=Meier |first=Jule |date=29 December 2024 |title=Prenzlauer Berg: »Hoolywood« macht zu |url=https://www.nd-aktuell.de/artikel/1187832.linke-subkultur-prenzlauer-berg-hoolywood-macht-zu.html |language=German |work=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=nd.Genossenschaft eG |access-date=17 February 2025 }} The owner of the store, Sven Friedrich, was involved in the supporter scene of BFC Dynamo in the East German era. The store has been an advertising partner of BFC Dynamo.{{cite news |last=Rüttenauer |first=Andreas |date=25 May 2004 |title=Der schöne Schein von Hoolywood |url=https://taz.de/!748613/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=14 October 2021}} Hollywood closed its store in Prenzlauer Berg in 2025, but continues its business online. German [[Rap (music)|rap]] musician Joe Rilla ([[:de:Hagen Stoll|de]]) has dedicated a song to BFC Dynamo. The song is called "Heb die Faust Hoch (BFC Dynamo Straßenhymne)" and was released in 2008. Joe Rilla comes from the locality of [[Marzahn]] and has a background in the 1990s BFC Dynamo hooligan scene.{{cite news |last=Zinsmaier |first=Markus |date=26 February 2008 |title=Der Schläger greift zum Stuhlbein |url=https://www.zeit.de/online/2008/09/echolot |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Zeit]] |location=Hamburg |publisher=Zeit Online GmbH |access-date=25 January 2022}} ===Rivalries=== V. |- !colspan="2"|Presidium |- ! scope="col" | Member ! scope="col" | Position |- |Norbert Uhlig |President |- | {{nowrap|Karsten Valentin}} |Vice President |- |Sven Radicke |Treasurer |- !colspan="2"|Economic Council |- ! scope="col" | Member ! scope="col" | Position |- |Peter Meyer |[[Chairman]] |- |Falk Stoltmann |Member |- |Dennis Wisbar |Member |- |Jens Redlich |Member |- !colspan="2"|Other officials |- ! scope="col" | Name ! scope="col" | Position |- |[[Enis Alushi]] |[[Sporting director|Sporting Managing Director]] |- |Tobias Bluhm |Head of youth department |- |Malik Polte |Marketing and finances at youth department |- |[[Jörn Lenz]] |Head of Kita-Proekt |- |Rainer Lüdtke |Fan representative |- |Gilbert "Lennon" Lubinsky |Tradition representative |- |Andreas Utzki |{{nowrap|Representative for fans with [[Disability|disabilities]]}} |- |Jenny Hopp |{{nowrap|Responsible for children and youths}} |- |Sven Schmieglitz |{{nowrap|Responsible for children and youths}} |- |Mike Fidorra |Security officer |- |Martin Richter |Spokesperson |- |Patrick Skrzipek |Club photographer |} ===Presidential history=== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;" |- !{{abbr|No.|number}} !Name !Period !class="unsortable"|Notes |- |align=left|1 |align=left|{{flagicon|GDR}} Manfred Kirste |align=left|1966–1988 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Manfred Kirste was the first chairman and is still the longest serving chairperson or president in club history. He served as chairman from 15 January 1966 to 30 August 1988.}} |- |align=left|2 |align=left|{{flagicon|GDR}} Herbert Krafft |align=left|1988–1990 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Served as chairman until 19 February 1990.{{cite magazine |date=27 February 1990 |title=Nicht nur Buchstabentausch |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1990_02_27.html |magazine=Neue Fußballwoche ([[:de:Sportverlag Berlin|de]]) |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag, Berlin ([[:de:Sportverlag Berlin|de]]) |volume=1990 |issue=9 |page=2 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=8 March 2021}}}} |- |align=left|3 |align=left|{{flagicon|GDR}} Jürgen Bogs |align=left|1990 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Became acting chairman after the dismissal of Herbert Krafft on 19 February 1990. Bogs served as acting chairman until 27 May 1990.}} |- |align=left|4 |align=left|{{flagicon|GDR}} Dr. Klaus Janz |align=left|1990 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Dr. Klaus Janz was elected as the club president on 28 May 1990.{{cite magazine |author= |date=6 June 1990 |title=Alles neu, Ende Mai |url=https://fcc-supporters.org/fuwo/files/FUWO%201990/FUWO%201990%2023.pdf |journal=Die neue Fußballwoche (FuWo) (De) |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag, Berlin |volume=1990 |issue=23 |page=2 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=2 September 2024 |quote=Freitag vergangener Woche wurde das Geheimnis gelüftet, Dynamo Dresden firmiert künftig unter dem neuen Namen 1. FC Dynamo Dresden. Der neugewälhte Vorstand, er trat erstmals am Dienstag vergagenger Woche zusammen, hat aus seinen Reihen auch den neuen Präsidenten gekürt, es ist der Geschäftsmann Rüdiger Ziegenbalg. En neues Präsidium wurde auch beim FC Berlin gewählt. Zum Präsidenten wurde der Staatsanwalt Klaus Janz berufen, dem Präsidium gehören weiter Dr. Wolfgang Höserich, Wolfgang Reusse und Geschäftsführer Jürgen Bogs.}} Janz served as president until 15 October 1990.{{cite news |author= |date=15 October 1990 |title="Geschäftsschädigung" |url=https://taz.de/!1748318/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=9 August 2022}} He asked to be relieved from the office due to professional stress as a lawyer. He continued as vice president.}} |- |align=left|5 |align=left|{{nowrap|{{flagicon|GER}} Dr. Wolfgang Hösrich}} |align=left|1990–1994 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Dr. Wolfgang Hösrich became president on 15 October 1990. He had a background as a club doctor for SC Dynamo Berlin and BFC Dynamo. Hösrich had previously served as vice president.{{cite news |date=15 October 1990 |title=_R |url=https://www.neues-deutschland.de/artikel/281111.r.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Neues Deutschland]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Neues Deutschland Druckerei und Verlag GmbH |access-date=8 March 2021}}{{cite magazine |date=16 October 1990 |title=Neuer Präsident |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1990_10_15.html |magazine=Neue Fußballwoche ([[:de:Sportverlag Berlin|de]]) |language=de |location=Berlin |publisher=Sportverlag GmbH ([[:de:Sportverlag Berlin|de]]) |volume=1990 |issue=42 |page=7 |issn=0323-8407 |access-date=20 August 2021}} The presidium under Dr. Wolfgang Hösrich was replaced by a new presidium under Eberhard Landmann on 20 May 1994.{{cite news |date=4 June 1994 |title=FCB-Präsidien bleiben im Streit |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1994_06_04.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=7 March 2021}}{{cite news |date=29 June 1994 |title=FCB: Weiter Streit in der Chefetage |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1994_06_29.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=7 March 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=146}}}} |- |align=left|6 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Eberhard Landmann |align=left|1994–1995 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Eberhard Landmann became president on 20 May 1994.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=146}} Landmann was a former insurance salesman. He only served as president for nine months.{{cite news |last=Burghause |first=Hans Günter |date=13 February 1995 |title=Klaus Bittroff heißt der neue Kopf |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1995_02_13_2.html |language=German |newspaper=Kicker |location=Nuremberg |publisher=Olympia Verlag GmbH |access-date=7 March 2021}}}} |- |align=left|7 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Klaus Bittroff |align=left|1995 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Klaus Bittroff was elected as the new president on 10 February 1995 with the votes 73-11. Volkmar Wanski was elected as one of two [[vice president]]s alongside re-elected Lutz Hoff.{{cite news |date=13 February 1995 |title=Klare Mehrheit für Bittroff: FC Berlin wählte am Freitag neuen Vorstand |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/1995_02_13.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=7 March 2021}}{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=146}} Bittroff had previously served as president of [[Wacker 04 Berlin]] from 1984 to 1991.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=146}} }} |- |align=left|8 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Volkmar Wanski |align=left|1995–2000 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Became new president after the resignation of Klaus Bittroff on 14 September 1995.{{sfn|Karas|2022|p=147}} Wanski resigned on 29 June 2000.{{cite news |last=Kepler |first=Wolf |date=3 July 2000 |title=Geldgeber LIPRO will die Macht |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2000_07_03.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=24 August 2021}}{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=10 July 2000 |title=Ronaldos Partner stürmt für den BFC – Oberliga-Saisonstart am 6. August in Schönberg |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2000_07_10.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]]}}}} |- |align=left|9 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Hans Reker |align=left|2000 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Sporting director Hans Reker was appointed acting president by the Economic Council following the resignation of Volkmar Wanski on 29 June 2000. Reker had held the position of vice president in the presidium of Volkmar Wanski. Karin Halsch took office as new president on 27 September 2000.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=28 September 2000 |title=Was glauben Sie – kann das gut gehen? – Der Ober-Dynamo ist jetzt eine Frau , BFC-Paukenschlag! |url=https://www.bz-berlin.de/artikel-archiv/was-glauben-sie-kann-das-gut-gehen |language=de |newspaper=[[B.Z. (newspaper)|B.Z.]] |location=Berlin |publisher=B.Z. Ullstein GmbH |access-date=29 August 2020}}}} |- |align=left|10 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Karin Halsch |align=left|2000–2001 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Karin Halsch was known as Karin Seidel-Kalmutzki during the era. Served from 27 September 2000 to 25 June 2001.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=26 June 2001 |title=Wildwest / Präsidentin Seidel-Kalmutzki tritt zurück, vier Millionen Mark Schulden drücken |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_06_26.html |language=de |newspaper=Der Tagesspiegel |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH}}}} |- |align=left|11 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Hans Reker |align=left|2001 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Sporting director Hans Reker served as acting president following the resignation of Karin Halsch on 25 June 2001. Hans Reker held the position of vice president in the presidium of Karin Halsch.{{cite news |date=25 January 2001 |title=Berliner Sport: Fahrverbote für Zwiener und Hagen und mehr |url=https://www.tagesspiegel.de/sport/berliner-sport-fahrverbote-fuer-zwiener-und-hagen-und-mehr/197030.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=30 August 2020}}{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Matthias |date=27 June 2001 |title=Nach dem Rücktritt der Präsidentin munkelt man beim BFC Dynamo von dubiosen Konten: Rette sich, wer kann |url=https://www.berliner-zeitung.de/nach-dem-ruecktritt-der-praesidentin-munkelt-man-beim-bfc-dynamo-von-dubiosen-konten-rette-sich-wer-kann-li.21053?pid=true |language=de |work=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=24 August 2021}} He resigned together with the entire [[presidium]] on 30 October 2001.{{cite news |last=Höfgen |first=Ingmar |date=31 October 2001 |title=Dynamo: Präsidium trat zurück |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_10_31.html |language=de |newspaper=[[Berliner Kurier]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |quote=Bis zur Mitgliederversammlung am 26.11. werden Rayk Bernt, André Sommer und Holger Zimmermann die Geschäfte führen.}} An emergency board formed by André Sommer, Rayk Bernt and press spokesman Holger Zimmermann took office. The emergency board was meant to serve until the extraordinary general meeting on 26 November 2001.{{cite news |last=Görke |first=André |date=1 November 2001 |title=Rocker statt Stasi |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2001_11_01.html |language=de |work=[[Der Tagesspiegel]] |location=Berlin |publisher=Verlag Der Tagesspiegel GmbH |access-date=3 October 2021}}}} |- |align=left|- |align=left|''Office vacant'' |align=left|2001–2002 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|An emergency board formed by André Sommer, Rayk Bernt and press spokesman Holger Zimmermann took office on 1 November 2001. No new president was appointed for the transitional board. Zimmerman soon resigned on 23 November 2001. A new presidium was due to be elected on the extraordinary general meeting on 26 November 2001. However, the meeting agenda was changed with the votes 87 to 59 at the insistence of the emergency board. The meeting was converted into an information event and new elections were postponed.{{cite news |last=Wolf |first=Mattias |date=28 November 2001 |title=Angst hinter verschlossenen Türen |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2002_01_18.html |language=German |newspaper=[[Berliner Zeitung]] |publisher=Berliner Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=3 August 2021}} Club members collected signatures for the election of a new presidium. But the signatures would be ignored by Sommer and Bernt.{{cite news |last1=Holzapfel |last2=Harbeck |first1=Sebastian |first2=Julia |date=4 May 2002 |title=Von der Nische ins Abseits |url=https://taz.de/!1112008/ |language=de |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=27 May 2023}} The Sommer and Bernt presidium was finally overthrown by supporters and the former coach of the women's team Volkmar Lucius after an application to the Charlottenburg district court. The Charlottenburg district court appointed Lucius as emergency board member. A new presidium was elected on the extraordinary general meeting on 31 May 2002.}} |- |align=left|12 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Mike Peters |align=left|{{nowrap|2002–2004}} |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Elected on an extraordinary general meeting on 31 May 2002.}} |- |align=left|13 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Mario Weinkauf |align=left|2004–2007 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Mario Weinkauf was elected president on 18 June 2004.{{cite news |last=Metzel |first=Lajos |date=21 June 2004 |title= Faustdicke Überraschung |url=https://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2004_06_21.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=15 December 2021}} He resigned on a meeting with the presidium on 22 June 2007. He was then dismissed in a vote of no-confidence on an extra-ordinary general meeting on 23 June 2007. Mario Weinkauf was succeeded by Volkmar Wanski as interim president. Volkmar Wanski had been co-opted into the presidium and elected as the provisional successor to Mario Weinkauf on the meeting with the presidium on 22 June 2007. However the interim presidency of Volkmar Wanski was controversial. It was put into question whether his election was compliant with club statutes. Volkmar Wanski resigned after only six days.{{cite news |last=Schulz |first=Jürgen |date=25 June 2006 |title=Showdown bei Dynamo |url=https://taz.de/!265899/ |language=German |newspaper=[[Die Tageszeitung]] |publisher=taz Verlags u. Vertriebs GmbH |location=Berlin |access-date=17 January 2021}}{{cite news |last=Koch |first=Matthias |date=2 July 2007 |title=300.000 auf Wanderschaft - Dynamos Palastrevolution mit Konsequenzen |url=http://home.snafu.de/erich.mielke/HTMLPresse/2007_07_02.html |language=de |newspaper=Fußball-Woche ([[:de:Fußball-Woche|de]]) |location=Berlin |publisher=Fußball-Woche Verlags GmbH |access-date=17 January 2021}}}} |- |align=left|14 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Frank Berton |align=left|2007–2008 |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Frank Bertron was launched as interim president by the new Economic Council under Peter Meyer. He was presented as new president on 28 June 2007.}} |- |align=left|15 |align=left|{{flagicon|GER}} Norbert Uhlig |align=left|2008– |align=left|{{refn|group=nb|Norbert Uhlig is the second longest serving president after Manfred Kirste. management and organisation |- |{{flagicon|GER}} [[Jörn Lenz]] |Team manager |- |{{flagicon|GER}} Stefan Malchow |Kit manager |- |} ===Coach history=== [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1987-0717-035, BFC Dynamo, Trainer Jürgen Bogs.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Jürgen Bogs was coach from 1977 to 1989, 1990 to 1993 and then from 1999 to 2001, and led BFC Dynamo to ten consecutive East German championships.]] SC Dynamo Berlin had six different coaches until the founding of BFC Dynamo in 1966. The first coach was Helmut Petzold, who was delegated along with the team of Dynamo Dresden to Dynamo Berlin and took office on 21 November 1954. Other coaches of Dynamo Berlin were Istvan Orczifalvi, Fritz Bachmann, [[János Gyarmati]], [[Fritz Gödicke]] and Karl Schäffner. Fritz Bachmann served as coach of Dynamo Berlin during the successful 1959 season. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;" |- !{{abbr|No.|number}} !Coach !Period !class="unsortable"|Notes |- |title=Neuer Trainer: BFC Dynamo setzt auf seinen Co-Chef |url=https://www.berliner-kurier.de/fussball/bfc_dynamo/neuer-trainer-bfc-dynamo-setzt-auf-seinen-co-chef-li.2250713 |language=de Verlag GmbH |location=[[Berlin]] |access-date=27 September 2024 }} |align=left|2024- |align=left| |- |} {{notelist|refs= {{efn|name=fn1|Interim coach}} {{efn|name=fn2|[[Co-coaching|Co-coach]]}} {{efn|name=fn3|Player-coach}} }} ==Honours== [[File:Bundesarchiv Bild 183-1989-0805-025, Supercup SG Dynamo Dresden - BFC Dynamo 1-4.jpg|thumb|The team celebrating the victory in the 1989 DFV-Supercup together with fans. [[Heiko Bonan]] is holding the trophy.]] BFC Dynamo was the most successful club in the [[DDR-Oberliga]]. The club won ten consecutive [[List of East German football champions|championships]], which is a feat no other team in East Germany has matched. The DDR-Oberliga was rebranded as the [[NOFV-Oberliga]] from the 1990–91 season. The league was then replaced by the [[Bundesliga]] as the highest competition from the 1991–92 season, as East Germany had joined [[West Germany]] to form the reunited [[Germany]]. ===Domestic=== * '''[[List of East German football champions|East German Champions]]''' ** '''Winners:''' (10) [[DDR-Oberliga 1978-79|1978–79]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1979-80|1979–80]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1980-81|1980–81]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1981-82|1981–82]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1982-83|1982–83]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1983-84|1983–84]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1984-85|1984–85]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1985-86|1985–86]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1986-87|1986–87]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1987-88|1987–88]] '''(record)''' **''Runners-up:'' (4) [[1960 DDR-Oberliga|1960]],{{efn|name=fn1}} [[DDR-Oberliga 1971-72|1971–72]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1975-76|1975–76]], [[DDR-Oberliga 1988-89|1988–89]] * '''[[FDGB-Pokal]]''' ** '''Winners:''' (3) [[1959 FDGB-Pokal|1959]],{{efn|name=fn1}} [[1987–88 FDGB-Pokal|1987–88]], [[1988–89 FDGB-Pokal|1988–89]] ** ''Runners-up:'' (6) 1961–62,{{efn|name=fn1}} 1970–71, [[1978–79 FDGB-Pokal|1978–79]], 1981–82, 1983–84, [[1984–85 FDGB-Pokal|1984–85]] * '''[[DFV-Supercup]]''' ** '''Winners:''' 1989 * '''Fuwo-Pokal''' ([[:de:Fuwo-Pokal|de]]) **''Runners-up:'' 1972{{efn|name=fn2}} * '''[[DDR-Liga]]''' (II) ** '''Winners:''' 1957,{{efn|name=fn1}} * '''[[DDR-Liga]] Nord''' (II) ** '''Winners:''' 1967–68 * '''[[DDR-Liga]] B''' (II) ** '''Winners:''' 1971–72{{efn|name=fn3}} ** ''Runners-up:'' 1974–75{{efn|name=fn3}} * '''[[DDR-Liga]] A''' (II) ** '''Winners:''' 1984–85{{efn|name=fn3}} {{notelist|refs= {{efn|name=fn1|Won by [[SC Dynamo Berlin]].}} {{efn|name=fn2|The Fuwo-Pokal was only arranged in 1972. All teams in the [[1971-72 DDR-Oberliga]] took part in the cup.}} {{efn|name=fn3|Won by BFC Dynamo II}} }} ===International=== * '''[[European Cup]]''' ** ''Quarter-finals'': [[1979–80 European Cup|1979–80]], [[1983–84 European Cup|1983–84]] * '''[[UEFA Cup Winners' Cup|European Cup Winners' Cup]]''' ** ''Semi-finals'': [[1971–72 European Cup Winners' Cup|1971–72]] ===Double=== * '''[[Double (association football)#East Germany .28defunct.29|DDR-Oberliga and FDGB-Pokal]]''' ** '''Winners:''' 1988 ===Regional=== * '''Bezirksliga Berlin''' (III) ** '''Winners:''' 1966–67,{{efn|name=fn1}} 1983–84{{efn|name=fn1}} *'''[[Regionalliga Nordost]]''' (IV) ** '''Winners:''' [[2021–22 Regionalliga#Regionalliga Nordost|2021–22]] *'''[[NOFV-Oberliga Nord]]''' (III–V) ** '''Winners:''' (3) [[1991–92 NOFV-Oberliga|1991–92]], [[2000–01 NOFV-Oberliga|2000–01]], [[2013–14 NOFV-Oberliga|2013–14]] ** ''Runners-up:'' [[2008–09 NOFV-Oberliga|2008–09]], [[2009–10 NOFV-Oberliga|2009–10]] *'''[[Berlin-Liga]]''' (V) ** '''Winners:''' 2003–04 *'''[[Berlin Cup]]''' (III–VII) ** '''Winners:''' (8) 1998–99, 2010–11, 2012–13, [[2014–15 Verbandspokal|2014–15]], [[2016–17 Verbandspokal|2016–17]], [[2017–18 Verbandspokal|2017–18]], [[2020–21 Verbandspokal|2020–21]], [[2024–25 Verbandspokal|2024–25]] ** ''Runners-up:'' 1999–00, 2009–10 {{notelist|refs= {{efn|name=fn1|Won by BFC Dynamo II}} }} ==Seasons== {{see also|List of Berliner FC Dynamo seasons}} ==European competitions== {| class="wikitable" !
