A reciprocal vaccination requirement for the cross-border trucking industry has been in place since January 22, when the United States enforced theirs, affecting unvaccinated American truckers returning to the United States. Unvaccinated Canadian truckers are denied entry to the United States. In a February 11 update, federal Health Minister [[Jean-Yves Duclos]] alongside Dr. Tam, said that the worst was behind us, most new cases were community-driven, and cross-border restrictions were less effective. Duclos announced an update on health restrictions in the following week.{{cite news |last1=Lavery |first1=Irelyne |last2=Connolly |first2=Amanda |date=2022-02-11 |title=Ottawa vows update on COVID-19 border rules next week: 'The worst is behind us' |url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8609898/covid-update-canada-top-health-officials/ |access-date=2022-02-11 |work=Global News}} Of the total number of 160,000 licensed truck drivers who serve cross-border routes, 120,000 are Canadian and 40,000 are American. Of the 120,000 Canadian truckers, [[Canadian Trucking Alliance]] estimates that 85 per cent are already vaccinated against COVID-19,{{cite news|last=Reynolds|first=Christopher|date=2022-01-25|title=GoFundMe withholding $4.7 million from trucker convoy until plan presented|newspaper=Toronto Star|url=https://www.thestar.com/business/2022/01/25/trucker-convoys-4-million-in-fundraising-frozen-by-gofundme.html|access-date=2022-01-29}} and that "as many as 12,000 to 16,000" Canadian truckers may not be able to work those cross-border routes because they are unvaccinated. In total, including the potential American cross-border truckers, these mandates could result in 26,000 of the 160,000 drivers in both countries who have been regularly running cross-border routes, losing that privilege. ==Convoy to Ottawa== {{See also|Timeline of the Canada convoy protest}} The first convoy departed [[Prince Rupert, British Columbia|Prince Rupert]] on January 22, arriving in [[Prince George, British Columbia|Prince George]] in the evening. The following day, another convoy left from [[Delta, British Columbia|Delta]] with supporters gathering along [[British Columbia Highway 1|Highway 1]], the [[Trans-Canada Highway]].{{cite web|url=https://www.interior-news.com/news/video-crowd-of-supporters-greets-anti-vaccine-mandate-truck-convoy-as-it-leaves-vancouver-for-ottawa-2/|title=VIDEO: Crowd of supporters greets anti-vaccine-mandate truck convoy as it leaves Vancouver for Ottawa|last=Meissner|first=Deb|date=2022-01-23|work=[[Black Press|Smithers Interior News]]|access-date=2022-01-24}} On January 24, a convoy drove through [[Regina, Saskatchewan]], and was greeted by supporters.{{cite web|date=2022-01-25|title=GoFundMe confirms trucker freedom convoy funds being held until 'clear plan' is revealed|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8536937/trucker-freedom-convoy-gofundme-update/|access-date=2022-01-26|website=Global News|language=en-US}} According to [[Regina Police Service|police in Regina]], about 1,200 vehicles reached the city.{{cite news|last=Lambert|first=Steve|date=2022-01-25|title=Trucker convoy opposed to COVID-19 vaccine mandates gets big greeting in Winnipeg|language=en-CA|work=The Globe and Mail|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/canada/article-trucker-convoy-opposed-to-covid-19-vaccine-mandates-gets-big-greeting/|access-date=2022-01-27}} On January 25, another convoy passed through [[Kenora]], Ontario, where [[Ontario Provincial Police]] (OPP) in contact with the convoy stated that 200–300 vehicles would be passing through Kenora.{{cite news|last1=Haughton|first1=Jay|title=Freedom Convoy set to roll through Kenora Tuesday night|url=https://www.kenoraonline.com/articles/freedom-convoy-set-to-roll-through-kenora-tuesday-night|access-date=2022-01-26|work=Kenora Online|publisher=Golden West Broadcasting|date=2022-01-25|archive-date=2022-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220126001418/https://kenoraonline.com/articles/freedom-convoy-set-to-roll-through-kenora-tuesday-night|url-status=dead}} The convoys consisted of three main routes across Canada, which would converge for the Ottawa protest on the weekend.{{cite web|title='Freedom convoy' expected to roll through QEW in Hamilton on Thursday morning|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8540105/freedom-convoy-qew-hamilton/|access-date=2022-01-27|website=Global News|language=en-US}} The [[Ottawa Police Service]] estimated up to 2,000 demonstrators in the city on the weekend.{{cite news|last=Marfo|first=Dorcas|date=2022-01-26|title=The 'Freedom Rally' is rolling through Toronto on Thursday. Here's what you need to know|language=en-CA|work=The Toronto Star|url=https://www.thestar.com/news/gta/2022/01/26/the-freedom-rally-is-rolling-through-toronto-on-thursday-heres-what-you-need-to-know.html|access-date=2022-01-27|issn=0319-0781}} As of January 26, the OPP estimated approximately 400 vehicles had entered Ontario from the Manitoba border as part of the eastbound convoy.{{cite web|url=https://www.tbnewswatch.com/local-news/thunder-bay-police-caution-motorists-about-heavy-traffic-from-the-truckers-convoy-4995561|title=Thunder Bay Police caution motorists about heavy traffic from the truckers' convoy|website=TBNewsWatch.com|date=2022-01-26}} The [[Kingston Police]] estimated approximately 300 vehicles (17 full tractor-trailers, 104 tractors without trailers, 424 passenger vehicles and six RVs) to go through Kingston.{{cite web|date=2022-01-27|title=Truck convoy rolls into Kingston, Ont.|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/truck-convoy-rolls-into-kingston-ont/|access-date=2022-01-28|website=Ottawa|language=en}} [[File:Freedom Convoy in Halton region Jan 27.png|thumb|left|Part of the convoy moving through [[Regional Municipality of Halton|Halton Region]] in Ontario.]] On January 27, [[David Akin]], a reporter for ''[[Global News]]'', tweeted that a convoy that had arrived that day in [[Thunder Bay]] from [[Winnipeg]] consisted of 113 commercial vehicles and 276 personal vehicles, citing the OPP. Winter weather closed a portion of [[Ontario Highway 17|Highway 17]], (the main route of the Trans-Canada) in northern Ontario, causing the eastbound convoy to be divided. Some of the truckers continued on to [[Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario|Sault Ste. Marie]], while others diverted to [[Ontario Highway 11|Highway 11]] (a northern branch of the TCH) and drove toward [[Cochrane, Ontario|Cochrane]].{{cite web|title=Truck convoy arrives in Sault Ste. Marie|first=Mike|last=McDonald|date=2022-01-28|website=CTV News|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/northern-ontario/article/truck-convoy-arrives-in-sault-ste-marie/|access-date=2022-01-28}} Also on January 27, a fatal crash occurred near [[Chatham, Ontario]]. OPP officers later identified the convoy movement as a contributing factor to the incident.{{cite web|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/windsor/article/truck-convoy-a-contributing-factor-in-fatal-chatham-kent-collision-opp/|title=Truck convoy a 'contributing factor' in fatal Chatham-Kent collision: OPP|date=2022-02-21|website=Windsor}}{{cite web|url=https://chathamvoice.com/2022/02/21/freedom-convoy-a-factor-in-fatal-crash-opp/|title=Freedom convoy a factor in fatal crash: OPP|work=Chatham Voice|date=2022-02-21}}{{cite web|url=https://lfpress.com/news/local-news/truck-convoy-a-contributing-factor-in-fatal-401-crash-near-chatham-opp|title=Truck convoy a 'contributing factor' in fatal crash along Hwy. 401: Police|website=lfpress}} Truckers from the [[Maritimes|Maritime provinces]] planned to meet in [[Moncton]] before departing for Ottawa.{{cite web|last=Lothian|first=Robert|date=2022-01-25|title=Atlantic Canadian Truckers Join 'Freedom Convoy' On Thursday|url=https://www.country94.ca/2022/01/25/atlantic-canadian-truckers-join-freedom-convoy-on-thursday/|access-date=2022-01-26|website=Country 94|language=en}}{{cite news|title=P.E.I. trucker joining 'freedom convoy' to Ottawa, trucking association opposes protest|work=CBC News|date=2022-01-25|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/prince-edward-island/pei-truckers-convoy-ottawa-1.6327617|access-date=2022-01-26}} On the morning of January 27, supporters gathered in [[Enfield, Nova Scotia]], where fireworks were set off as 10 to 15 trucks departed for the New Brunswick border.{{cite news|title=Protesters against COVID-19 measures gather in Enfield to see off Ottawa-bound convoy|work=CBC News|date=2022-01-27|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/nova-scotia/freedom-convoy-nova-scotia-supporters-enfield-1.6329385|access-date=2022-01-28}} [[Royal Canadian Mounted Police|RCMP]] in PEI reported that approximately 70 trucks and supporters' vehicles crossed the [[Confederation Bridge]] into New Brunswick, but that most immediately turned around and returned to the island.{{cite news|title=Trucker convoy headed to Ottawa 'peaceful,' says RCMP|work=CBC News|date=2022-01-27|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/prince-edward-island/pei-rcmp-covid-19-trucker-convoy-1.6329540|access-date=2022-01-28}} About 24 trucks reportedly passed through [[Fredericton]] in the afternoon, bound for Ottawa.{{cite web|title=Some N.B. truckers join Ottawa-bound protest, but not with trucking group's blessing|work=CBC News|date=2022-01-27|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/new-brunswick/nb-truck-convoy-heading-to-ottawa-1.6329450|access-date=2022-01-28}} On January 27, one convoy group passed through the [[Greater Toronto Area]]. Hundreds of protesters gathered at highway overpasses in support of the convoy.{{cite web|last=Fox|first=Chris|date=2022-01-27|title=Trucker protest passes through GTA as crowds line route of 'Freedom Convoy'|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/toronto/article/trucker-protest-passes-through-gta-as-crowds-line-route-of-freedom-convoy/|access-date=2022-01-28|website=CTV News Toronto|language=en}} On January 28, a convoy was seen passing through [[Quebec]].{{cite web|title=Truckers Freedom Convoy passes through Quebec on Friday|url=https://montrealgazette.com/news/local-news/truckers-freedom-convoy-passes-through-quebec-on-friday|access-date=2022-01-28|website=montrealgazette|language=en-CA}} About 600 convoy vehicles were expected to stay the night in [[Arnprior]] before heading to [[Parliament Hill]] the following morning. On January 28, the province of [[Nova Scotia]] banned gatherings along highways, specifically on the Trans-Canada ([[Nova Scotia Highway 104|Highway 104]]) between the Nova Scotia and [[New Brunswick]] border, in relation to protests related to the freedom convoy.{{cite web|last=Sims|first=Harley|date=2022-01-28|title=Nova Scotia bans gatherings along highway in support of trucker freedom convoy|url=https://tnc.news/2022/01/28/nova-scotia-bans-gatherings-along-highway-in-support-of-trucker-freedom-convoy/|access-date=2022-01-29|language=en-US}}{{cite web|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/atlantic/article/new-order-bans-highway-border-blockades-in-nova-scotia/|title=New order bans highway border blockades in Nova Scotia|date=2022-01-28|website=Atlantic}} ==Ottawa protest== {{See also|Timeline of the Canada convoy protest}} ===Peak size of Parliament Hill demonstration=== According to the Ottawa Police Service, crowd estimates ranged from 5,000 to 18,000 protesters on January 29,{{cite news|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-ottawa-police-say-more-officers-will-be-deployed-downtown-as-thousands/|title=Ottawa police say more officers will be deployed downtown as thousands of protesters are expected this weekend|first1=Janice|last1=Dickson|first2=Marieke|last2=Walsh|newspaper=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-02-04|via=www.theglobeandmail.com}}{{cite web|url=https://www.nationalobserver.com/2022/02/02/news/trucker-protest-occupy-ottawa-options-police-chief|title=Ottawa's top cop says military may be needed to end trucker protest, turned "occupation"|first1=Laura|last1=Osman|last2=Ibrahim|first2=Erika|access-date=2022-02-02|date=2022-02-02|website=Canada's National Observer}} dwindling to approximately 3,000 demonstrators the following day.{{cite news|title=Trucks, crowds at Canada anti-vaccination protest exaggerated|first=Josh|last=Kelety|date=2022-01-31|work=AP News|url=https://apnews.com/article/fact-checking-423630924484|access-date=2022-02-09}} By February 1, they estimated as few as 250 protesters remained. On February 9, [[CNN]]'s [[Daniel Dale]] fact-checked numerous claims about the protests in general. Dale echoed the previous claims by police, adding that there was also an estimated 200 vehicles from the [[Atlantic provinces]].{{cite web|url=https://www.cnn.com/2022/02/09/politics/fact-check-canadian-convoy-protest-ottawa/index.html|title=Fact check: Debunking false claims about the Canadian convoy protests|author=Daniel Dale|website=CNN|date=2022-02-09}} Some Facebook posts claimed as many as 50,000 trucks would be participating in the convoys.{{cite web|last=Evon|first=Dan|date=2022-01-27|title=Did 50,000 'Freedom Convoy' Trucks Set Guinness World Record?|url=https://www.snopes.com/fact-check/freedom-convoy-guinness/|access-date=2022-01-28|website=Snopes.com|language=en-US}}{{cite web|last=Warmington|first=Joe|date=2022-01-26|title='Freedom' truckers may form world's longest convoy|work=torontosun |url=https://torontosun.com/news/local-news/warmington-freedom-truckers-may-form-worlds-longest-convoy|access-date=2022-01-28}}{{cite news|title=Fox News brought in Theo Fleury to warn viewers about Canada|url=https://dailyhive.com/vancouver/fox-news-theo-fleury-warn-viewers-canada|access-date=2022-01-29|work=Daily Hive Offside|date=2022-01-26}} On his blog, Fox News commentator [[Sean Hannity]] reported that the convoy was made up of 10,000 heavy trucks,{{cite web|last=Hannity|first=Sean|date=2022-01-27|title=Truck Yeah: Canada Forms Freedom Convoy of 10K Trucks to Protest Vax Mandates, 'Overreach is Over'|url=https://hannity.com/media-room/truck-yeah-canada-forms-freedom-convoy-of-10k-trucks-to-protest-vax-mandates-overreach-is-over/|access-date=2022-01-28|publisher=iHeartMedia}} and Joe Warmington of the ''Toronto Sun'' reported that the event may set a [[Guinness World Records|Guinness World Record]] for the largest truck convoy on record. These claims were grossly exaggerated.{{cite web|date=2022-01-26|title=Canada anti-vaccine mandate convoy did not set Guinness World Record|url=https://factcheck.afp.com/doc.afp.com.9X72HG|access-date=2022-01-28|website=AFP Fact Check|publisher=Agence France-Presse}} Claims that the truck convoy "made the ''[[Guinness Book of World Records]]''" are also false; the current record is 480 trucks, set in [[Cairo, Egypt]] in 2020, and the Freedom Convoy did not submit an application for an attempt at setting a new record. === Incidents and community impact in Ottawa === Various images of specific acts during January 29 received wide condemnation. Images of a Canadian flag marked with a [[swastika]] were seen, as was a [[Confederate flag]].{{cite news|title=Thousands in Ottawa protest COVID mandates, many rebuked|url=https://apnews.com/article/coronavirus-pandemic-health-business-justin-trudeau-canada-45778ef0bdca45501411fb21066c46a0|access-date=2022-01-29|work=Associated Press News|date=2022-01-29}} Key organizers denounced symbols of hate with one stating, "we are going to overcome this through love and light, not hate and division."{{Cite web |date=January 25, 2022 |title=Organizer behind anti-vaccine mandate convoy says it won't tolerate extremists as online rhetoric heats up |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/organizer-extremists-convoy-no-violence-1.6327650 |website=cbc.ca}} Additionally, [[Tamara Lich]], organizer of the national convoy stated, "If you see participants along the way that are misbehaving, acting aggressively in any way or inciting any type of violence or hatred, please take down the truck number and their licence plate number so that we can forward that to the police."{{Cite web |date=January 31, 2022 |title=Images, symbols of hate among truck convoy condemned, labelled deplorable by Manitoba premier, others |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/manitoba/manitoba-premier-heather-stefanson-condemns-hate-images-deplorable-1.6334461 |website=CBC News}} Some protesters were seen drinking and dancing on the [[Canadian Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] at the [[National War Memorial (Canada)|National War Memorial]]. Chief of the Defence Staff Gen. [[Wayne Eyre]] described it as a desecration, and the [[Royal Canadian Legion]] condemned the actions.{{cite news|author=|title=Defence chief criticizes protesters for dancing on National War Memorial|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/defence-chief-criticizes-protesters-for-dancing-on-national-war-memorial|access-date=2022-01-29|work=The Ottawa Citizen|date=2022-01-29|location=Ottawa ON}} A statue of cancer fundraiser [[Terry Fox]] was decorated with an upside-down Canadian flag and a protest sign. The [[Terry Fox Foundation]] said Fox "believed in science and gave his life to help others".{{cite news|last1=Crawford|first1=Tiffany|title=Mayor of Terry Fox's hometown in B.C. calls out vaccine mandate protesters for defacing statue|url=https://vancouversun.com/news/mayor-of-terry-foxs-hometown-in-b-c-calls-out-vaccine-mandate-protesters-for-defacing-statue|access-date=2022-01-29|work=Vancouver Sun|date=2022-01-29|location=Vancouver BC}}{{cite web|last=Gallant|first=Jacques|title=Terry Fox statue used in 'stunt' in Ottawa, sparking condemnation|website=Toronto Star|url=https://www.thestar.com/politics/federal/2022/01/29/terry-fox-statue-defaced-in-ottawa-sparking-condemnation.html|date=2022-01-29|access-date=2022-01-30}} The statue was cleaned later by other protesters.{{cite web|url=https://globalnews.ca/video/8582676/trucker-convoy-protesters-clean-up-terry-fox-statue-in-ottawa-following-outcry/|title=Trucker convoy: Protesters clean-up Terry Fox statue in Ottawa following outcry; Watch News Videos Online|website=Global News}} A local [[soup kitchen]], the [[Shepherds of Good Hope]], alleged that some protestors were harassing the shelter for free food.{{cite web|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/trucker-convoy-more-trucks-expected-on-saturday-traffic-impacts-expected-to-worsen|title=Trucker convoy: Police report no injuries, 'no incidents of violence' after first day of protest|website=ottawacitizen}} The kitchen alleged in a tweet that one resident was assaulted, and a security guard faced racial slurs and threats, though no charges were reported against these protestors.{{cite web|last=Massie|first=Graeme|date=2022-01-30|title=Soup kitchen says Freedom Convoy truckers 'harassing' staff for meals|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/convoy-truckers-canada-soup-kitchen-b2003765.html|access-date=2022-01-31|website=[[The Independent]]}} Its press release said trucks were towed after blocking its ambulance drop-off zone for around half a day. In contrast, many protesters organized food distribution, delivering pizzas and setting up communal meals for the homeless, with excess food donated to shelters.{{Cite web |date=February 7, 2022 |title=Did Freedom Convoy supporters steal food from the homeless? Alexa Lavoie investigates |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7OFyIR9K8DA}} Ottawa residents described being afraid and feeling trapped in their homes as the trucks spilled over from the Parliament Hill area into surrounding residential areas.{{cite web|last=Molina|first=Kimberley|date=2022-02-08|title=Fearful Ottawa residents flee downtown as protest drags on|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/downtown-residents-flee-home-protest-fears-1.6341652|access-date=2023-02-09|website=CBC News}}{{cite web|last=Bresge|first=Adina|date=2022-01-31|title=Calling the Ottawa protests 'peaceful' plays down non-violent dangers, critics say|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/canada/article-calling-the-ottawa-protests-peaceful-downplays-non-violent-dangers/|access-date=2023-02-09|website=The Globe and Mail}} Some Ottawa businesses near Parliament Hill closed around the time of the convoy's arrival out of an abundance of caution, while others closed later after staff reported being harassed or attacked by convoy participants for wearing a mask, or because of their race.{{cite web|last=Molina|first=Kimberley|date=2022-02-07|title=Centretown ice cream shop closes after worker reports assault on way to work|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/moo-shu-ice-cream-employee-assaulted-ottawa-1.6341207|access-date=2023-02-09|website=CBC News}}{{cite web|title='It's not safe': Ottawa residents say they've been accosted by 'Freedom Convoy'|url=https://www.nbcnews.com/news/-just-dont-feel-safe-ottawa-residents-describe-fears-trucker-protest-c-rcna15218|access-date=2023-02-10|website=NBC News|date=2022-02-11}} [[Ottawa Police Service|Ottawa police]] reported that they had received hundreds of calls for service from residents, made some arrests, and in partnership with by-law services had issued tickets to convoy participants for actions impacting residents such as excessive honking and use of fireworks, in addition to tickets for driving infractions.{{cite web|date=2022-02-08|title=Ottawa Police Service Makes 23 Arrests in Relation to Demonstration – 1,300 Tickets Issued|url=https://ottawapolice-icrt-ops1.esolg.ca/en/news/ottawa-police-service-makes-23-arrests-in-relation-to-demonstration-1_300-tickets-issued.aspx|access-date=2023-02-10|website=Ottawa Police Service}} “Evidence provided to the commission showed the noise levels outside their homes reached 100 decibels at times — about as loud as a lawn mower”, enough to cause a local resident to later testify at the ''Emergencies Act'' Inquiry that she had experienced an "assault on my hearing”.{{cite web|title=CityNews|website=CityNews|date=2022-10-14|url=https://ottawa.citynews.ca/2022/10/14/protest-convoy-truck-noise-caused-hearing-loss-woman-tells-emergencies-act-inquiry-5954848/|access-date=2023-08-24}}{{cite web|last1=Jordan|first1=Matthew|last2=Forde|first2=Sydney|title=Canadian trucker protests show how the loudest voices in the room distort democracy|website=The Conversation|date=2023-08-19|url=http://theconversation.com/canadian-trucker-protests-show-how-the-loudest-voices-in-the-room-distort-democracy-177022|access-date=2023-08-24}}{{cite web|last=Tunney|first=Catharine|date=2022-02-07|title=Court grants injunction to silence honking in downtown Ottawa for 10 days|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/injunction-ottawa-granted-1.6342468|access-date=2024-02-21|website=CBC News}} ===Ottawa protests and entrenchment=== [[File:Freedom Convoy protest 2 mosbo6.jpg|thumb|Protester vehicles lined up in the periphery of the National War Memorial on January 31]] The January 29 protest had a party-like atmosphere, with some handing out coffee and cookies and consuming beer and marijuana.{{cite web|date=2022-01-29|title=Updates: Officials condemn 'desecration' of monuments, hateful signs on display at trucker convoy protest|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-truck-freedom-convoy-ottawa-live-update/|access-date=2022-01-30|website=[[The Globe and Mail]]}} Protesters [[Barbecue|barbecued food]], played [[street hockey|hockey]], and set up [[Inflatable castle|bouncy castles]] for their children to play in.{{cite web|last=Laube|first=Aly|date=2022-02-05|title=Three strange things at the Ottawa "Freedom Convoy"|url=https://dailyhive.com/vancouver/ottawa-freedom-convoy-barbecues-bouncy|access-date=2022-02-17|website=[[Daily Hive]]}} Ottawa Police reported de-escalating multiple "high-risk situations," but made no arrests.{{cite news|last1=Somos|first1=Christy|title=Ottawa braces for day two of trucker protests|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/ottawa-braces-for-day-two-of-trucker-protests-1.5760424|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220130141207/https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/ottawa-braces-for-day-two-of-trucker-protests-1.5760424|url-status=dead|archive-date=January 30, 2022|access-date=2022-01-30|work=CTV News|date=2022-01-30|location=Ottawa ON}} [[File:Freedom Convoy protest mosbo6.jpg|thumb|left|Rideau Street in Ottawa on January 31]] During this first weekend of protest in Ottawa, the city's COVID-19 wastewater signal indicated a sharp increase in COVID-19 cases, suggesting a link between the gathering of protesters and the spread of COVID-19.{{cite news|date=2022-02-03|title=A sharp jump in Ottawa's COVID-19 wastewater index when trucker convoy arrives|work=National Post|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/covid-19-ontario-death-toll-up-by-75|access-date=2022-02-03}} A January 31 press conference was held at an undisclosed location, open only to invited right-wing outlets.{{cite news|first1=Catharine|last1=Tunney|first2=Christian|last2=Paas-Lang|title=Ottawa mayor would like protesters to move on, but organizers say they're not going anywhere|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/truck-convoy-protests-sunday-1.6332893|access-date=2022-01-31|work=CBC News|date=2022-01-30}} The House of Commons resumed on January 31, after the holiday break. On January 31, Ottawa Paramedics announced that protesters had thrown rocks at an ambulance over the course of the weekend and called paramedics racial slurs. Paramedics had treated 19 people over the course of the weekend, mostly due to alcohol-related intoxication.{{cite web|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/trucker-convoy-traffic-disruption-continue-downtown-as-mayor-urges-protesters-to-leave|title=Trucker convoy: 'It's time to get our city back' McKenney says; rocks hurled at ambulance|website=Ottawa Citizen}} Healthcare workers and patients reported that they had difficulty getting to hospitals because of jammed bridges. Some healthcare workers had to stay in hotel rooms near the hospitals. Many staff members from the [[Children's Hospital of Eastern Ontario]] (CHEO) felt besieged, and expressed concerns about swastikas and other symbols of hate displayed by protesters.{{cite web|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/ambulances-pelted-with-rocks-during-protest-health-workers-patients-face-added-stress-delays|title=Ambulances pelted with rocks during protest; health workers, patients face added stress, delays|website=Ottawa Citizen}} On the afternoon of January 31, Sloly said that "The situation of the demonstration has scaled down over the last 12 hours" and that "We want that trend to continue until this demonstration comes to a complete end. I cannot guarantee you that right now but I can guarantee that every effort at negotiation, coordination, de-escalation, has continued throughout the last four days and will continue until the complete end of this demonstration."{{cite web|last1=Raymond|first1=Ted|last2=Woods|first2=Michael|date=2022-02-01|title=What to expect from the 'Freedom Convoy' protest today|url=https://ottawa.ctvnews.ca/what-to-expect-from-the-freedom-convoy-protest-today-1.5762245|access-date=2022-02-01|website=CTV News Ottawa|archive-date=February 1, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220201090859/https://ottawa.ctvnews.ca/what-to-expect-from-the-freedom-convoy-protest-today-1.5762245|url-status=dead}} On February 1, large downtown Ottawa amenities like the [[Rideau Centre]], [[Ottawa City Hall]], and the [[National Arts Centre]] continued to stay closed due to security concerns. Ottawa police set up a hotline for hate-motivated crimes to be reported.{{cite web|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/trucker-convoy-shutdown-continues-for-fifth-day-shepherds-of-good-hope-overwhelmed-by-donations|title=Trucker convoy: Ford asks protesters to 'let the people of Ottawa live their lives'|website=ottawacitizen}} On the night of February 1, Ottawa Police said that about 250 protesters remained around and on Parliament Hill.{{cite web|last1=Raymond|first1=Ted|last2=Woods|first2=Michael|date=2022-02-02|title=Ottawa police arrest third person in 'Freedom Convoy' protests as truckers vow to stay|url=https://ottawa.ctvnews.ca/ottawa-police-arrest-third-person-in-freedom-convoy-protests-as-truckers-vow-to-stay-1.5764002|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220202163710/https://ottawa.ctvnews.ca/ottawa-police-arrest-third-person-in-freedom-convoy-protests-as-truckers-vow-to-stay-1.5764002|url-status=dead|archive-date=February 2, 2022|access-date=2022-02-02|website=CTV News Ottawa}} Ottawa residents reported numerous incidents in which convoy protesters physically assaulted them, or threatened rape or death for wearing masks.{{cite web|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8594809/covid-freedom-convoy-ottawa-masks/|last=Gilmore|first=Rachel|date=2022-02-04|title=Ottawa residents should follow public health advice amid convoy protest, top doctor says|website=Global News}} A protester threatened to stab a 16-year-old employee at a Tim Hortons in Centretown, after being informed that patrons must wear masks.{{cite web|url=https://www.nationalobserver.com/2022/02/01/opinion/its-not-clear-whether-ottawa-police-took-permissive-approach-trucker-protestors/|last=Fawcett|first=Max|date=2022-02-01|title=It's not clear whether the Ottawa police took a permissive approach with trucker protestors because they sympathized with them or were scared of them|website=National Observer}} Convoy supporters physically attacked an Ottawa pastor because he was wearing a mask, and they routinely urinated on his church. The Ottawa People’s Commission documented many alleged hate crimes, including an incident in which an Ottawa resident of Asian ancestry was beaten by three convoy supporters after being asked to perform a dragon dance.{{cite web|url=https://pressprogress.ca/it-was-violent-peoples-commission-report-shines-light-on-violence-harassment-and-hate-crimes-during-freedom-convoy/|last=LeBrun|first=Luke|date=2023-01-31|title='It Was Violent': People's Commission Report Shines Light on Violence, Harassment and Hate Crimes During 'Freedom Convoy'|website=PressProgress}} In a separate incident, two convoy supporters yelled anti-Asian slurs at an employee of a downtown ice cream shop who was wearing a mask while walking to work; the two men then threw the employee to the ground. Because of safety concerns, the shop closed for an extended period of time.{{cite web|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/moo-shu-ice-cream-employee-assaulted-ottawa-1.6341207|last=Molina|first=Kimberley|date=2022-02-07|title=Centretown ice cream shop closes after worker reports assault on way to work|website=CBC News}} A video circulated showing protesters appropriating [[First Nations in Canada|First Nations]] drumming while dancing, drinking beer, and chanting "[[The Flintstones|yabba dabba doo]]" and "fuck Trudeau".{{cite web|last=Forester|first=Brett|date=2022-02-01|title=Soft police approach to anti-vax protest reveals 'pure racism' say critics|url=https://www.aptnnews.ca/national-news/soft-police-approach-to-ottawa-anti-vax-protest-reveals-pure-racism-say-critics/|access-date=2022-02-01|website=[[APTN National News]]|language=en-US}} Senator and [[Mi'kmaq]] leader [[Brian Francis (politician)|Brian Francis]] tweeted: "I am disgusted, appalled and saddened. This blatant act of racism must be vehemently condemned by all. Drumming is sacred to all First Nations. Our ways of life should never be mimicked, mocked and appropriated for political or other gain."{{cite web|last=Forester|first=Brett|date=2022-02-01|title=Soft police approach to anti-vax protest reveals 'pure racism' say critics|url=https://www.aptnnews.ca/national-news/soft-police-approach-to-ottawa-anti-vax-protest-reveals-pure-racism-say-critics/|access-date=2022-02-01|website=[[APTN News]]|language=en-US}} The [[Algonquins of Pikwàkanagàn First Nation]], whose [[Aboriginal land title in Canada|traditional unceded territory]] includes the city of Ottawa, issued a statement condemning protesters who held an Indigenous [[pipe ceremony]] and set up a [[tepee]] in the city's [[Confederation Park]] without permission of the First Nations on February 2.{{cite news|title=Algonquin Nation issues statement saying 'does not support' ceremony, and truckers convoy actions on traditional territory|work=APTN National News|date=2022-02-02|url=https://www.aptnnews.ca/national-news/algonquin-nation-truckers-convoy-traditional-territory-ottawa/|access-date=2022-02-02}} On February 2, organizers of the protest said that they would not leave Ottawa until governments across Canada "end all mandates associated with COVID-19", and would stay "as long as it takes for freedom to be restored". On February 3, the protesters started building a wooden structure in Confederation Park and gathering fuel supplies.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60251921|title=Freedom Convoy: No plans to call in military, says Trudeau|date=2022-02-03|website=BBC}} Social media reports suggested that the structure was intended to serve as a community kitchen for the protesters.{{cite news|title=Protesters build wooden structure in downtown Ottawa park|first=Josh|last=Pringle|date=2022-02-03|work=CTV News|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/protesters-build-wooden-structure-in-downtown-ottawa-park/|access-date=2022-02-04}} On February 6, Ottawa Police promised to increase enforcement on protesters, including handing out tickets and arresting anyone who attempts to bring material aid, such as fuel, to protesters. A [[States of emergency in Canada|state of emergency]] was declared by the City of Ottawa on the same day. In a statement, the city said that its declaration "reflects the serious danger and threat to the safety and security of residents posed by the demonstrations and highlights the need for support from other jurisdictions and levels of government". On the evening of February 6, Police performed a raid on a stockpile hub of supplies for protesters, seizing fuel and making two arrests for mischief.{{cite web|url=https://www.cp24.com/news/ottawa-mayor-declares-state-of-emergency-as-police-crackdown-on-convoy-supplies-1.5769928|title=Ottawa mayor declares state of emergency as police crackdown on convoy supplies|first=Jordan|last=Press|date=2022-02-06|website=CP24}} [[File:2022 Ottawa convoy supply truck.jpg|thumb|A protester with Canadian and American flags loads up on supplies in Ottawa]] An emergency debate of the House of Commons was held on the night of February 7, at the request of NDP leader Jagmeet Singh. Trudeau stated that the convoy is "trying to blockade our economy, our democracy and our fellow citizens' daily lives. It has to stop." Transport minister Omar Alghabra used the debate to encourage [[Premier of Ontario|Ontario Premier]] [[Doug Ford]] to use regulatory powers to discourage the occupation, such as suspending commercial licences or insurance of the truckers.{{cite news|author1=Nick Boisvert|author2=Peter Zimonjic|title='It has to stop': Trudeau accuses protesters of blockading 'democracy' during Commons debate|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/ottawa-protest-parliament-1.6342221|access-date=2022-02-07|work=CBC News|date=2022-02-07|location=Toronto ON}}{{cite news|title=Canada's NDP leader says trucker convoy aims to 'overthrow' gov't|url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/2/7/canada-ndp-leader-trucker-convoy-aims-to-overthrow-govt|access-date=2022-02-07|work=Al-Jazeera|date=2022-02-07|location=Qatar}}{{cite news|title=Hong Kong to Weigh Curbs; Trudeau Slams Truckers: Virus Update|url=https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/hong-kong-to-weigh-more-curbs-emergency-in-ottawa-virus-update|access-date=2022-02-07|work=Financial Post|agency=Bloomberg News|date=2022-02-07|location=Toronto ON}} It was reported on February 7 that the [[Children's Aid Society (Ontario)|Children's Aid Society]] (CAS) was working with the OPS to ensure the welfare and safety of the children who were living in about a quarter of the 418 convoy trucks, according to Ottawa Police Deputy Chief Steve Bell.{{cite news|url=https://globalnews.ca/video/8604019/trucker-protests-ottawa-police-investigating-children-living-inside-convoy-trucks/|work=Global News|date=2022-02-07|access-date=2022-02-11|title=Trucker protests: Ottawa police investigating children living inside convoy trucks}} Concerns were raised about sanitation, noise levels, and fumes.{{cite news|author1=Michael Woods|author2=Ted Raymond|title=About 100 convoy trucks in downtown Ottawa have children living inside: police|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/about-100-convoy-trucks-in-downtown-ottawa-have-children-living-inside-police/|access-date=2022-02-09|work=CTV News Ottawa|date=2022-02-08|location=Ottawa ON}} According to the ''Canadian Press'', there were about 100 children living in the convoy trucks on February 8.{{cite news|last=Osman|first=Laura|title=Roughly 100 kids living in trucks involved in Ottawa convoy protest, say police|work=The Canadian Press|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/canada/article-roughly-100-kids-living-in-trucks-involved-in-ottawa-convoy-protest/|date=2022-02-08|access-date=2022-02-14}} Bell said that the presence of children is an "important factor" complicating a "challenging operation". By February 9, people with police, military, and survivalist experience had helped the protesters set up camp with tents and wooden kitchens, creating a supply chain to ensure continued protest.{{cite news|last=Trinh|first=Judy|title=How organizers with police and military expertise may be helping Ottawa convoy protest dig in|work=CBC News|access-date=2022-02-13|date=2022-02-09|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/convoy-protesters-police-tactical-knowledge-1.6345854}} The logistic centre at the [[Raymond Chabot Grant Thornton Park]] (RCGP) baseball stadium included trailers of food, tents, toilets, and distribution points for gasoline and propane. The Freedom Convoy's head of security and spokesman was [[Daniel Bulford]], a former RCMP officer who served on Prime Minister Trudeau's security detail.{{cite news|title=Fact Check- Video of former member of Trudeau's security detail speaking against vaccine mandates predates trucker protest|work=Reuters|access-date=2022-02-13|date=2022-02-10|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/factcheck-securitytrudeau-video-idUSL1N2UL2VN}} Police on Guard, a Facebook group of about 100 police officers, mainly retired, and 50 soldiers endorse the convoy. Two serving members of the [[Joint Task Force 2]] (JTF2) are under investigation for alleged involvement.{{cite news|last=Pugliese|first=David|title=Two members of military counter-terrorism unit under investigation for allegedly taking part in convoy protests|work=The Ottawa Citizen|location=Ottawa ON|access-date=2022-02-13|date=2022-02-13|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/national/defence-watch/two-members-of-military-counter-terrorism-unit-under-investigation-for-allegedly-taking-part-in-convoy-protests}} On February 10, leaders of the Freedom Convoy declared February 11 an "[[Orange Shirt Day]]" and called for student walkouts to end COVID-19 restrictions in schools. Indigenous leaders condemned this announcement as misappropriation.{{cite news|date=2022-02-12|title=Indigenous leaders condemn misappropriation of Orange Shirt Day by protest convoy|work=CBC News|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/indigenous-leaders-condemn-misappropriation-of-orange-shirt-day-by-protest-convoy-1.6349344|access-date=2022-02-12}} On Day 15, February 11, [[Doug Ford]] declared a state of emergency in Ontario as protesters continued to occupy the Ambassador Bridge, while Ford referred to the situation in [[downtown Ottawa]] as a "siege".{{cite news|last=Carter|first=Adam|title=Ontario declares state of emergency in response to convoy protests|work=CBC News|access-date=2022-02-11|date=2022-02-11|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/toronto/ford-announcement-ontario-protests-1.6347810}} By February 12, police had cleared trucks from the bridge,{{cite news|last1=MacDonald|first1=Christine|last2=Wisely|first2=John|last3=Altavena|first3=Lily|title=Trucks are gone from Ambassador Bridge, but more protesters arrive on foot|work=Detroit Free Press|access-date=2022-02-12|date=2022-02-12|url=https://www.freep.com/story/news/local/michigan/2022/02/12/ambassador-bridge-truck-protest-police/6764936001/}} with busloads of police, some with heavy weapons, and an armoured vehicle.{{cite news|title=Ambassador Bridge: Police launch bid to clear Canada trucker blockade|work=BBC News|access-date=2022-02-12|date=2022-02-12|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60361533}} By day 14, February 10, the newest tactic was "convoy circles". Ottawa-Centre MPP Joel Harden noted that a "convoy organizer" had been encouraging protesters to form them around public schools in Ottawa.{{cite news|title=Trucker convoy: 25 arrests made, 1,775 tickets issued since start of protests; Police say there's a 'concerted effort' to flood 911 line|work=Ottawa Citizen|access-date=2022-02-10|date=2022-02-10|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/trucker-convoy-day-14-dawns-in-ottawa-key-canada-us-bridge-closed}} A group of about 60 convoy vehicles headed to the [[Ottawa Macdonald–Cartier International Airport]] at 8 am, slow-circling it, honking, and blocking traffic until about 10 am, then returning to their Coventry Road{{cite news|title=Protesters briefly target Ottawa airport as demonstration reaches 2-week mark|work=CBC News|access-date=2022-02-10|date=2022-02-10|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/ottawa-airport-convoy-protest-traffic-1.6346256}} protest encampment.{{cite web|title=UPDATE: Ottawa police negotiate for number of protest trucks to leave Coventry Road, Bank and Laurier areas|work=CityNews Ottawa|access-date=2022-02-10|date=2022-02-10|url=https://ottawa.citynews.ca/police-beat/ottawa-police-negotiate-for-number-of-protest-trucks-to-leave-coventry-road-bank-and-laurier-areas-5048192}} Chief Stoly said many "vexatious calls" from the United States to [[9-1-1]] flooded the system and endangered lives.{{cite news|last1=Connolly|first1=Amanda|last2=Lord|first2=Craig|last3=Stober|first3=Eric|title=Ottawa police say 'flood' of false 911 calls came 'significantly' from U.S. sources|work=Global News|access-date=2022-02-10|date=2022-02-10|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8609284/ottawa-trucker-convoy-airport-disruptions/}} Mayor Watson sent a letter to Tamara Lich and Keith Wilson to negotiate terms with protesters.{{cite web|last=Cochrane|first=David|title=Office of the Mayor letter to Keith Wilson|work=Twitter|access-date=2022-02-13|date=2022-02-12|url=https://twitter.com/davidwcochrane/status/1492960386987048961/photo/1}} On February 13 afternoon, Mayor Watson's Office said that they had reached an agreement with protesters that they would limit the protest perimeter to "Wellington Street, between Elgin Street and the Sir John A. Macdonald Parkway" to "reduce pressure on residents and businesses", and truckers would leave residential neighbourhoods. On February 15, Ottawa Police Chief Peter Sloly resigned after weeks of criticism aimed at his handling of the protests.{{cite news|title=Ottawa police chief resigns amid anti-mandate protests|date=2022-02-15|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60395241|website=BBC News}} Demonstrating a [[Monarchy of Canada#Public perception and understanding|misunderstanding of the functions]] of the [[Monarchy of Canada|Canadian monarchy]], many involved in the protest called and emailed the office of the Governor General to pressure her into unconstitutionally dismissing Trudeau or dissolving Parliament, leading to Simon making a rare public statement on the role of the Governor General. Constitutional scholar Philippe Lagassé pointed to that as "evidence of a recent trend in protest movements", saying, "it has become routine in Canadian politics to write a letter to the Queen, Governor General, or a lieutenant governor asking them to exercise their powers in some way, contrary to constitutional conventions. This is political theatre, no more."{{citation| url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/charter-gg-disinformation-civic-awareness-1.6365223| last=Zimonjic| first=Peter| title=What many convoy protesters get wrong about constitutional rights and the Governor General| date=27 February 2022| publisher=CBC News| accessdate=13 March 2024}} ===After clearance=== Organizers [[Christopher John Barber|Chris Barber]] and Tamara Lich were each arrested on February 17. Lich's bank account had previously been frozen.{{cite news|author1=Sara Frizzell|author2=Shaamini Yogaretnam|title=Convoy protest organizers Tamara Lich, Chris Barber arrested in Ottawa|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/tamara-lich-chris-barber-arrested-ottawa-1.6355960|access-date=2022-02-17|work=CBC News Ottawa|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-17|location=Ottawa ON}}{{cite news|last1=Villarreal|first1=Daniel|title=Canadian Trucker Convoy Organizers Barber, Lich Arrested, in Custody|url=https://www.newsweek.com/canadian-trucker-convoy-organizer-chris-barber-arrested-custody-1680429|access-date=2022-02-17|work=Newsweek|date=2022-02-17}}{{cite news|author1=Star staff and wire services|title=Convoy protests: Protest organizers Tamara Lich, Chris Barber arrested in Ottawa|url=https://www.newhamburgindependent.ca/news-story/10574203-convoy-protests-protest-organizers-tamara-lich-chris-barber-arrested-in-ottawa/|access-date=2022-02-17|work=New Hamburg Independent|agency=Toronto Star|publisher=Metroland Media Group|date=2022-02-17}} Organizer [[Pat King (activist)|Pat King]] was later arrested on February 18, and an Alberta woman in the convoy, Kerry Komix, offered $50,000 for bail.{{cite web|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/freedom-convoy-leader-pat-king-arrested-live-on-social-media|title=Pat King, leader of the "Freedom Convoy" arrested live on social media|website=ottawasun}} The Crown lawyer noted that allowing her to act as surety was like "one thief being tasked to supervise another burglar."{{cite news|title=Convoy leader Pat King to hear bail decision Friday as woman puts up $50K in support|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/pat-king-charges-ottawa-police-convoy-1.6359934?cmp=rss|access-date=2022-02-22|work=CBC News Ottawa|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-22|location=Ottawa ON}}{{cite news|author1=Mia Rabson|author2=Jim Bronskill|title=Key convoy organizer Tamara Lich denied bail while Patrick King appears in court|url=https://www.brandonsun.com/national/breaking-news/ottawa-judge-expected-to-issue-bail-decision-for-protest-leader-tamara-lich-576232632.html|access-date=2022-02-22|work=The Brandon Sun|agency=The Canadian Press|date=2022-02-22|location=Brandon MB}} Komix was listed as the co-creator of a crypto donation platform.{{cite web|last1=McGregor|first1=Glen|title=Okay, I'm back in after multiple attempts. Crown Moiz Karimjee is continuing his cross of proposed surety Kerry Komix, asking about Facebook post in which she is listed as creator of a crypto donation platform "Tokens for Truckers."|url=https://twitter.com/glen_mcgregor/status/1496191565332725761|website=Twitter|access-date=2022-02-22|date=2022-02-22}} McGregor is a CTV National News journalist. King was denied bail on February 25, after the presiding Justice ruled that Komix was not suitable surety, and expressed concern that King had a high risk of reoffending.{{cite web|date=2022-02-24|title='Freedom Convoy' leader Pat King denied bail, remains in jail|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/pat-king-jail-bail-hearing-1.6363724|access-date=2022-02-25|website=CBC News}} On February 28, a convoy leader from High Prairie, Alberta named Tyson 'Freedom George' Billings was denied bail.{{cite news|last1=Helmer|first1=Aedan|title=Third accused convoy leader remains in custody as Tyson 'Freedom George' Billings denied bail|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/third-accused-convoy-leader-remains-in-custody-as-tyson-freedom-george-billings-denied-bail|access-date=2022-03-01|work=Ottawa Citizen|publisher=Postmedia|date=2022-03-01|location=Ottawa ON}} Various nearby rural areas became new encampments for the protesters. More than fifty trucks moved to a truck stop in [[Champlain, Ontario|Champlain Township]], near [[Vankleek Hill]], immediately following the Ottawa clearance.{{cite news|title=Cleanup crews replace protesters in downtown Ottawa as heavy police presence remains|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/downtown-ottawa-streets-protests-police-1.6358396|access-date=2022-02-20|work=CBC News|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-20|location=Toronto ON}}{{cite news|title=Les manifestants d'Ottawa se rassemblent à un nouvel emplacement|url=https://www.tvanouvelles.ca/2022/02/20/les-manifestants-dottawa-se-rassemblent-a-un-nouvel-emplacement|access-date=2022-02-20|work=TVA Nouvelles|publisher=Groupe TVA|date=2022-02-20|location=Montreal QC|language=fr|quote=Dimanche après-midi, des dizaines de camionneurs et leurs partisans avaient convergé vers le relais routier Herb's de Vankleek Hill.}}{{cite news|author1=Mélanie Marquis|author2=Henri Ouellette-Vézina|author3=Florence Morin-Martel|title=L'occupation terminée à Ottawa, des groupes se relocalisent|url=https://www.lapresse.ca/actualites/national/2022-02-20/l-occupation-terminee-a-ottawa-des-groupes-se-relocalisent.php|access-date=2022-02-20|work=La Presse|publisher=La Presse Inc.|date=2022-02-20|location=Montreal QC|language=fr}} In interviews, those at the site mention plans to continue the convoy, with no specifics. Sites have also opened in [[Trent Lakes]] and near Kinburn, in [[West Carleton-March Ward]].{{cite news|title=Trucker Protest: Day 24 — 'Businesses should feel safe to reopen', police say; 191 arrested|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/trucker-protest-day-24-police-maintaining-presence-downtown-fences-used-to-guard-cleared-areas|access-date=2022-02-20|work=Ottawa Citizen|publisher=Postmedia|date=2022-02-20|location=Ottawa ON}} (Protesters originally intended to use the Arnprior Airport.){{cite news|last1=McIntyre|first1=Bruce|title=Protesters hole up on farm near Arnprior|url=https://www.eganvilleleader.ca/breaking-news/small-group-of-ottawa-protestors-hole-up-on-farm-near-arnprior/|access-date=2022-02-28|work=The Eganville Leader|date=2022-02-22|location=Eganville ON}} As of February 17, 2022, a farm in [[Russell Township, Ontario|Russell Township]], outside of [[Embrun, Ontario|Embrun]], continued to be used by truckers as a convoy base camp. The Ontario Provincial Police is monitoring the site.{{cite news|last1=LeBrun|first1=Luke|title=The Convoy is Dividing a Rural Community Being Used as a Staging Ground for the Illegal Siege of Ottawa|url=https://pressprogress.ca/the-convoy-is-dividing-a-rural-community-being-used-as-a-staging-ground-for-the-illegal-siege-of-ottawa/|access-date=2022-02-20|work=PressProgress|publisher=Institut Broadbent Institute|date=2022-02-17|location=Ottawa ON}}{{cite news|author1=Radio-Canada|title=Des manifestants contre les mesures sanitaires s'installent à Embrun|url=https://ici.radio-canada.ca/nouvelle/1861712/camionneurs-manifestants-installes-embrun-terre-agricole|access-date=2022-02-20|work=ICI Ottawa-Gatineau|date=2022-02-11|location=Ottawa ON|language=fr}} As of February 20, the site near Kinburn remained active. Prime Minister Trudeau has commented, saying the groups were showing a "desire or an openness" to return.{{cite news|author1=Kristy Kirkup|author2=Ian Bailey|title=Ottawa is ending the use of the Emergencies Act, Prime Minister Justin Trudeau says|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-ottawa-drops-invocation-of-the-emergencies-act-prime-minister-justin/|access-date=2022-02-23|work=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-02-23|location=Toronto ON}} "A few days" before February 21, a convoy from [[Fort McMurray, Alberta]] was turned away at the Manitoba border.{{cite news|title=Trudeau concerned blockades could return in Ottawa, says convoy stopped en route|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/manitoba/trudeau-convoy-blockades-return-1.6359605|access-date=2022-02-23|work=CBC News Manitoba|agency=The Canadian Press|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-21|location=Toronto ON}} At a special meeting of the Ottawa Police Services Board on February 24, Acting Chief Bell feels the Highway Traffic Act and other laws would be enough to manage a possible return, despite removal of the ''Emergencies Act''.{{cite news|last1=Woods|first1=Michael|title=Ottawa police chief 'confident' in ability to keep downtown secure|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/ottawa-police-chief-confident-in-ability-to-keep-downtown-secure/|access-date=2022-02-24|work=CTV News Ottawa|publisher=BellMedia|date=2022-02-24|location=Toronto ON}}{{cite news|author1=Charles Antoine Gagnon|author2=Julien Paquette|title=Surveillance étroite du centre-ville d'Ottawa à l'approche du week-end [Close monitoring of downtown Ottawa as the weekend approaches]|url=https://www.ledroit.com/2022/02/24/surveillance-etroite-du-centre-ville-dottawa-a-lapproche-du-week-end-54b338a669071d8d12b1f831a9ea0363|access-date=2022-02-24|work=le Droit|date=2022-02-24|location=Montreal QC|language=fr}} On March 5, a [[Human chain (politics)|human chain]] was formed along Wellington Street, as a "freedom protest." Some later relocated to the [[Ottawa-Carleton Detention Centre]], demanding Lich and King be released. Ottawa residents protested the delayed police response.{{cite news|last1=Elsiufi|first1=Rachelle|title=Two weeks after convoy occupation, opposing rallies take to Ottawa's downtown|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/opposing-rallies-protests-ottawa-downtown-covid-19-convoy-accountability-1.6374461|access-date=2022-03-06|work=CBC News Ottawa|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-03-05|location=Ottawa ON}} On March 26, a convoy demonstration from Quebec made what police called a "symbolic passage" through downtown Ottawa on its way to [[Vankleek Hill]].{{cite web|last=Lavery|first=Irelyne|date=2022-03-26|title=Vehicle convoy rolls through downtown Ottawa on its way to Vankleek Hill|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8712807/freedom-convoy-ottawa-vankleek-hill/|access-date=2022-03-27|website=Global News|language=en-US}} == Major blockades == {{See also|Timeline of the Canada convoy protest}} ===Alberta–Montana=== {{See also|2022 alleged plot to kill Coutts RCMP officers}} On January 29, the date that the Freedom Convoy arrived in Ottawa, a group of truckers blockaded the [[Sweetgrass–Coutts Border Crossing]]—one of the busiest ports of entry west of the [[Great Lakes]] which connects [[Sweet Grass, Montana]], via [[Interstate 15|US Interstate 15]] with the village of [[Coutts, Alberta]], via [[Alberta Highway 4]].{{Cite web |title=Coutts mayor says RCMP was caught off guard by blockade despite warnings |url=https://globalnews.ca/news/9264433/emergencies-act-inquiry-coutts-border-blockades/ |access-date=2024-06-21 |website=Global News |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |title=RO7-Twenty Busiest Border Crossings for Trucks |url=https://tdih-cdit.tc.canada.ca/en/addendum-tables/2021/ro7-twenty-busiest-border-crossings-trucks#field_tabledata-0-csvfiletable |access-date=2024-06-21 |website=tdih-cdit.tc.canada.ca |archive-date=June 21, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240621213946/https://tdih-cdit.tc.canada.ca/en/addendum-tables/2021/ro7-twenty-busiest-border-crossings-trucks#field_tabledata-0-csvfiletable |url-status=dead }} The [[United States Department of Agriculture]]'s Food Safety and Inspection Service is located at this crossing, making it the principal port of entry for Alberta's large meat industry, and one of three main points where all meat products from the rest of western Canada cross the border, according to the [[Canadian Meat Council]].{{cite web|first=Shaun|last=Haney|title=Why the Coutts border crossing is so critical for Western Canada's meatpacking and cattle sector|work=Real Agriculture|date=2022-02-02|access-date=2022-02-02|url=https://www.realagriculture.com/2022/02/why-the-coutts-border-crossing-is-so-critical-for-western-canadas-meatpacking-and-cattle-sector/}}{{cite news|title=Protesters at Alberta border blockade defy RCMP orders to disband|first1=Carrie|last1=Tait|first2=Kristy|last2=Kirkup|first3=Michelle|last3=Carbert|first4=Kelly|last4=Cryderman|newspaper=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-02-01|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-its-time-to-let-the-people-in-ottawa-get-back-to-their-lives-premier/|access-date=2022-02-02}} The group of around 100 protesters demanded an end to all COVID-19 restrictions.{{cite news|title=Protesters ignore RCMP warnings as blockade at Coutts border crossing continues|first=Michael|last=Rodriguez|newspaper=Calgary Herald|date=2022-01-31|url=https://calgaryherald.com/news/local-news/protesters-ignore-rcmp-warnings-southern-alberta-border-crossing-blockaded-by-convoy-for-third-day|access-date=2022-02-02}}{{cite news|title=Alberta RCMP assaulted in attempt to remove Coutts border blockade, Kenney says|first=Kieran|last=Leavitt|date=2022-02-01|url=https://www.thestar.com/news/canada/2022/02/01/we-just-want-to-go-home-stranded-truckers-in-us-plead-for-alberta-blockade-to-end.html|access-date=2022-02-02}} The blockade, which extended about {{cvt|2|km}} into Canada, also blocked public and emergency vehicle access to the village of Coutts, prompting condemnation from the town's mayor.{{cite news|title=Protestors continue blockade at Coutts border crossing|first=Radana|last=Williams|work=Global News|date=2022-01-30|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8581612/coutts-protestors-blockade-border-crossing/|access-date=2022-02-02}} [[Premier of Alberta|Alberta Premier]] [[Jason Kenney]] called the blockade illegal, and threatened that protesters would face penalties under the province's [[Critical Infrastructure Defence Act]].{{cite news|title=Jason Kenney says truckers' Coutts border blockade violates traffic laws and must end|first=Rob|last=Drinkwater|newspaper=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-01-30|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/canada/article-kenney-says-truckers-border-blockade-violates-traffic-laws-and-must/|access-date=2022-02-02}}{{cite news|title=Freedom Convoy: Blockade at Alberta border crossing 'unlawful'|work=BBC|date=2022-02-01|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60218354|access-date=2022-02-02}} Alberta RCMP initially negotiated with protesters for a peaceful end to the blockade, and protesters agreed to release about 50 vehicles trapped on Highway 4 and [[Interstate 15 in Montana|US Interstate 15]]. On February 1, police ceased negotiations and demanded that protesters leave the highway, and set up a [[police roadblock]] on Highway 4 about {{cvt|20|km}} north of Coutts.{{cite news|title=Kenney calls for calm at Alberta border blockade after some protesters breach police barriers|first1=Hannah|last1=Kost|first2=Rob|last2=Easton|date=2022-02-01|work=CBC News|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/calgary/coutts-border-blockade-protest-alberta-1.6334990|access-date=2022-02-02}} A few vehicles left, most remained, and several incidents of violence against police and civilians were reported, including protesters [[car ramming attack|ramming]] RCMP vehicles. Some vehicles wanting to join the blockade evaded the RCMP roadblock by driving through a ditch and drove south in the highway's northbound lanes, resulting in a [[head-on collision]]. The two men involved in the collision were taken to hospital.{{cite web|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/calgary/article/rcmp-investigate-serious-collision-during-southern-alberta-convoy-protest/|title=RCMP investigate serious collision during southern Alberta convoy protest|date=2022-02-05}} On February 12, due to ongoing protests, the Canada Border Services Agency suspended all travel at the Coutts border crossing.{{cite web|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8615799/coutts-entry-suspended/|title=Coutts port of entry temporarily suspended: CBSA |website=Global News}}{{cite web|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/calgary/article/operations-at-coutts-alta-crossing-disrupted-until-further-notice/|title=Operations at Coutts, Alta., crossing disrupted until further notice|date=2022-02-12|website=CTV}} On February 14, RCMP stepped in and arrested 13{{cite web|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/canada/article/emergencies-act-a-turning-point-to-end-trucker-occupation-ottawa-interim-police-chief/|title=Emergencies Act a 'turning point' to end trucker 'occupation': Ottawa interim police chief|date=2022-02-15|website=CTVNews}} people, seizing [[long guns]], [[handguns]], [[body armour]], a large amount of ammunition and [[high capacity magazines]].{{cite web|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8618494/alberta-coutts-border-protest-weapons-ammunition-seized/|title=RCMP arrest 13 people, seize weapons and ammunition near Coutts border blockade |work=Global News}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20220214181820/https://www.myprincegeorgenow.com/155991/eleven-people-arrested-weapons-seized-at-coutts-alberta-border-blockade/ Eleven people arrested, weapons seized, at Coutts Alberta border blockade] at MyPrinceGeorgeNow.com; published February 14, 2022; via [[archive.org]] Four men were subsequently charged with [[conspiracy to murder]] RCMP officers.{{cite news|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/canada/alberta/article-rcmp-charge-four-men-with-plotting-to-murder-officers-in-connection/|title=RCMP charge four men with plotting to murder officers in connection with Coutts border protest|first=Jana G.|last=Pruden|newspaper=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-02-15}} The border crossing was reopened on February 15. ===Ontario–Michigan=== [[File:Trucks lined up on Ambassador Bridge Feb 8.png|thumb|left|150px|Trucks unable to cross the border, lined up on the Ambassador Bridge February 8, 2022]] Protesters at the [[Ambassador Bridge]], connecting [[Windsor, Ontario]], with Detroit, Michigan, and one of the busiest international border crossings in North America, blockaded the border crossing on February 7.{{cite news|title=2nd day of Ambassador Bridge protest halts vehicles from leaving Michigan, limits Windsor to U.S. traffic|work=CBC News|date=2022-02-08|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/ambassador-bridge-protest-1.6343344|access-date=2022-02-09}} On the evening of February 7, movement at the bridge came to a complete halt.{{cite web|url=https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/local/detroit-city/2022/02/07/covid-protest-sparks-traffic-delays-ambassador-bridge/6698271001/|title=Police: Ambassador Bridge traffic to U.S. open after Canada protest|first=Mark|last=Hicks|date=2022-02-07|website=The Detroit News}} The [[blockade]] continued into February 8.{{cite web|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/windsor/article/supporters-of-trucker-convoy-delay-traffic-at-canadas-busiest-border-crossing/|title=Supporters of trucker convoy delay traffic at Canada's busiest border crossing|date=2022-02-07|website=CTV News}} On the morning of February 8, officials declared the bridge reopened, but the blockade resumed, pushing trips to the [[Blue Water Bridge]] connecting [[Sarnia]] and [[Port Huron, Michigan|Port Huron]].{{cite news|last1=Murphy|first1=Jessica|last2=Debusmann|first2=Bernd Jr.|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60267841|title=Canada truckers protest: Ambassador Bridge reopened|work=BBC News|date=2022-02-08}}{{cite news|title=2nd day of Ambassador Bridge protest halts vehicles from leaving Michigan, limits Windsor to U.S. traffic|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/ambassador-bridge-protest-1.6343344|access-date=2022-02-08|work=CBC News Windsor|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-08|location=Windsor ON}} Supply shortages caused by the blockade led to work shortages at [[Ford Motor Company]]'s [[assembly plant]]s in Oakville and in [[Lansing, Michigan]], and shut down its Windsor plant.{{cite news|last1=Jackson|first1=Hannah|title=Ford Canada shuts Windsor auto plant as Ambassador Bridge blockade continues|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8608461/auto-plant-delays-ambassador-bridge-blockade/|access-date=2022-02-09|work=Global News|publisher=Corus Entertainment|date=2022-02-09|location=Toronto ON}} Parts manufacturers for [[Chrysler]] were also shut down, and on February 10, [[Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada|Toyota]] revealed that its three Ontario plants could not produce vehicles for the rest of the week.{{cite news|title=Ford, Toyota halt some output amid Canadian trucker protests|agency=Reuters|via=Al Jazeera|date=2022-02-10|url=https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2022/2/10/ford-toyota-halt-some-output-over-canadian-trucker-protests|access-date=2022-02-10}} The [[Bank of Canada]] warned that the blockade could have a "measurable impact" on the Canadian economy. The Windsor-Essex Children's Aid Society condemned protesters for using children as [[human shields]] during the protest.{{cite news|title=Protesters allow traffic lane to open at Ambassador Bridge as Ontario court hears injunction attempt|work=CBC|access-date=2022-02-19|date=2022-02-11|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/windsor-ambassador-bridge-blockade-fifth-day-1.6347766}} On the morning of February 12, aided by [[Ontario Provincial Police]] and the [[RCMP]],{{cite web|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8615208/ambassador-bridge-police-convoy-clearing/|title=Police, protesters in standoff at Ambassador Bridge as clear-out efforts stall |website=Global News}} Windsor Police began to clear out the blockade using new powers.{{cite news|url=https://www.nytimes.com/live/2022/02/12/world/canada-protest-trudeau|title=Canada Live Updates: Police Begin Clearing Truckers Blocking Ontario Bridge|first1=Dan|last1=Bilefsky|first2=Vjosa|last2=Isai|first3=Aurelien|last3=Breeden|newspaper=The New York Times|date=2022-02-12}} Despite the heavy police presence, by the end of the day on February 12, most protesters remained, although had been pushed much further from the bridge. Police arrested two individuals, towed seven vehicles and issued eleven tickets.{{cite web|date=2022-02-12|title=Windsor police make several arrests as officers begin removal of protesters at Ambassador Bridge|url=https://toronto.citynews.ca/2022/02/12/protesters-standing-their-ground-despite-injunctions-prospects-of-harsh-penalties/|access-date=2022-02-13|website=CityNews}} On the morning of February 13, joint police forces took control of the area and began to make arrests.{{cite news|last1=Gollom|first1=Mark|last2=Craggs|first2=Samantha|date=2022-02-13|title=Police break up remaining protest at Windsor's Ambassador Bridge, make 12 arrests|work=CBC News|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/windsor-ambassador-bridge-police-protest-1.6350120|access-date=2022-02-13}} In the late evening of February 13, the Ambassador Bridge had fully reopened.{{cite web|url=https://apnews.com/article/coronavirus-pandemic-business-health-canada-blockades-65453655bfffcd349388c283d14f972a|title=Key US-Canada bridge reopens after police clear protesters|date=2022-02-13|website=AP NEWS}} 42 arrests were made in total, with 37 vehicles seized. Windsor Police requested resources from other police organizations on February 9, 2022, after the number of participants climbed to around 100, with between 50 – 70 vehicles.{{cite web|last=Rocca|first=Ryan|date=2022-02-09|title=Windsor officials requesting assistance as Ambassador Bridge blockade continues|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8606062/southern-ontario-convoy-demonstrations/|access-date=2022-02-18|website=Global News|language=en-US}} [[Windsor, Ontario]], where the convoy blocked the Ambassador Bridge, intervened in an action by automobile manufacturers in Superior Court for an injunction. Mayor Drew Dilkens declared his intention to clear the bridge with the assistance of police reinforcements.{{cite news|author1=Celina Tebor|author2=Ryan W. Miller|title=Canadian mayor seeks injunction to clear truck protest at US border|url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/health/2022/02/11/covid-vaccines-mandates-masks-cases-deaths/6744118001/|access-date=2022-02-11|work=USA Today|date=2022-02-11}} An injunction was granted February 11 effective 7 pm.{{cite news|title=Court grants injunction against Ambassador Bridge blockade|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/ambassador-bridge-injunction-ruling-1.6348767|access-date=2022-02-11|work=CBC News|date=2022-02-11}} A deadline of midnight was set. Local police were to be employed to clear the bridge.{{cite news|author1=Riley Beggin|author2=Jordyn Grzelewski|author3=Kalea Hall|title=Ontario judge orders Ambassador Bridge protesters to stop blockade|url=https://www.detroitnews.com/story/business/2022/02/11/ambassador-bridge-canada-trucker-protest-freedom-convoy-traffic-blockade/6749811001/|access-date=2022-02-11|work=The Detroit News|date=2022-02-11|archive-date=2022-02-11|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220211221236/https://www.detroitnews.com/story/business/2022/02/11/ambassador-bridge-canada-trucker-protest-freedom-convoy-traffic-blockade/6749811001/|url-status=dead}} At 9 am, police from several Ontario cities, including [[London, Ontario]], were at the bridge in force together with buses and tow trucks, an armored personnel carrier, and a sniper. The Windsor police announced via Twitter that enforcement had begun. Some protestors, after discussion with police, had peacefully departed with their vehicles.Velshi MSNBC February 12, 2022 The police, from Windsor and 5 other jurisdictions, succeeded in clearing the area of tractor-trailers, but a large crowd gathered in the area; the crowd was peaceful, even festive, singing the national anthem, but the bridge remained closed. The police maintained a strong presence in the area, but did not attempt mass arrests. In the evening one arrest was made.{{cite news|author1=Francis X. Donnelly|author2=James David Dickson|title=Authorities, protesters in stalemate over Ambassador Bridge blockade; arrest made|url=https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/local/michigan/2022/02/12/ambassador-bridge-blockade-border-protest-windsor-detroit/6764897001/|access-date=2022-02-13|work=The Detroit News|date=2022-02-12}} The protesters were completely cleared by February 13 at approximately 8 a.m, although the border was not immediately opened.{{cite news|author1=Kayla Tarnowski|author2=Julio Cesar Chavez|title=Key bridge to U.S. remains closed as Canada police clear protesters|url=https://www.reuters.com/world/americas/canada-protesters-police-deadlocked-tensions-simmer-blocked-border-bridge-2022-02-13/|access-date=2022-02-13|work=Reuters|date=2022-02-13}} The bridge re-opened in the evening with 25 to 30 people arrested and 12 small vehicles seized. There was a substantial police presence and [[Jersey barrier]]s were installed to keep the three-mile long access road to the bridge open.{{cite news|author1=Sarah Rahal|title=Arrest, tow totals from the Ambassador Bridge blockade in Windsor|url=https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/local/detroit-city/2022/02/13/ambassador-bridge-blockade-windsor-totals-arrests-vehicles-towed/6777599001/|access-date=2022-02-14|work=The Detroit News|date=2022-02-14|archive-date=2022-02-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220214074504/https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/local/detroit-city/2022/02/13/ambassador-bridge-blockade-windsor-totals-arrests-vehicles-towed/6777599001/|url-status=dead}} On February 16, police intercepted a convoy of six or seven trucks {{Convert|250|km|mi}} away from Windsor – heading towards the city after leaving Ottawa.{{cite news|title=Police intercepted Ottawa convoy suspected of heading to Windsor, Ont.|work=CBC|access-date=2022-02-19|date=2022-02-16|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/windsor-police-huron-church-road-1.6353891}} == Participants and response == === Groups === In the lead-up to the planned arrival in Ottawa, it was reported on January 25 that far-right and [[White supremacy|white supremacist]] groups were hoping for violence on Parliament Hill akin to the [[2021 United States Capitol attack]].{{cite web|last1=Boutilier|first1=Alex|last2=Gilmore|first2=Rachel|title=Far-right groups hope trucker protest will be Canada's 'January 6th'|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8537433/far-right-groups-trucker-protest-jan-6/|website=Global News|publisher=Corus Entertainment|access-date=2022-01-26}} This has caused organizer Tamara Lich to address convoy members and denounce political violence, saying that protesters should "hold a peaceful protest" instead.{{cite web|last1=Zimonjic|first1=Peter|title=Organizer behind anti-vaccine mandate convoy says it won't tolerate extremists as online rhetoric heats up|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/organizer-extremists-convoy-no-violence-1.6327650|website=CBC|access-date=2022-01-26}}{{cite news |last1=Gilmore |first1=Rachel |date=2022-01-27 |title='Fringe minority' in truck convoy with 'unacceptable views' don't represent Canadians: Trudeau |url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8539610/trucker-convoy-covid-vaccine-mandates-ottawa/ |access-date=2022-01-30 |work=Global News |publisher=Corus Entertainment}} Organizers and leaders of the convoy condemned extremist groups, and asked that participants report lawbreakers to the police.{{cite web|title="Freedom Convoy" organizer says it's not affiliated with extremist groups |url=https://dailyhive.com/vancouver/freedom-convoy-extremists|access-date=2022-01-29|website=dailyhive.com}} They also stated that any extremists found would be "removed" from the convoy.{{cite web|title=Organizer behind anti-vaccine mandate convoy says it won't tolerate extremists as online rhetoric heats up|url=https://www.msn.com/en-ca/news/canada/organizer-behind-anti-vaccine-mandate-convoy-says-it-wont-tolerate-extremists-as-online-rhetoric-heats-up/ar-AAT8KPR|access-date=2022-01-29|website=MSN}} Despite this, some protesters were photographed with [[Modern display of the Confederate battle flag|Confederate flags]] and other flags with [[Nazi symbolism|Nazi symbols]] at the rally.{{cite web|title=Swastikas displayed at Canadian 'Freedom Convoy' protests against vaccination mandates|website=Jewish Telegraphic Agency|url=https://www.jta.org/2022/01/30/politics/swastikas-displayed-at-canadian-freedom-convoy-protests-against-vaccination-mandates|date=2022-01-30|access-date=2022-01-30}}{{cite web|title=Head of Canadian Military Condemns Anti-Vax Convoy Protesters 'Dancing on Tomb of the Unknown Soldier'|website=PressProgress|date=2022-01-30|url=https://pressprogress.ca/head-of-canadian-military-condemns-anti-vax-convoy-protesters-dancing-on-tomb-of-the-unknown-soldier/|access-date=2022-01-30}} ''The Globe and Mail'' noted that "signs comparing vaccine mandates to the persecution of Jewish people by the Nazis remained" until the clearance.{{cite news|author1=Erin Anderssen|author2=Marieke Walsh|title=Convoy protestors uncertain about future after Ottawa blockades end with no resolution|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/canada/article-demonstrators-uncertain-about-future-after-blockades-end-with-no/|access-date=2022-02-22|date=2022-02-21|work=The Globe and Mail}} On January 28, Prime Minister [[Justin Trudeau]] expressed concern that a small group of protesters were going to be posing a threat during the weekend.{{cite web|title=Trudeau says 'very small, very angry' trucker convoy is trying to 'lash out' |url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8579418/justin-trudeau-trucker-convoy-violence-ottawa/|access-date=2022-01-29|website=Global News}} One of the lead organizers of the convoy, James Bauder, has previously stated support for [[QAnon]], endorsed [[COVID-19 misinformation|conspiracy theories about the COVID-19 pandemic]] and the [[attempts to overturn the 2020 United States presidential election|2020 US presidential election]], and called for the arrest of Prime Minister Justin Trudeau for alleged treason.{{cite web|title=MPs Told to Hide From Anti-Vaxxer Convoy by Parliament Security Chief|website=Vice World News|date=2022-01-28|url=https://www.vice.com/en/article/mps-told-to-hide-from-anti-vaxxer-trucker-convoy-by-parliament-security-chief/|access-date=2022-01-29}} On February 3, 2022, [[Romana Didulo]] arrived in Ottawa with supporters. Didulo, a conspiracy theorist linked to QAnon who calls herself the "Queen of Canada",{{cite news|author1=Leyland Cecco|author2=Amru Salahuddien|author3=Tracey Lindeman|title=Ottawa protests: tensions grow as 'intolerable' truck blockade paralyzes Canada capital|url=https://www.theguardian.com/discover-cool-canada/2022/feb/03/ottawa-protests-tensions-grow-truck-blockade-paralyzes-canada-capital|access-date=2022-02-03|work=The Guardian|date=2022-02-03|location=London UK}} had called in November 2021 on her 73,000 [[Telegram (software)|Telegram]] followers to shoot healthcare workers administering [[COVID-19 vaccines]].{{cite news|author1=Andrew Russell|author2=Stewart Bell|title=Self-declared 'Queen of Canada' detained by RCMP after alleged threats to health-care workers|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8417379/queen-of-canada-covid-online-threats/|access-date=2022-02-03|work=Global News|date=2021-12-01|location=Toronto ON}} An episode of ''[[The Faulkner Focus]]'', hosted by [[Harris Faulkner]], was criticized by ''[[The Daily Beast]]'' in February 2022 for trying to [[Normalization (sociology)|normalize]] the protests and [[anti-vaccine sentiment]]. The episode said that the protesters were not a fringe minority, but represented the mainstream of society and had international support. The montage of protesters she showed prominently displayed imagery relating to QAnon.{{cite news|last1=Baragona|first1=Justin|title=QAnon Flags Fly Onscreen After Fox News Describes Anti-Vax Truckers as Mainstream|url=https://www.thedailybeast.com/qanon-flags-fly-onscreen-after-fox-news-describes-anti-vax-truckers-as-mainstream|access-date=2022-02-06|work=The Daily Beast|date=2022-02-03}} The Facebook page for the convoy has shared content from, and listed as an organizer, the [[Maverick Party|Wexit]] co-founder and [[Yellow Vest Canada]] organizer [[Pat King (activist)|Patrick "Pat" King]], who has previously hosted counter-protests to [[anti-racism]] rallies, spread [[COVID-19 misinformation]], and spread the [[Great Replacement]] conspiracy theory.{{cite web|date=2020-09-17|title=Wexit Co-Founder Threatens Demonstrators Ahead Of Second Counter Protest|url=https://www.antihate.ca/wexit_co_founder_pat_king_threatens_demonstrators|access-date=2022-01-26|publisher=[[Canadian Anti-Hate Network]]}}{{cite web|title=Video: King dives head first into the Great Replacement/white genocide myth|url=https://twitter.com/VestsCanada/status/1159997274900041729|website=Twitter|publisher=@vestscanada|access-date=2022-01-26}}{{cite web|last=Helwig|first=David|date=2022-02-11|title=The former Sault man at the heart of the trucker convoy|url=https://www.sootoday.com/local-news/the-former-sault-man-at-the-heart-of-the-trucker-convoy-5045377|access-date=2022-02-12|website=SooToday.com}} In December 2021, while talking about COVID-19 public health measures, King said "The only way this is going to be solved is with bullets." On February 8, King falsely claimed that "50% of the Ottawa police force have all turned in their resignation today".
