task|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/emergencies-act-trudeau-vaccine-protest-convoy-ottawa-1.6373636|publisher=CBC|date=2022-03-06}}{{cite news|last1=Boutilier|first1=Alex|title=Committee examining Liberals' emergency powers debates scope of inquiry|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8707822/committee-emergency-powers-debates-scope-of-inquiry/|publisher=Global News, a division of Corus Entertainment Inc|date=2022-03-24}} ====FINA Committee==== The FINA committee had its first session on the ''Invocation of the Emergency Act and Related Measures'' on February 22 and, as of April 28, had elicited testimony from 22 witnesses over seven hearings.{{cite news|url=https://www.ourcommons.ca/Committees/en/FINA/StudyActivity?studyActivityId=11535190|title=INVOCATION OF THE EMERGENCY ACT AND RELATED MEASURES|access-date=2022-05-21|publisher=Parliament of Canada}} ====SECU Committee==== As early as February 10, before the protest ended, the SECU committee devoted two sessions to the ''Crowdfunding Platforms and Extremism Financing'' investigation,{{cite news|title=CROWDFUNDING PLATFORMS AND EXTREMISM FINANCING|url=https://www.ourcommons.ca/Committees/en/SECU/StudyActivity?studyActivityId=11502643|access-date=2022-05-21|publisher=Parliament of Canada}} and interviewed 17 witnesses in the ''Occupation of Ottawa and the Federal Government's Response to Convoy Blockades'' segment.{{cite news|title=OCCUPATION OF OTTAWA AND THE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT'S RESPONSE TO CONVOY BLOCKADES|url=https://www.ourcommons.ca/Committees/en/SECU/StudyActivity?studyActivityId=11502643|access-date=2022-05-21|publisher=Parliament of Canada}} On February 25, in sworn witness testimony to the SECU committee, Public Safety Minister [[Marco Mendicino]], in answer to the Shadow Minister for Public Safety [[Raquel Dancho]], stated that "I would just point out that from the very outset of the illegal blockade, that a number of organizers and leaders of the so-called Freedom Convoy made a number of profoundly concerning and extremist statements calling for the overthrow of the government through violence."{{cite news|last1=Tunney|first1=Catharine|title=Mounties used emergency powers as a 'big deterrent,' says head of RCMP|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/emergencies-act-lucki-rcmp-1.6364575|publisher=CBC|date=2022-02-25}} On March 24, the Commissioner of the [[Ontario Provincial Police]] [[Thomas Carrique (police officer)|Thomas Carrique]] stated in sworn testimony before the committee that "the province's intelligence bureau identified a threat associated with the lengthy protest in the national capital on February 7, one week before the federal government invoked the [[Emergencies Act]]...that the [[Freedom Convoy]] blockades were a provincial and national emergency" and that the "situation and the associated events simultaneously taking place across Canada required unprecedented national collaboration to prevent injury, preserve life and protect critical infrastructure."{{cite news|last1=Ibrahim|first1=Erika|title=Convoy protest was a national security threat, Ontario police boss says|url=https://www.timescolonist.com/national-news/convoy-protest-was-a-national-security-threat-ontario-police-boss-says-5195808|agency=The Canadian Press|publisher=Victoria Times Colonist Glacier Media Digital|date=2022-03-24}} On May 19, Shadow Minister for Emergency Preparedness [[Dane Lloyd]] asked [[Minister of Public Safety]] [[Marco Mendicino]] about the need for the invocation of the Emergencies Act and elicited the comment that the latter "stands by previous statements that the federal government invoked the Emergencies Act on the recommendation of law enforcement officials."{{cite news|last1=Kirkup|first1=Kristy|last2=Carbert|first2=Michelle|title=Public Safety Minister stands by statements that law enforcement recommended using Emergencies Act|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-mendicino-still-says-law-enforcement-recommended-using-emergencies-act/|publisher=The Globe and Mail Inc|date=2022-05-19}} The "previous statements" were in witness testimony that Mendicino made at a SECU committee hearing in April, to wit that the government "invoked the act because it was the advice of non-partisan professional law enforcement that the existing authorities were ineffective at the time to restore public safety." ====PROC Committee==== On May 17, the PROC committee heard sworn testimony from interim Ottawa Police Chief [[Steve Bell (Ottawa police chief)|Steve Bell]] that at no time did he request the invocation of the [[Emergencies Act]] from the Trudeau government.{{cite news|last1=Woods|first1=Michael|title=No direct request for Emergencies Act from Ottawa police, interim chief says|url=https://ottawa.ctvnews.ca/ottawa-police-did-not-request-emergencies-act-during-convoy-protest-interim-chief-1.5907355|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220517190151/https://ottawa.ctvnews.ca/ottawa-police-did-not-request-emergencies-act-during-convoy-protest-interim-chief-1.5907355|url-status=dead|archive-date=May 17, 2022|agency=CTV News|publisher=BellMedia|date=2022-05-17}}{{cite news|last1=Ritchie|first1=Sarah|title=Ottawa's interim police chief didn't ask for Emergencies Act during Freedom Convoy|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/freedom-convoy-ottawa-police-emergencies-act-1.6457488|agency=The Canadian Press|publisher=CBC|date=2022-05-17}}{{cite news|last1=Kirkup|first1=Kristy|last2=Spearchief-Morris|first2=Joy|title=Ottawa Police did not make direct request for invocation of Emergencies Act, says interim chief|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-ottawa-police-did-not-make-direct-request-for-invocation-of/|publisher=The Globe and Mail Inc|date=2022-05-17}}{{cite news|last1=Ritchie|first1=Sarah|title=Ottawa interim police chief Steve Bell didn't ask feds to invoke Emergencies Act|url=https://www.cp24.com/news/ottawa-interim-police-chief-steve-bell-didn-t-ask-feds-to-invoke-emergencies-act-1.5907944?cache=pajhxigfncymiz+|agency=CP24|publisher=BellMedia|date=2022-05-17}} ====DEDC Committee==== Early on in the life of the DECD committee its NDP co-chair, [[Matthew Green (Canadian politician)|Matthew Green]], said that committee members "have a 'responsibility' to work together" in light of the disagreement of the Official Opposition over the constitution of the committee. It consisted of seven MPs and four senators. On March 6, co-chair Green was "interested in looking at the actions of police (or the lack of them), the role played by 'dark money' raised through" the [[GoFundMe]] and [[GiveSendGo]] crowdfunding platforms. The Senate co-chair (one of three), [[Gwen Boniface]], was the OPP commissioner from 1998 until 2006. On May 11, RCMP Commissioner [[Brenda Lucki]] stated under oath to the DECD committee that "while her agency was consulted, it never requested nor recommended the [Emergency Act]'s use".{{cite news|last1=Benson|first1=Stuart|title=RCMP neither requested nor planned for Emergencies Act powers, commissioner tells MPs, Senators|url=https://www.hilltimes.com/2022/05/11/rcmp-neither-requested-nor-planned-for-emergencies-act-powers-commissioner-tells-mps-senators/361423|publisher=Hill Times Publishing|date=2022-05-11}} ==== Public Order Emergency Commission ==== On April 25, Trudeau announced the establishment of the [[Public Order Emergency Commission]], an independent inquiry into the invocation of the ''Emergencies Act'' headed by Justice [[Paul Rouleau]].{{cite news|last=Fraser|first=David|date=2022-04-25|title=Trudeau calls public inquiry into use of Emergencies Act during convoy protests|work=[[CBC News]]|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/emergencies-act-inquiry-justin-trudeau-1.6430084|access-date=2022-04-25}} The inquiry is independent of the parliamentary review committee.{{cite news|last=Fraser|first=David|date=2022-04-21|title=Inquiry into Emergencies Act set to start soon|work=[[CBC News]]|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/inquiry-emergencies-act-1.6425037|access-date=2022-04-21}} By law, the inquiry must complete its report and submit it to Parliament by February 20, 2023. The inquiry was scheduled initially to start on September 19, 2022, and run until October 28, 2022.{{cite web|title=Emergencies Act inquiry will be delayed until October due to commissioner's illness|url=https://torontosun.com/news/national/emergencies-act-inquiry-will-be-delayed-until-october-due-to-commissioners-illness|access-date=2022-09-06|website=windsorstar|language=en-CA}} Due to Justice Rouleau undergoing surgery to address a health issue, it is now expected to complete its public hearings on November 25, 2022. A government press release said it is hoped investigations will "prevent these events from happening again".{{cite news|last=Tunney|first=Catherine|date=2022-04-26|title=Ottawa Police Service's control of convoy protest zone created 'challenges,' minister says|work=[[CBC News]]|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/rcmp-csis-committee-emergencies-act-1.6431376|access-date=2022-04-27}} Conservatives said the investigations are too focused on the actions of protesters and their fundraising, and not on justifying the use of the ''Emergencies Act'' or determining whether it was appropriate for it to have been invoked. The commission, which released its report on February 17, 2023,{{cite web|title=Final Report|url=https://publicorderemergencycommission.ca/final-report/|website=Public Order Emergency Commission|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230217232958/https://publicorderemergencycommission.ca/final-report/|archive-date=2023-02-17}} concluded that the threshold for invoking the Emergencies Act had been met.{{cite web|title=Commission 'reluctantly' rules Trudeau justified to use Emergencies Act to end Freedom Convoy|website=National Post|date=2023-02-17|url=https://nationalpost.com/news/politics/public-order-emergency-commission-emergencies-act-freedom-convoy|access-date=2023-02-17}} === Economic loss and costs === On February 15, there were reports that the blockage of the Ambassador Bridge could result in and costs to the automobile industry representing approximately US$1.2 billion in the economies of both the United States and Canada.{{cite news|last=Kiertzner|first=Jim|title=Ambassador Bridge blockage cost could be $1 billion to US, Canadian economy|work=WXYZ News|location=Detroit|access-date=2022-02-15|date=2022-02-15|url=https://www.wxyz.com/money/auto-news/ambassador-bridge-blockage-cost-could-be-1-billion-to-us-canadian-economy}} The Director of the [[University of Waterloo]]'s Cross Border Institute stated to ''CBC News'' that the economic loss from the Ambassador Bridge blockade was between $3 billion and $6 billion. Every day, vehicles and auto parts valued at approximately US$141.1 million flow across the Bridge. Major auto manufacturers were forced to pause production; "automakers and their suppliers" were having to "scramble to get parts to plants on time" for the current week's run.{{cite magazine|last=Leonhardt|first=Megan|magazine=Fortune|title=Here's how much the 'Freedom Convoy' has cost the U.S. and Canada|url=https://fortune.com/2022/02/14/freedom-convoy-protest-cost-us-canada/|date=2022-02-14|access-date=2022-02-14}} A previous estimate by BBC on February 12 said that the estimated total cost to the automobile industry was about US$600 million (c. CA$763 million). Experts say that the economic effects will be "felt by the auto industry and others for weeks". The demand for vehicles is strong even with prices at record highs, but dealers' lots remain empty. Reuters reported citing [[IHS Markit]]'s data that the "estimated loss" by February 14 "to the auto industry alone could be as high as $850 million."{{cite news|last1=Tarnowski|first1=Kayla|last2=Morgan|first2=David|last3=Helgren|first3=Chris|title=U.S.-Canada bridge reopens after police clear protesters|work=Reuters|access-date=2022-02-25|date=2022-02-14|url=https://www.reuters.com/world/americas/canada-protesters-police-deadlocked-tensions-simmer-blocked-border-bridge-2022-02-13/}} Twenty-five per cent of the value of "all U.S.-Canada goods trade" pass over the Ambassador Bridge daily{{emdash}}approximately $360 million in both directions a day. During the first week alone, businesses in downtown Ottawa, including the city's busiest and largest shopping mall, the Rideau Centre, lost nearly CA$20 million, according to the Retail Council of Canada.{{cite news|last=Pittis|first=Don|date=2022-02-14|access-date=2022-02-15|title=As the blockades have shown, anarchy is usually bad for business|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/anarchism-convoy-column-don-pittis-1.6347821|work=CBC News}} ==== In Ottawa ==== Canadian economist [[Armine Yalnizyan]] estimated losses such as workers' wages{{emdash}}CA$11 million a day for a total of $264 million{{emdash}}as well as other costs that Canadian taxpayers would have to pay for damages caused by the "Ottawa occupation". The estimated loss of sales at the [[Rideau Centre]]{{emdash}}$3 million a day according to the [[Retail Council of Canada]]{{emdash}}amounts to a total of $72 million. Over 50 per cent of the 235 businesses in the area that was blockaded lost revenue. Taxpayers will pay for the $300 a week for those workers who are eligible for the [[Canada Worker Lockdown Benefit]]. They will also pay for the $20 million the federal government will provide to local businesses who lost revenues. The $2.5 million for Ottawa LRT free public transit will be paid by taxpayers, as will the millions of dollars for extra police reinforcements. Yalnizyan also noted the "incalculable damage" to Canada's international reputation as a trade partner which could have a negative impact on supply chains and "political discourse." She did not include the cost to those who lived in the neighbourhoods most impacted, who could not leave their homes and in some cases were "unable to sleep or access clean air or, at times, even food." As well, other communities and neighbourhoods had to live with a reduced police force and therefore reduced safety, as officers were seconded to Ottawa's downtown core.{{cite news|issn=0319-0781|last=Yalnizyan|first=Armine|title=Why you'll help foot the bill for billions lost due to Ottawa protests — whatever your views|work=[[The Toronto Star]]|access-date=2022-02-24|date=2022-02-23|url=https://www.thestar.com/business/opinion/2022/02/23/were-all-going-to-foot-the-bill-for-the-billions-lost-in-ottawa-regardless-of-our-views.html}} The Ottawa Police Service (OPS) initially claimed that every day protests were ongoing was costing CA$800,000.{{cite news|first=Anne|last=Gaviola|title=Economic impacts of trucker convoy protests could be felt for months, experts warn|work=Global News|access-date=2022-02-15|date=2022-02-08|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8602966/freedom-convoy-economic-impact/}}{{cite web|url=https://toronto.citynews.ca/2022/02/02/ottawa-truckers-convoy-protest-arrests/|title=CityNews|website=toronto.citynews.ca|date=February 2, 2022 }} Consistent with earlier estimates, Ottawa Police Services Board announced on February 15 that the cost was around $785,000 per day, resulting in an estimated $14.1 million over the course of 18 days.{{cite web|date=2022-02-15|title=$14.1 million spent on police over 18 days of Ottawa convoy occupation|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/141-million-spent-on-police-over-18-days-of-ottawa-convoy-occupation/|access-date=2022-02-16|website=CTV News Ottawa}} According to Mayor Watson, the Chief of Police's request for 1,800 additional officers could increase the cost to $2.5 million daily. In comparison, by February 8, the cost of the truck convoy had already eclipsed the $620,000 in average policing costs for Ottawa's annual [[Canada Day]] celebrations. In addition to policing costs, by February 7, the convoy cost an additional $1 million in other city services. The closure began January 29, with the mall reopening on February 22. An imitation firearm in the mall resulted in a police-led evacuation, multiple hour closure, and closure of the O-Train at nearby stations.{{cite news|author1=Megan Gillis|author2=Matthew Lapierre|author3=Taylor Blewett|title=Rideau Centre reopens for the day after police investigation, arrest|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/reports-rideau-centre-evacuated-police-ask-people-to-avoid-dalhousie-street-to-the-rideau-canal|access-date=2022-02-22|work=Ottawa Citizen|publisher=Postmedia|date=2022-02-22|location=Ottawa ON}}{{cite news|title=Arrest made as police evacuate Ottawa's Rideau Centre|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/ottawa-rideau-centre-1.6360293|access-date=2022-02-22|work=CBC News Ottawa|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-22|location=Ottawa ON}}{{cite news|last1=Marquis|first1=Mélanie|title=L'intervention policière est terminée au Centre Rideau|url=https://www.lapresse.ca/actualites/justice-et-faits-divers/2022-02-22/vol-a-l-etalage/l-intervention-policiere-est-terminee-au-centre-rideau.php|access-date=2022-02-22|work=La Presse|date=2022-02-22|location=Montreal QC|language=fr}} Based on a [[Freedom Convoy class action lawsuit|class-action lawsuit]] against organizers, lawyer Paul Champ estimated that lost wages and revenue in the downtown core totalled at least $CA306 million.{{cite news|title=More Ottawa residents, businesses join lawsuit against protesters|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/class-action-protesters-money-1.6355146|access-date=2022-02-22|work=CBC News Ottawa|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-22|location=Ottawa ON}} Efforts to fundraise for affected charities includes one called "Make Ottawa Boring Again."{{cite news|title=How 'Make Ottawa Boring Again' went from inside joke to serious fundraiser|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/make-ottawa-boring-again-fundraiser-1.6360646|access-date=2022-02-28|work=CBC News Ottawa|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-23|location=Ottawa ON}} ==== Alberta–Montana ==== According to a February 3 CTV News report, the economic loss of the [[Sweetgrass–Coutts Border Crossing]] border crossing closure was estimated at CA$220 million.{{cite news|last=Thomas|first=Stephanie|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/calgary/article/coutts-alta-border-blockade-estimated-loss-of-220m-economic-activity-industry-experts/|title=Coutts, Alta., border blockade estimated loss of $220M economic activity: industry experts|date=2022-02-03|access-date=2022-02-15|location=Calgary|work=CTV News}} Canadian Manufacturers & Exporters estimated on February 3, that $44 million in daily cross-border trade was affected by the blockade at the border crossing that averages CA$15.9 billion annually in two-way trade.{{cite web|title=Canadian Manufacturers & Exporters calls for reopening of Coutts border crossing|website=Canadian Manufacturers & Exporters|date=2022-02-03|url=https://cme-mec.ca/blog/canadian-manufacturers-exporters-calls-for-reopening-of-coutts-border-crossing/|access-date=2022-02-14}} Alberta RCMP tried to negotiate with the protesters blocking access to the Coutts border crossing. Police attempted to clear the blockade on February 2, 2022, by blocking access to the protest site and ordering participants to leave or face arrest.{{cite web|last=Guse|first=Jessika|date=2022-02-03|title='It was terrifying': Woman says family vehicle was hit during rush of Coutts protesters |url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8589283/woman-vehicle-hit-coutts-blockade-protest/|access-date=2022-02-18|website=Global News|language=en-CA}} The operation was halted due to safety concerns, after multiple vehicles drove around the police barricades to reach the border blockade, travelled on the wrong side of the highway, and two vehicles collided. A local resident alleged that her SUV was hit by one of these vehicles while she and her family were attempting to get groceries. Someone from the other vehicle punched the resident's husband, who received stitches as a result. The RCMP began a new operation to clear the blockade, after discovering and seizing a substantial cache of guns and armour on February 14. There were 13 arrests on February 14 in relation to this seizure.{{cite news|title=Protesters begin leaving Alberta border crossing.|url=https://www.nytimes.com/live/2022/02/15/world/canada-protests-news#coutts-alberta-border-protest|access-date=2022-02-15|author=Dan Bilefsky|author2=Allison Hannaford|work=The New York Times|date=2022-02-15}} The border crossing fully re-opened on February 15, after protest participants agreed to leave peacefully.{{cite web|last=Graveland|first=Bill|date=2022-02-15|title=Border operations resume after Coutts blockade dismantled in southern Alberta |url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8621249/alberta-coutts-border-blockade-dismantled/|access-date=2022-02-19|website=Global News|language=en-CA}} ==== Ontario–Michigan ==== The economic loss caused by the blockade at the [[Ambassador Bridge]] connecting Windsor and Detroit, due to initial delays, was estimated at US$50 million per day when border crossing was still open but at a standstill, according to a February 9 ''Guardian'' article.{{cite news|last=Lindeman|first=Tracey|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2022/feb/09/us-auto-plants-face-shortages-shutdowns-layoffs-protesters-block-canada-bridge|work=The Guardian|title=US-Canada bridge blockade risks huge economic damage, governments warn|date=2022-02-09|access-date=2022-02-15}} BBC reported on February 12 that due to rerouting to other border crossings, that the estimated total cost to the automobile industry was calculated at approximately US$600 million (c. CA$763 million).{{cite news|title=Ambassador Bridge: Canada court orders end to trucker blockade|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60356461|access-date=2022-02-15|date=2022-02-12}} According to the United States State Department Ambassador Bridge website, there is US$323 million worth of goods crossing it daily; with 10,000 commercial vehicles crossing each week day; and US$1.7 billion in total "value in trade between Canada and the US every day." ==== Manitoba–North Dakota ==== The blockade in [[Emerson, Manitoba]], was estimated to be causing CA$70–73 million in economic loss each day. === Criticism of Ottawa Police === {{Update|section|reason=the section describes investigations begun in 2022 but not their outcomes.|date=November 2024}} In response to criticism that police had been too soft on "disruptive protesters", on February 1, Chief Sloly said that it was a "measure of success" that there were "no riots, no injuries, no deaths".{{cite news|last=Bronskill|first=Jim|title=Ottawa officials defend soft police response to disruptive protesters|work=Canada's National Observer|access-date=2022-02-01|date=2022-02-01|url=https://www.nationalobserver.com/2022/02/01/news/ottawa-officials-defend-soft-police-response-disruptive-protesters|archive-date=2022-02-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220201144053/https://www.nationalobserver.com/2022/02/01/news/ottawa-officials-defend-soft-police-response-disruptive-protesters|url-status=dead}} By February 4, Ottawa Police's response to the protests and later encampments of lingering protesters drew criticism from local residents. Complaints focused around lack of enforcement on noise due to constant horn-honking,{{cite news|url=https://nationalpost.com/opinion/chris-selley-ottawa-police-are-here-to-help-further-erode-confidence-in-government|title=Chris Selley: Ottawa police are here to help ...further erode confidence in government|first=Chris|last=Selley|newspaper=National Post|date=2022-02-04}}{{cite news|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/opinion/article-ottawa-police-deliver-a-masterclass-in-how-not-to-handle-a-protest/|title=Opinion: With the trucker convoy, the Ottawa police deliver a masterclass on how not to handle a protest|first=Gary|last=Mason|newspaper=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-02-03|via=www.theglobeandmail.com}} disruption of livelihoods and continued gridlock.{{cite news|last=Bronskill|first=Jim|url=https://www.thestar.com/politics/2022/01/31/ottawa-protest-gridlock-sparks-criticism-of-police-as-residents-demand-city-back.html|title=City officials defend Ottawa police response as protest gridlock sparks criticism|date=2022-01-31|newspaper=Toronto Star}} Police later addressed some concerns by issuing 30 tickets on February 3.{{cite web|last=Lord|first=Craig|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8591904/trucker-protest-tickets-ottawa-police/|title=Ottawa police hand out 30 tickets, including for honking, over convoy disruptions|website=Global News|date=2022-02-03}} Criticism has also focused on "the sharp contrast between the occupiers' reception and the ways police forces all over Canada treat Black and Indigenous people, who are violently policed for existing in public space, let alone protesting."{{cite web|last=Drake|first=Anna|date=2022-02-08|title=So-called "Freedom Convoy" is a symptom of a deeply unequal society|url=https://policyoptions.irpp.org/magazines/february-2022/so-called-freedom-convoy-is-a-symptom-of-a-deeply-unequal-society/|access-date=2022-02-11|website=[[Institute for Research on Public Policy]]|language=en}} On February 4, Ottawa Police hired [[Navigator Ltd]], a [[Crisis management|crisis public relations]] firm to aid in handling messaging to the public.{{cite news|last=Chianello|first=Joanne|date=2022-02-04|title=Ottawa police hire elite crisis-management firm to handle protest messaging|work=CBC News|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/ottawa-police-elite-crisis-management-firm-protest-messaging-1.6339630|access-date=2022-02-05}} By February 13, on Day 17, federal Minister Blair "urged the police to do their jobs, enforce the law and restore order".{{cite news|last=Boutilier|first=Alex|title=Trudeau government actively considering emergency powers to end blockades: Blair|work=Global News|access-date=2022-02-13|date=2022-02-13|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8616616/trudeau-government-emergency-powers-freedom-convoy/}} Police enforcement of "layers of laws, injunctions, and emergency orders already in effect" was minimal, which further "emboldened" protesters on Ottawa's downtown core. On day 19 of the protests, February 15, Sloly resigned as Chief of the Ottawa Police Service. Public Safety Minister [[Marco Mendicino]] announced that the RCMP and OPP would assume control of the situation in Ottawa.{{cite news|title=Ottawa Police Chief Peter Sloly resigns amid criticism of how vaccine-mandate protests were handled|first1=Kristy|last1=Kirkup|first2=Marieke|last2=Walsh|first3=Michelle|last3=Carbert|newspaper=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-02-15|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-ottawa-police-chief-peter-sloly-resigns-amid-criticism-of-how-vaccine/|access-date=2022-02-15}} On February 16, Ottawa city councillor [[Diane Deans]] was removed by council as chair of the Police board, in a 15–9 vote. Councillors described the board as ineffective in oversight, and were critical of the hiring of an interim chief without consultation. Hiring is something the Ontario Police Services Act allows them to do, and the choice had received support of the Ontario Solicitor General. A vote to remove councillor Carol Anne Meehan from the board failed. Councillor Rawlson King resigned from the board during the meeting. A citizen vice chair resigned earlier in the day.{{cite news|author1=Kate Porter|author2=Joanne Chianello|title=Diane Deans ousted from police services board by 15 members of council|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/police-chief-diane-deans-1.6354150|access-date=2022-02-16|work=CBC News|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-16|location=Ottawa ON}}{{cite news|author1=Craig Lord|author2=Saba Aziz|title=Ottawa city council ousts Diane Deans as police board chair|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8625249/ottawa-city-council-ousts-diane-deans-freedom-convoy/|access-date=2022-02-16|work=Global News|date=2022-02-16|location=Toronto ON}}{{cite news|last1=Willing|first1=Jon|title=Council turfs police board leader Diane Deans amid trucker protest tensions|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/council-holds-special-meeting-on-the-ongoing-occupation-crisis|access-date=2022-02-16|work=Ottawa Citizen|publisher=Postmedia|date=2022-02-16|location=Ottawa ON}} Six investigators and two forensic investigators from the [[Special Investigations Unit (Ontario)|Special Investigations Unit]] are investigating two incidents involving the police. The first is an incident on Friday, at about 5:14 p.m. on [[Rideau Street]] and Mackenzie Avenue involving a [[Toronto Police Service]] officer in the mounted police unit and a 49-year-old woman, who "has a reported serious injury".{{cite press release|publisher=Special Investigations Unit|title=News Release|location=Ottawa, Ontario|access-date=2022-02-21|date=2022-02-20|url=https://www.siu.on.ca/siu-investigating-reported-serious-injury-of-woman-in-interaction-with-police-horse-less-lethal-firearm-discharges-at-ottawa-demonstrations-en7495}} The family of the woman said that "she is alive and is recovering from a broken clavicle." This was also confirmed by paramedics.{{cite news|last=Raymond|first=Ted|title=Ontario police watchdog investigating use of horses, non-lethal rounds at Ottawa protest clearing|work=CTV News|location=Ottawa ON|access-date=2022-02-21|date=2022-02-20|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/ontario-police-watchdog-investigating-use-of-horses-non-lethal-rounds-at-ottawa-protest-clearing/}}{{efn|The Special Investigations Unit is "an independent government agency that investigates the conduct of police officers that may have resulted in death, serious injury, sexual assault and/or the discharge of a firearm at a person."[https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/ontario-police-watchdog-investigating-use-of-horses-non-lethal-rounds-at-ottawa-protest-clearing/ City TV]}} On "Saturday, at about 7:18 p.m. [[Vancouver Police Department]] officers discharged Anti-Riot Weapon Enfields ([[Non-lethal weapon|less-lethal firearms]]) at individuals in the area of Sparks Street and Bank Street". The Ottawa Police Services Board unanimously supported a motion on February 24, for the [[Ontario Civilian Police Commission]] to open an investigation into the response to the protest.{{cite news|last1=Willing|first1=Jon|title=Police board asks provincial watchdog to investigate 'freedom' occupation response|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/police-watch-for-any-flare-ups-of-freedom-occupation-heading-into-weekend|access-date=2022-02-24|work=The Ottawa Citizen|publisher=PostMedia|date=2022-02-24|location=Ottawa ON}} From an [[Access to Information Act|access-to-information request]], emails between the [[National Capital Commission]] and Ottawa Police Services revealed that law enforcement overall was hampered by poor communication, disagreement on approaches and tactics, and confusion over jurisdiction.{{cite web|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/communication-breakdown-ottawa-police-ncc-freedom-convoy-1.6453113|title=Police, NCC emails show communication breakdown during Freedom Convoy|last=Fraser|first=David|date=2022-05-16|website=CBC News|access-date=2022-05-16}} === Criminal investigations === Active investigations are underway for a number of widely publicized incidents including those related to the Unknown Soldier's memorial and the Terry Fox statue as well as for more general incidents, including bribery, threats, assault, dangerous driving, and setting a fire in a building.{{cite news|last1=Jackson|first1=Hannah|title='Several' investigations underway after monuments defaced during Ottawa trucker rally: police|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8581382/ottawa-police-investigations-trucker-rally/|access-date=2022-02-01|work=Global News|date=2022-01-30}}{{cite news|issn=0190-8286|last1=Coletta|first1=Amanda|last2=Hassan|first2=Jennifer|title=Police investigate 'illegal' acts at Ottawa anti-vaccine mandate trucker protests|newspaper=Washington Post|location=Toronto ON|access-date=2022-02-01|date=2022-01-31|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2022/01/31/canada-ottawa-protests-trucker-coronavirus-vaccine/}}{{cite news|first1=Rob|last1=Gillies|first2=Tracey|last2=Lindeman|title=Canada pushes back against GOP support for COVID protests|url=https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2022-02-07/canada-pushes-back-against-gop-support-for-covid-protests|access-date=2022-02-07|work=Los Angeles Times|date=2022-02-07|location=Los Angeles CA}} An OPS hotline has been set up where victims can report crimes, including hate crimes. Chief Sloly warned offenders that if they had come from elsewhere and committed a crime, including hate crime in Ottawa, there have been "intelligence officers, investigative officers, and multi-jurisdictional support" from across Canada at work in the background. He said, that "no matter where you live, no matter where your vehicles are registered ... You will be investigated ... We will look for you. We will charge you, if necessary, will arrest you, and we will pursue prosecutions against you." On February 7, some two dozen arrests were made, and nearly 80 criminal investigations had begun.{{cite news|last1=Murphy|first1=Jessica|last2=Debusmann|first2=Bernd Jr.|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60267841|date=2022-02-07|title=Canada truckers: Arrests as police warn of 'volatile' protesters|website=BBC}} On March 21, a 21-year-old Ottawa man unrelated to the protest was charged in connection with the fire.[https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/apartment-building-lisgar-arson-1.6392406 "Police charge 1 man, search for 2nd in arson during occupation of Ottawa", CBC News] On April 6, a 41-year-old Ottawa man, also unrelated to the protest, was similarly charged.[https://globalnews.ca/news/8740736/ottawa-police-have-charged-a-second-man-with-arson-after-fire-in-downtown-apartment/ "Ottawa police have charged a second man with arson after fire in downtown apartment", by Sarah Ritchie, Canadian Press] On November 22, 2024, [[Pat King (activist)|Pat King]] was convicted on charges of five criminal charges including mischief and disobeying a court order.{{cite web|url=https://apnews.com/article/canada-truck-protests-freedom-convoy-ottawa-guilty-3975bb6bbd0c089e0c56cebbe9187fd2 |title=Prominent figure from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions found guilty |website=[[AP News]]|date=November 23, 2024|access-date=November 23, 2024}} On February 19, 2025, he was sentenced to three months' house arrest.{{cite web|url=https://apnews.com/article/canada-truck-protests-covid-pat-king-sentence-075be3c7658bb50b27c285fba03f2a09 |title=Leader of Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions gets 3 months house arrest |website=[[AP News]]|date=20 February 2025|access-date=20 February 2025}} On April 3, [[Tamara Lich]] and [[Christopher John Barber|Chris Barber]] were convicted of mischief in encouraging people to join or remain at the protest, despite knowing its adverse effect on residents and businesses in Ottawa.{{Cite news |date=April 4, 2025 |title=Canada trucker protest organizers found guilty of mischief |url=https://apnews.com/article/canada-trucker-protests-organizers-guilty-a45b9c16148a1453f290b86350fea795 |access-date=April 4, 2025 |publisher=[[AP News]] |language=en}} In March 2025, the Crown said it was appealing King's sentence.{{cite news |last1=Raymond |first1=Ted |title=Crown appealing 'Freedom Convoy' organizer Pat King's sentence |url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/crown-appealing-freedom-convoy-organizer-pat-kings-sentence/ |access-date=25 July 2025 |agency=CTV News |date=21 March 2025}} === Lawsuits === ==== Class-action lawsuit ==== On February 4, 2022, Ottawa human rights lawyer Paul Champ filed a $9.8 million [[Class-action lawsuit|class action lawsuit]] against Chris Barber, Benjamin Ditcher, Tamara Lich, Patrick King and others as organizers of the Freedom Convoy on behalf of downtown Ottawa residents over continuous [[air horn]] and [[train horn]] noise.{{cite web|last1=McGregor|first1=Glen|last2=Aiello|first2=Rachel|date=2022-02-04|title=Lawsuit filed against convoy organizers, seeking damages on behalf of downtown Ottawa residents|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/article/lawsuit-filed-against-convoy-organizers-seeking-damages-on-behalf-of-downtown-ottawa-residents/|access-date=2022-02-05|website=CTV News}} Zexi Li, who lived in a high rise building in the centre of Ottawa and was a data analyst for [[Shared Services Canada]], was chosen by Champ to be the lawsuit's lead complainant.{{Cite web |title=Who is Zexi Li? An overwhelmingly positive, strong and resilient symbol of hope for protest-weary Centretown residents |url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/who-is-zexi-li-an-overwhelmingly-positive-strong-and-resilient-symbol-of-hope-for-protest-weary-centretown-residents |access-date=2022-11-05 |website=ottawacitizen |language=en-CA}}{{Cite news |date=9 Feb 2022 |title=Meet the 21-year-old Ottawa woman behind the injunction that silenced the honking |work=CBC |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/zexi-li-ottawa-injunction-trucker-protest-convoy-1.6344503}}{{Cite news |last=Steele |first=Alistair |date=14 Oct 2022 |title=Downtown residents compare 'chaos' of convoy to dystopian horror |work=CBC |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/ottawa-emergencies-act-commission-1.6616463}}{{Cite news |date=21 Dec 2022 |title=Zexi Li looks back on the year that changed her — and Ottawa — forever |work=CBC |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/zexi-li-freedom-convoy-year-in-review-1.6205324}} In response to the suit, convoy organizers agreed to cease horn noise until 1:00 p.m. on February 6. Many protesters ignored the agreement.{{cite news|title=After night of fireworks, calls from convoy organizers for quieter morning go unheard|work=CBC News|date=2022-02-06|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/ottawa-convoy-protests-second-weekend-1.6341510|access-date=2022-02-06}} The lawsuit went to court on the afternoon of February 5 and a hearing was adjourned to February 7.{{cite web|last1=Raymond|first1=Ted|last2=Pringle|first2=Josh|date=2022-02-05|title=Hearing into lawsuit to stop convoy horns in Ottawa adjourned to Monday|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/hearing-into-lawsuit-to-stop-convoy-horns-in-ottawa-adjourned-to-monday/|access-date=2022-02-06|website=CTV News Ottawa}} On February 6, Champ posted an ultimatum to protesters in Ottawa that they would be released from the lawsuit if they left Ottawa by 10:00 a.m. [[Eastern Standard Time|EST]] on February 7.{{cite news|title=Ottawa protest's lawyer floats chance of quieter nights as police say resources are stretched thin|first=Christian|last=Paas-Lang|date=2022-02-05|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/convoy-protest-ongoing-second-week-1.6341030|access-date=2022-02-06}} On February 7, [[Ontario Superior Court of Justice]] Hugh McLean issued a 10-day [[injunction]] on vehicles from honking their horns in downtown Ottawa.{{cite web|last=Ranger|first=Michael|url=https://toronto.citynews.ca/2022/02/07/ottawa-residents-protest-horn-honking-court/|title=Judge grants injunction against honking in downtown Ottawa|website=CityNews Toronto|date=2022-02-07}} By February 17, the claim had expanded to include a class of businesses and was then at $CDN306 million. Union 613 and Happy Goat Coffee were added as plaintiffs.{{cite web|last=McGregor|first=Glen|title=Claim expands to include a class of businesses. Claim now up to $306 million|work=Twitter|access-date=2022-02-17|date=2022-02-17|url=https://twitter.com/glen_mcgregor/status/1494336658862329860}} On February 18, as part of a class-action civil suit against the Freedom Convoy organizers, an Ontario Superior Court judge has frozen as much as CA$20 million under a Mareva order that targets cryptocurrency, raised for the convoy protests in Ottawa. Paul Champ is the lawyer for the plaintiffs, residents of downtown Ottawa. The Mareva injunction, is separate from the federal government's efforts to work with banks to have accounts frozen. The injunction freezes convoy donations which could eventually be redistributed to residents who are including in the suit.{{cite news|last=Freeze|first=Colin|date=2022-02-18|title=Ontario court freezes millions in cash, cryptocurrency donated to Ottawa protesters|work=The Globe and Mail|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/canada/article-ontario-court-freezes-millions-in-cash-cryptocurrency-donated-to/|access-date=2022-02-18}} In a decision announced on February 6, 2024, a judge ruled against a motion filed by the protesters. Convoy organizers had attempted to get the lawsuit tossed, using a motion that referred to [[Strategic lawsuit against public participation|anti-SLAPP]] (strategic lawsuit against public participation) legislation. Superior Court Justice Calum MacLeod sided with Ottawa residents and businesses.{{cite web|last=Fraser|first=David|date=2022-02-08|title=Lawsuit against convoy organizers moves forward|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/ottawa/lawsuit-against-convoy-organizers-moves-forward-1.7106554|access-date=2024-02-21|website=CBC News}} In February 2025, the [[Court of Appeal for Ontario]] upheld the dismissal, finding the lower court ruling had properly balanced public interest in protest with the harm to downtown residents and businesses and that there it had not seen evidence that police had directed the defendants to park for the duration and honk their horns with the intensity that they did.{{Cite news |date=March 6, 2025 |title=Proposed class-action lawsuit against 'Freedom Convoy' organizers clears hurdle |url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/canada/article/proposed-class-action-lawsuit-against-freedom-convoy-organizers-clears-hurdle/ |access-date=March 6, 2025 |work=[[CTV News]] |agency=[[The Canadian Press]]}} ==== Canadian Civil Liberties Association ==== On February 17, the [[Canadian Civil Liberties Association]] (CCLA) announced it was suing the federal government over the Act's invocation, stating that the ''Emergencies Act'' must be reserved for national emergencies, which they argued was a "legal standard that has not been met."{{cite news|last=Passifiume|first=Bryan|date=2022-02-18|title='Legal standard not met': Two court challenges filed over Trudeau's use of Emergencies Act|language=en|work=National Post|url=https://nationalpost.com/news/politics/legal-challenge-against-emergencies-act-planned-by-constitutional-rights-group|access-date=2022-02-18}} The CCLA criticized the invocation of the Act in a press release, saying, "The federal government has not met the threshold necessary to invoke the ''Emergencies Act''. This law creates a high and clear standard for good reason: the Act allows government to bypass ordinary democratic processes. This standard has not been met." In the same press release, the CCLA stated that the normalization of [[State of emergency|emergency]] legislation, "threatens our democracy and our civil liberties."{{Cite news|date=2022-02-15|title=CCLA warns normalizing emergency legislation threatens democracy, civil liberties|work=Global News|agency=Canadian Press|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8620547/ccla-emergency-legislation-democracy-civil-liberties/|url-status=live|access-date=2022-02-15|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220215141815/https://globalnews.ca/news/8620547/ccla-emergency-legislation-democracy-civil-liberties/|archive-date=2022-02-15}} On January 23, 2024, Judge [[Richard Mosley]] of the [[Federal Court of Canada|Federal Court]] ruled that while the protest itself "reflected an unacceptable breakdown of public order," the use of the Emergencies Act was unreasonable, ''[[ultra vires]]'', and had violated [[Section 2 of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms|Section 2(b)]] and [[Section 8 of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms|Section 8]] of the Charter of Rights and Freedoms.{{Cite news |first=Catharine |last=Tunney |date=2024-01-23 |title=Ottawa's use of Emergencies Act against convoy protests was unreasonable, violated Charter, court rules |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/emergencies-act-federal-court-1.7091891 |work=[[CBC News]] |access-date=2024-02-23 |language=en}}{{Cite news |first=Ted |last=Raymond |date=2024-02-17 |title=Freedom Convoy: Which Charter rights did the Emergencies Act breach? |url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/ottawa/article/freedom-convoy-which-charter-rights-did-the-emergencies-act-breach/ |work=[[CTV News]] |access-date=2024-02-26 |language=en}} The court found that orders made under the act were "overbroad" infringing on the freedom of expression of protesters who did not intend to breach the peace, and that freezing of bank accounts and collecting of financial information from banks had amounted to unreasonable search and seizure.{{Cite web |author=Federal Court of Canada |date=2024-01-23 |title=News Bulletin: Public Order Emergency |url=https://www.fct-cf.gc.ca/Content/assets/pdf/base/2024-01-23-News-Bulletin-Public-Order-Emergency.pdf |work=[[Federal Court of Canada]] |access-date=2024-02-23 |language=en}}''Canadian Frontline Nurses v. Canada (Attorney General)'', 2024 FC 42, available on [[CanLII]] at [https://canlii.ca/t/k2d9l https://canlii.ca/t/k2d9l]. When asked about the ruling on the day of its release, Deputy Prime Minister [[Chrystia Freeland]] defended her government's actions, saying it was the correct decision at the time, and announced their intention to appeal.{{Cite news |first=Christopher |last=Nardi |date=2024-01-23 |title=Court rules Liberals' use of Emergencies Act was unjustified, unreasonable |url=https://nationalpost.com/news/canada/liberal-governments-invoking-of-emergencies-act-during-freedom-convoy-unreasonable-unjustified-ruling |work=[[National Post]] |access-date=2024-02-23 |language=en}}{{Cite news |date=2024-01-23 |title=Freeland: Feds will appeal court ruling |url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/video/c2852829-freeland--feds-will-appeal-court-ruling/ |work=[[CTV News]] |access-date=2024-02-23 |language=en}} In February 2024, the government appealed the ruling.{{Cite news |author=CBC News |date=2024-02-23 |title=Ottawa appeals court decision calling use of Emergencies Act on convoy protests unreasonable |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/ottawa-appeals-emergencies-act-court-decision-1.7124513 |work=[[CBC News]] |access-date=2024-02-23 |language=en}} == Statements and reactions == === Canadian politicians === ==== Liberal Party ==== {{quote box|align=right|width=300px|quote="Freedom of expression, assembly and association are cornerstones of democracy, but Nazi symbolism, racist imagery and desecration of war memorials are not."|author={{emdash}} Justin Trudeau}} [[Justin Trudeau]], the [[Prime Minister of Canada]], dismissed the supply chain disruption concerns as unfounded on the basis that most Canadian truckers have been vaccinated.{{cite web|last=Gilmore|first=Rachel|date=2022-01-24|title=Majority of truckers are vaccinated, Trudeau says, as 'freedom convoy' heads to Ottawa|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8533779/truckers-convoy-canada-vaccine-mandate/|access-date=2022-01-24|website=|publisher=Global News}} On January 31, Trudeau called the protests an "insult to truth",{{cite news|title=Freedom Convoy: Trudeau calls trucker protest an 'insult to truth'|work=BBC News|date=2022-01-31|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60202050|access-date=2022-01-31}} saying that "we are not intimidated by those who hurl abuse at small business workers and steal food from the homeless" and "We won't give in to those who fly racist flags. We won't cave to those who engage in vandalism, or dishonour the memory of our veterans."{{cite web|last=Gilmore|first=Rachel|date=2022-01-31|title='We won't give in' Trudeau says as trucker convoy protest continues|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8582787/justin-trudeau-freedom-convoy-wont-give-in/|access-date=2022-01-31|website=Global News}} [[Omar Alghabra]], the [[Minister of Transport (Canada)|Minister of Transport]], said on January 31, that since January 15 when the truck drivers' vaccine mandate came into effect, the traffic volumes of transport trucks crossing the Canada-US border had not decreased. Compared to the fall of 2021, and based on [[Statistics Canada]]'s most recent figures, even with a "massive snowstorm, even though it was a U.S. holiday, we had almost 100,000 truckers cross the border".{{cite news|issn=0099-9660|last=Vieira|first=Paul|title=Canada Says Trucker Vaccine Mandate Hasn't Dented Border Crossings|work=Wall Street Journal|access-date=2022-02-02|date=2022-01-31|location=Ottawa ON|url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/canada-says-trucker-vaccine-mandate-hasnt-dented-border-crossings-11643668223}}{{cite news|last=Zimonjic|first=Peter|title=Transport minister says there's 'no sign' vaccine mandate is affecting cross-border truck volume|work=CBC|access-date=2022-02-02|date=2022-01-31|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/no-sign-whatsoever-mandate-impacts-border-traffic-1.6334092}} On CTV's January 30 Question Period, Alghabra said that some voices in the crowd of protestors are "really disturbing and unacceptable" and "must be condemned"; this included those carrying signs with swastikas and Confederate flags, and those who called for the overthrow of the government.{{cite news|last=Turnbull|first=Sarah|title='Cannot look the other way': Transport minister on hateful symbols at trucker protests|work=CTV News|access-date=2022-02-01|date=2022-01-31|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/article/cannot-look-the-other-way-transport-minister-on-hateful-symbols-at-trucker-protests/}}{{cite news|title=Freedom Convoy: Trudeau calls trucker protest an 'insult to truth'|work=BBC News|access-date=2022-02-01|date=2022-01-31|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-60202050}}{{cite news|last1=Thurton|first1=David|title=Black parliamentarians say protest convoy is a venue for 'white supremacists'|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/convoy-covid-vaccines-ottawa-black-parliamentary-1.6338720|access-date=2022-02-09|agency=CBC}}{{cite news|title=Thousands across Canada join truckers protesting COVID curbs|url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/2/6/thousands-across-canada-join-truckers-protesting-covid-curbs|access-date=2022-02-09|work=Al-Jazeera|language=en}} On January 24, he described them as a "small number of far-right, vocal opposition that is polluting" the debate surrounding vaccine mandates.{{cite web|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/truckers-vaccine-vandetta-conservative-mps-1.6325761|title=Conservative MPs accuse Trudeau of pushing 'vaccine vendetta' as convoy protest heads to Ottawa|last1=Tasker|first1=John Paul|date=2022-01-24|website=CBC News|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|access-date=2022-01-24}}{{Cite news|date=2022-01-26|title=Transport minister concerned trucker convoy becoming a lightning rod for far-right fringe|work=National Post|publisher=The Canadian Press|url=https://nationalpost.com/news/canada/alghabra-says-vaccine-mandate-for-truckers-not-causing-food-shortage-empty-shelves|access-date=2022-01-26}} ====Conservative Party==== [[Erin O'Toole]], [[Leader of the Official Opposition (Canada)|Leader of the Official Opposition]] and the [[Conservative Party of Canada|Conservative Party]] as the convoys started, initially declined to support the protest, saying instead that the best way to maintain supply chains is for truckers to get vaccinated. O'Toole then later said he would meet with the protesters, but would not participate in nor attend their demonstration in Ottawa.{{cite news|title=Erin O'Toole says he'll meet with representatives from trucker convoy|first=Stephanie|last=Levitz|date=2022-01-27|newspaper=Toronto Star|url=https://www.thestar.com/politics/federal/2022/01/27/erin-otoole-says-hell-meet-with-representatives-from-trucker-convoy.html|access-date=2022-01-28}} O'Toole tweeted "I support their right to be heard, and I call on Justin Trudeau to meet with these hard-working Canadians to hear their concerns". O'Toole, a veteran of the [[Canadian Armed Forces]], later condemned the protesters for desecrating the war memorials on Parliament Hill.{{cite web|title=Officials decry 'desecration' of monuments during Ottawa protest|website=Toronto Star/Canadian Press|url=https://www.thestar.com/news/canada/2022/01/29/officials-decry-desecration-of-monuments-during-ottawa-protest.html|date=2022-01-29|access-date=2022-01-30|archive-date=2022-01-29|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220129235154/https://www.thestar.com/news/canada/2022/01/29/officials-decry-desecration-of-monuments-during-ottawa-protest.html|url-status=dead}} O'Toole was ousted on a leadership review on February 2, some party members citing his policy reversals on issues such as his support for the protest.{{cite news|title=O'Toole resigns as Conservative leader, will stay on as MP|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/o-toole-resigns-as-conservative-leader-will-stay-on-as-mp-1.5764196|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220202215722/https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/o-toole-resigns-as-conservative-leader-will-stay-on-as-mp-1.5764196|url-status=dead|archive-date=February 2, 2022|work=CTV News|date=2022-02-02}} Conservative MPs [[Candice Bergen (politician)|Candice Bergen]], [[Andrew Scheer]],{{cite web|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/article/conservative-mps-cheer-on-trucker-convoy-voice-opposition-to-vaccine-mandates/|title=Conservative MPs cheer on trucker convoy, voice opposition to vaccine mandates|first=Rachel|last=Aiello|date=2022-01-25|website=CTV News}} [[Garnett Genuis]], [[Pierre Poilievre]],{{cite news|title=Conservative MP discusses his support for truckers' protest|url=https://www.cbc.ca/player/play/1996667971692|website=CBC|access-date=2022-01-31}}{{cite news|url=https://nationalpost.com/news/im-proud-of-the-truckers-says-poilievre-in-lambasting-justin-trudeaus-response-to-protests|title='I'm proud of the truckers,' says Poilievre in lambasting Justin Trudeau's response to protests|first=Catherine|last=Lévesque|date=2022-02-11|newspaper=National Post}} [[Martin Shields]],{{cite web|last1=Macfarlane|first1=Bill|last2=Ryan|first2=White|date=2022-01-24|title=Truck convoy organizer raises over $3.8M; protest draws support and criticism|url=https://calgary.ctvnews.ca/truck-convoy-organizer-raises-over-3-8m-protest-draws-support-and-criticism-1.5752185|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220125023542/https://calgary.ctvnews.ca/truck-convoy-organizer-raises-over-3-8m-protest-draws-support-and-criticism-1.5752185|url-status=dead|archive-date=January 25, 2022|access-date=2022-01-24|website=CTV News}} [[Warren Steinley]], [[Jeremy Patzer]],{{cite news|title=Conservative MP Pictured With Ex-WEXIT Campaigner Who Suggested Fighting COVID-19 Restrictions "With Bullets"|url=https://pressprogress.ca/conservative-mp-pictured-with-ex-wexit-campaigner-who-said-covid-19-restrictions-should-be-fought-with-bullets/|access-date=2022-01-29|work=PressProgress|date=2022-01-28|location=Ottawa ON}} and [[Leslyn Lewis]]{{cite web|last=Maher|first=Stephen|date=2022-02-01|title=The Conservative revolt has begun|url=https://www.macleans.ca/politics/the-conservative-revolt-has-begun/|access-date=2022-02-02|website=Macleans}}{{cite tweet|user=leslynlewis|number=1486000209427681295|title=I'm proud of the Truckers. Peaceful protest is a cornerstone of our democracy. The liberal mandates are unscientific, vindictive, mean-spirited and promote segregation. The people have a moral obligation to oppose unjust laws and mandates.}} all expressed their support for the convoy and truckers' movement, although some, such as Poilievre, denounced individual protesters who were promoting extremism.{{cite news|title=Conservative MP discusses his support for truckers' protest|website=CBC|url=https://www.cbc.ca/player/play/1996667971692|access-date=2022-01-31}}{{cite news|last=Lévesque|first=Catherine|date=2022-02-11|title='I'm proud of the truckers,' says Poilievre in lambasting Justin Trudeau's response to protests|url=https://nationalpost.com/news/im-proud-of-the-truckers-says-poilievre-in-lambasting-justin-trudeaus-response-to-protests|newspaper=National Post}} [[Damien Kurek]] and [[Michael Cooper (politician)|Michael Cooper]] attended the rally, serving food.{{cite news|author=|title=Edmonton, St. Albert mayors concerned by local MP's presence at 'freedom convoy' rally in Ottawa|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/edmonton/article/edmonton-st-albert-mayors-concerned-by-local-mps-presence-at-freedom-convoy-rally-in-ottawa/|access-date=2022-01-30|work=CTV News Edmonton|date=2022-01-29|location=Edmonton AB}} Cooper was further interviewed on television. A person in behind Cooper had an upside-down Canadian flag with a [[swastika]]; Cooper says that he was unaware.{{cite news|title=Alberta mayors criticize Conservative MP's 'lack of judgment' at Ottawa protest|url=https://nationalpost.com/news/alberta-mayors-criticize-conservative-mps-lack-of-judgment-at-ottawa-protest|access-date=2022-01-30|work=National Post|agency=Postmedia News|date=2022-01-29|location=Toronto ON}} Bergen, who became interim Conservative leader after Erin O'Toole's ouster, has privately advocated against dispersing protesters,{{cite news|last1=Walsh|first1=Marieke|title=Interim Tory Leader Candice Bergen advocated against asking protesters to leave Ottawa|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-interim-conservative-leader-candice-bergen-advocated-against-asking/|access-date=2022-02-04|work=The Globe and Mail|date=2022-02-04|location=Toronto ON}}{{cite news|last1=Turnbull|first1=Sarah|title=Bergen advised against telling truckers to leave Ottawa, said protests should be made 'PM's problem'|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/article/bergen-advised-against-telling-truckers-to-leave-ottawa-said-protests-should-be-made-pms-problem/|access-date=2022-02-04|work=CTV News|date=2022-02-03|location=Toronto ON}} and insisted in the House that Trudeau extend an "olive branch".{{cite news|title=Bergen aims to make protests Trudeau's 'problem,' calls for government 'olive branch'|url=https://www.nanaimobulletin.com/news/bergen-aims-to-make-protests-trudeaus-problem-calls-for-government-olive-branch/|access-date=2022-02-04|work=Nanaimo Bulletin|agency=The Canadian Press|date=2022-02-04|location=Nanaimo BC|archive-date=February 5, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220205021658/https://www.nanaimobulletin.com/news/bergen-aims-to-make-protests-trudeaus-problem-calls-for-government-olive-branch/|url-status=dead}} Before O'Toole was removed from the Conservative leadership, Bergen had implored him to support the protestors, saying that "there were good people on both sides", which has been described by writers as an echo of the phrase made by Donald Trump in regard to the [[Unite the Right rally]] in 2017.{{cite web|date=2022-02-04|title=Bergen pushed O'Toole to back convoy saying there are 'good people on both sides': sources|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/bergen-pushed-o-toole-to-back-convoy-saying-there-are-good-people-on-both-sides-sources-1.5768337|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220204194046/https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/bergen-pushed-o-toole-to-back-convoy-saying-there-are-good-people-on-both-sides-sources-1.5768337|url-status=dead|archive-date=February 4, 2022|access-date=2022-02-10|website=CTV News}} On February 10, as a third border blockade began in Manitoba, the Conservatives reversed their position supporting the border blockades. Bergen called for the blockades to disperse, "for the sake of the economy", but vowed to continue pushing the governing Liberals to release a clear plan for ending COVID-19 restrictions.{{cite news|title=Federal Conservatives reverse course on supporting trucker blockades, call for an end to all barricades|first1=Marieke|last1=Walsh|first2=Ian|last2=Bailey|date=2022-02-10|newspaper=The Globe and Mail|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-federal-conservatives-call-for-an-end-to-all-blockades-after-first/|access-date=2022-02-10}}{{cite news|last1=Tasker|first1=John Paul|title=Federal Conservatives call on anti-vaccine mandate protesters to go home|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/conservatives-covid-19-restrictions-1.6346576|access-date=2022-02-10|work=CBC News|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-10|location=Toronto ON}} During debate on the ''Emergencies Act'', Conservative members of parliament dismissed the convoy; deputy whip [[Lianne Rood]] called it joyful, and compared it to Canada Day. Conservative Member [[Michael Cooper (politician)|Michael Cooper]] called a Liberal Member "a despicable human being".{{cite news|last1=Raj|first1=Althia|date=2022-02-20|title=Debate rages over government's use of Emergencies Act in convoy crackdown|work=Toronto Star|location=Toronto|url=https://www.thestar.com/opinion/star-columnists/2022/02/20/debate-rages-over-governments-use-of-emergencies-act-in-convoy-crackdown.html|url-status=live|url-access=subscription|access-date=2022-05-27|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220221002228/https://www.thestar.com/opinion/star-columnists/2022/02/20/debate-rages-over-governments-use-of-emergencies-act-in-convoy-crackdown.html|archive-date=2022-02-21}} ====Other Canadian politicians==== [[People's Party of Canada]] leader [[Maxime Bernier]], [[Ontario Party]] Leader [[Derek Sloan]],{{cite tweet|user=truedereksloan|number=1488601123304968194|title=Only an angry, negative extremist could look at the Freedom Convoy in Ottawa and see anything but love of country, of freedom, and of fellow Canadians. I know because I was there and I experienced it. Where was Erin?}}{{cite web|last=Aiello|first=Rachel|date=2022-01-28|title=Protesters arrive on Parliament Hill ahead of main trucker convoy|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/article/protesters-arrive-on-parliament-hill-ahead-of-main-trucker-convoy/|access-date=2022-02-02|website=CTV News}} and [[independent politician|independent]] Ontario MPP [[Randy Hillier (politician)|Randy Hillier]] expressed support for the convoy protests. The People's Party organized a rally in [[Waterloo, Ontario|Waterloo]] on January 23 supporting the convoy, at which Bernier and Hillier both spoke.{{cite web|date=2022-01-24|title=Hundreds gather in Waterloo Square for PPC rally to support truck protest|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/8533842/waterloo-square-protest-january-23|access-date=2022-01-24|website=Global News}} Bernier also attended the January 29 event at Parliament Hill, criticizing Erin O'Toole for not attending.{{cite news|author1=Catherine Lévesque|author2=Anja Karadeglija|author3=Swikar Oli|author4=Michael Higgins|title=Freedom Convoy 2022 live updates: Thousands pack Parliament Hill for protest|url=https://nationalpost.com/news/politics/covid-19-freedom-convoy-2022-news|access-date=2022-01-29|work=National Post|date=2022-01-29|location=Toronto ON}} Hillier would later speak at the convoy, stating "this is the hill we die on."{{cite news|author1=Elizabeth Payne|author2=Blair Crawford|title='Everybody is at a breaking point': Police board seeks more help as protest strengthens|url=https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/everybody-is-at-a-breaking-point-police-board-seeks-more-help-as-protest-strengthens|access-date=2022-02-09|work=Ottawa Citizen|date=2022-02-06}} {{cite news|title=Trucker crowds free to clog Ottawa, Toronto and Vancouver|url=https://bayview-news.com/2022/02/trucker-crowds-free-to-clog-ottawa-toronto-and-vancouver.html/|access-date=2022-02-09|work=The South Bayview Bulldog|date=2022-02-05|location=Toronto ON|archive-date=February 9, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220209232300/https://bayview-news.com/2022/02/trucker-crowds-free-to-clog-ottawa-toronto-and-vancouver.html/|url-status=dead}} Hillier also spoke on [[RT (TV network)|Russia Today]] about the convoy, and later tweeted "Russia news provides a platform for objective journalism where Canadian msm [sic] creates fabrications".{{cite news |last=Parkhill |first=Maggie |date=2022-02-10 |title=Trucker protest: Leaders and major influencers |url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/canada/article/who-is-who-a-guide-to-the-major-players-in-the-trucker-convoy-protest/ |access-date=2022-02-10 |work=CTV News}} Other Canadian politicians—including [[Jagmeet Singh]], leader of the New Democratic Party, and [[Ottawa City Council]]lor [[Catherine McKenney]]—described the protests as extremist.{{cite web|last1=Black|first1=Alex|title='Event we're looking at this weekend is not what it appears:' Ottawa city councillor issues statement on trucker convoy|url=https://ottawa.citynews.ca/local-news/event-were-looking-at-this-weekend-is-not-what-it-appears-catherine-mckenney-issues-statement-on-trucker-convoy-4994688|website=CityNews|date=January 26, 2022 |publisher=Rogers Sports and Media|access-date=2022-01-26}}{{cite news|last1=Tasker|first1=John Paul|last2=Dhanraj|first2=Travis|title=NDP's Jagmeet Singh denounces trucker convoy, disagrees with brother-in-law's donation to the cause|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/singh-trucker-convoy-brother-in-law-1.6328689|access-date=2022-01-27|work=CBC|date=2022-01-26}} Mayor of [[Port Coquitlam]] Brad West condemned the defacing of Fox's statue during the protest. [[Premier of Ontario]] [[Doug Ford]] called the protest in Ottawa "an illegal occupation" and called for an end to the protests.{{cite web|last=Vakil|first=Caroline|date=2022-02-04|title=Canadian premier calls truckers protesting COVID-19 vaccine mandate an 'occupation'|url=https://thehill.com/policy/international/592949-canadian-premier-calls-canadian-truckers-protesting-covid-vaccine|access-date=2022-02-06|website=The Hill}}{{cite news|title=Ontario Premier Ford calls for end to 'illegal' Ambassador Bridge protest tying up 'vital trade corridor'|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/ambassador-bridge-protest-third-day-windsor-1.6344647|access-date=2022-02-11|work=CBC News Windsor|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-09|pages=Windsor ON}} On January 29, [[Premier of Saskatchewan]] [[Scott Moe]] issued a letter in support of the protest. Although repeatedly encouraging vaccination, Moe argued that it would only reduce the chance of severe outcomes, and did not prevent infection or transmission of COVID-19—a statement that was subsequently disputed—and for that reason he did not support the cross-border vaccine mandate, and would lift proof of vaccination requirements in Saskatchewan "in the not too-distant future".{{cite web|date=2022-01-29|title=Sask. premier pledges to end proof of vaccination policies in letter to truckers|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/regina/article/sask-premier-pledges-to-end-proof-of-vaccination-policies-in-letter-to-truckers/|access-date=2022-01-29|website=CTV News Regina}}{{cite web|title=Truckers rally in Regina, Moe promises end to Sask. vaccine mandate|url=https://thestarphoenix.com/news/saskatchewan/scott-moe-supports-truckers-promises-end-to-proof-of-vaccination-in-province|access-date=2022-01-30|website=thestarphoenix|language=en-CA}} Members of Parliament reported an increase in inappropriate and threatening calls to their offices, in correlation with the protests.{{cite news|last1=Zandbergen|first1=Rebecca|title=Regardless of political stripe, MPs say they're fielding vile calls from belligerent constituents|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/london/regardless-of-political-stripe-mps-say-they-re-fielding-vile-calls-from-belligerent-constituents-1.6353480|access-date=2022-02-17|work=CBC News London|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|date=2022-02-17|location=London ON}} ===American politicians and media figures=== Multiple [[Republican Party (United States)|Republican]] minority'|url=https://www.foxnews.com/politics/republicans-congress-canada-trucker-convoy-vaccine-mandates-trudeau|access-date=2022-02-02|website=Fox News|language=en-US}} and [[Rand Paul]],{{cite news|last1=Bella|first1=Timothy|title=Rand Paul urges truckers to disrupt Super Bowl and come to D.C.: 'I hope they clog up cities'|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2022/02/12/rand-paul-trucker-protest-super-bowl/|newspaper=The Washington Post|date=2022-02-12|access-date=2022-02-12}} [[United States House of
